exceed five thousand dollars, bearing interestat the
rateof six per centum per annum , and due and pay-
able in ten years afterdate ; and said bonds , when
issued and signed by the mayor , shall be sold, and
the money thus arising applied to the grading or
macadamizing as heretofore stated; and the fund
hereinbefore provided for shall be continued to pay
offthe bonds atmaturity.
§5 . All laws inconsistentwith this are hereby re-
pealed .
§ 6. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April3,1890.

CHAPTER 535 .
AN ACT tolevya tax and providefor the improvement of roads in
Adair and Laurel counties .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the court of claims for the counties of
Adair and Laurel shall,on or before the firstMonday
in October, one thousand eight hundred and ninety,
and as often thereafteras may be necessary, divide
allthe publicroads and passways of said county into
highways and passways .
§ 2. All main roads and chief branches of main
roads in Adair and Laurel counties, that lead to or
from towns or villages,or other public places of said
county,shallbe deemed highways .
§ 3. All inferior roads or branches of inferiorroads
Constitutepass-in said county that lead to or from other roads,neigh-
borhoods, localities, stations, depots, mills, stores,
post-offices, school-houses,churches or other places,
for the convenience of the public, shall be deemed
passways .
Duties of road
commissioners .
§ 4. It shall also be the duty of said court of
claims , on or before said date , to elect a commis-
sioner of roads (which officeis hereby created),
whose duty it shall be to divide said highways into
sections of not less than three nor more than five
miles in length, according to the amount of labor re-
quired on saidsection,and to appointover each of
said sectionsa surveyor or overseer, to hold officefor
one year, or during good service, notifying said sur-
veyorof allpersonsrequiredtowork on saidsection,
whose duty it shall be, under the directionof said
surveyor, to keep in good repair the full length of
saidsection,according to the requirements of section
ten of this act .
§ 5. Said road commissioner shallhave the right,for
and in behalf of said county, to go upon any land or

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 57
ground near or adjacent to said highways or passways
(except burying -grounds , gardens and yards), and
viewout and purchase or have condemned and opened
tothe public any land or rightof way , not exceeding
thirtyfeet wide , for said highway or passway , that
may be necessary to carry into effectthe require
ments of this act ; and said commissioner shall have
allthe rights , powers and privileges, and be subject
tothe same duties and penalties, as are now conferred
and imposed upon surveyors and viewers of roads
and passways in chapter ninety-four of the General
Statutes, entitled Roads and Passways : Provided ,
That said commissioner shall not agree to pay more
than a fair compensation for said land or right of
way : And provided further, That said compensa-
tion shall not exceed the difference between the value
of the old road way and the new one , when both of
saidroad-ways areon thesame premises,or areowned
by the same party, in whole or in part; and that no
compensation shall be allowed foropening old road-
ways that have been obstructed or changed without
proper authority within fiveyears of the passage of
this act, except that said commissioner may make
some allowance for the removal of fencing, or have
said fencing removed by the surveyor and hands of
said road .
$ 6 . It shall be the duty of said road commissioner
and his successors, on or before the firstMonday in
December ,beginning with the year one thousand eight
hundred and ninety, to re-survey, or view out and
open to the public, as soon as practicable,beginning
with the worst ,not lessthan two nor more than six
of said highways in each and every year,untilall of
saidhighways shall have been re-surveyed and re-es-
tablished; and to make such changes in said high-
ways as may be necessary to avoid steep hills; to
shun creek channels ; to straightenshortor unneces-
sary crooks ; to avoid sidling or mirey places; to
shun bad fords,and to make such other changes in

sionershallprocure only such things as are actually
needed for use on saidhighways .
§ 16. Any work or improvements required under
the provisionsof this act on the roads of Adair and
Laurel countiesthat can not be convenientlyor fully
performed by the surveyorsand hands of saidroads,
shall be done under the immediate direction of the
commissioner of roads , and said commissioner shall
employ such hands ,at a compensation of not exceed-
ing seventy-fivecents a day ,as may be necessary to
perform said labor, or make said improvements as
arerequiredby thisact,allof which shallbe allowed
and paid out of the county funds as other county
claims : Provided , That foremen of said hands may
be paid not exceeding eighty cents a day for said
service .
§ 17. Any person who shall obstruct the roads of
Adair and Laurelcountiesby fallentrees,brush,logs,
fencingor other impediments ,or change or vary said
roads without the consent of the commissioner of
roads, and then only for the greater convenience of
the public and the improvement of said roads, shall
be subject to a fine of not lessthan twenty-fivenor
more than fiftydollarsforeach and everyoffense.
§ 18. All laws or partsof laws in conflictwith this
act are hereby repealed, so faras they apply to Adair
and Laurel counties .
§ 19. This act shall take effectfrom and after its
passage; but shallnot apply to turnpikesor toll-
roads .
Approved April 3,1890.

CHAPTER 536 .
AN ACT to amend an act to incorporatethe town of Lockport, in
Henry county , approved March ninth, one thousand eighthundred
and fifty-four,and amendments thereto.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act to incorporate the town of Lock-
port, in Henry county , approved March ninth, one
thousand eight hundred and fifty-four, and the sev-
eral amendments thereto, be, and they are hereby ,
amended as follows, namely : The boundary of the
town is now extended and enlarged so as to run down
and take into said town the wharf frontingthe river,
the lock and cribbing, that is to say,it'sextended
down the Kentucky river to the lower cribbingof the
lock, and all the land fronting the river down to and
including the lower cribbingof the lock is taken into
the town ; and the town isgiven the right to establish
and maintain a wharf for the town at any point there-
inalong the riverfront.
2. That as the town was establishedby the Leg-
islature and has had no election of officers for some
years,and those persons who were electedhave moved
offand died , the following persons are now ap-
pointed trustees of said town ,and shall hold their
officesuntil the next regular town election under
theircharter , and until theirsuccessorsare elected
and qualified, namely : EliasShonnon,Doctor E. D.
Jones,F. M. Edwards,R. Criagmireand W. T. Hos-
kins are appointed trustees; Henry H. Willis, police
judge,and Humphry M. Sanderstown marshal. The
trusteesshall take an oath beforesome person author-
izedto administeran oath,beforetheyenterupon the
dischargeof theirduties,and shouldany one,forany
cause, failor refuse to qualify,the otherswho do may
fillthe vacancy by appointment until his successor is
electedand qualified.

64 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§3 . That the police judge shall be commissioned by
the Governor and take the oath before he is author-
ized to act, and shall only hold officeuntil his suc-
cessor is elected and qualified. The marshal shall
take the oath and execute the bond required by the
original charter to which this is an amendment .
§ 4. That the trusteesare hereby clothedwith power
and authorityto pass allordinances and by -laws for
the government and improvement of the town , not
inconsistent with the laws of this State or United
States;may compel persons owning property fronting
on any of the streetsof the town to make sidewalks
in frontof theirproperty, of eitherbrick or stone or
plank walks , as the board may directby ordinance ;
said walks shall not be less than five feet wide nor
more than eight feetwide ; if of brick or stone, shall
be laidin sand not lessthan three inches deep ; if of
wood or plank , the trusteeshave the right to say of
what width, thickness, and so forth.
§5 . That if any person shallfailor refuseto so im-
prove the sidewalks,as directedby the board by town
ordinance,afterreasonablenotice,the town may do so
at the cost of the owner or owners of such lots or
parts of lots, and the same shall be a lien on said
lotsas other taxes,and collectedby the tax-collector
as othertaxesare, and ifthatcan not be done,the
town isauthorizedto sue and asserttheirlienagainst
said lotsor partsof lots in the Henry CircuitCourt ,
the town of Lockport , plaintiff, against the defend-
ants,justsame as ifthe suitwas to enforcea mort-
gage againstthe property. No judgment shall be
rendered against a defendant for any more than the
actualoutlay and expenses incurred,and six per cent .
interestfrom thedateof the completionof the work ,
and cost of suit .
§ 6. Thisactshallbe in forcefrom itspassage.
Approved April3,1890 .

county withintwo years.

sage .

under his control,and in which he may have for sale

normore than three hundred dollars, and confined in
thejailof Logan county not lessthan thirtydays nor
more than six months . Prosecutions under this act
may be by warrant issued by any justiceof peace in
andforLogan county, or by indictment in the Logan
CircuitCourt, or policejudge of any town in said
county.

CHAPTER 559 .
AN ACT to increasethe jurisdictionof justicesof the peace in Mc-
Cracken county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the jurisdictionof courtsof justicesof the
peace in McCracken county in civilcases be, and the
same ishereby,increased; and said courtsshallhave
jurisdictionconcurrent with the circuit, common
pleas and quarterly courts of said county in all
cases for the recovery of money or personal prop-
erty in which the matter in controversydoes not
exceed two hundred and fiftydollars, exclusive of
interest and cost .
§ 2. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 4,1890 .

rent with the circuitcourt, the same rules of law and

court for similar services . In all other cases the same
feesshall be taxed as are now allowed by law to jus-
ticesof the peace and constables. Appeals shallbe to
thequarterlycourtfrom theordersand judgments of

he circuit courts where the amount in controversy,

152 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
exclusive of interest and cost, is not less than fifty
dollars. The pleadings and mode of procedure and
practicein the policecourt shallbe governed by the
Civil and Criminal Codes of Practice. The police
judge shallbe a citizenof the town of Greenup , and a
qualifiedvoter of the Commonwealth . His term of

tees are elected . In case of a tie between two or more

tween them by lot. Vacancies shallbe filledby the

upon the certificateof his election or appointment
from the chairman and clerk of the board of trustees .

be determined in the same manner that contested elec-
tionsof county judge are. The policejudge,upon the

scribed by the Constitutionbefore any person au-
thorized to administer an oath , and execute bond

port of allfinesand forfeiturescollectedby him and
due the town since lastreport,and pay same to the

lectedby him ,due the Commonwealth ,he shallreport

person authorized to receive it. Any person who

tion, may be imprisoned in the county jailof Greenup
county untilsuch fineand cost shall be paid or re-
plevied; but such imprisonment shall not exceed one

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 153
day for each one dollarof such fineand cost. It shall
be the duty of the jailerof Greenup county to receive
and keep in the jailof said county all persons com-
mitted thereto under any order of or a capias from
thesaid police court; but the fees of said jailerfor
imprisoning, keeping and providing for all persons
committed for violations of the ordinances of said
town shall be allowed by the board of trusteesand

priorto the passage of thisact.
§49 . This act shall take effectfrom itspassage.

CHAPTER 563 .
AN ACT to amend an actincorporatingthetown of Sulphur,inHenry
county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That hereafter the boundary of the town of
Sulphur, inHenry county,shallbe as follows,and to
thatend the act incorporatingthe town is amended
to read : Beginning at Sulphur Fork railroadbridge,
running south a straightline to and including the
house of William Sparks ; thence to a point on the
railroad, so as to include the house of Gloss Moore ;
thence in a straightlineto and including the house of
Stephen Redwill ; thenceto and includingthe dwell-
ing of P. M. Hogeland ; thence to the mouth of Sul-
phur Fork creek ; thence to the beginning,shall be
theboundary of the town .

154 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 2. That T.E. Garrottshallbe policejudge; W.
D. Rodmon , marshal of said town ; Joseph A. Veal-
ing, S. Y. Rodmon , J. H. Watkins , James D. Shouse
and F. W. McElwaim , trusteesof said town , all of
whom may hold theirofficeuntiltheir successorsare
duly electedand qualified.
§ 3. That an election is ordered to be held on the
last Saturday in April, one thousand eight hundred
and ninety, in said town forpolicejudge, town mar-
shal and five trustees, to hold their officefor one
year, and until their successors are elected and
qualified;and allmale persons over twenty -one years
of age, and residingin said town , shall be entitled
to vote at said election; and the board trustees shall
appoint officersto hold and conduct elections.
§ 4. That the policejudge shallnot be authorized
to exercisethe officeuntilhe is commissioned by the
Governor and take an oath,as now required by law ;
nor shall the marshal act until he take an oath and
give bond as constables do ; and he is authorized to
act as a constable in his town .
§ 5. That saidtrusteesshallhave the righttolicense
and controlthe sale of spirituous, vinous or malt
[iquors in said town , and fix the tax on all tavern-
saloons in said town ; and the licenseto sellspiritu-
ous, vinous or malt liquorsin said town for twelve
months shallbe as follows, to wit : To sellas drug-
gistor merchant , fortwelve months , shallnot be less
than twenty-fivedollarsnor more than fiftydollars;
to sellas a tavern or saloon-keeper for twelve months
shall be not less than fiftydollars, nor more than one
hundred dollars ; and the board trustees shall not
grant any licenseuntilthe applicanthas paid the
county clerk the tax going to the State and exhibits
his receiptthereforto the board of trustees.
§ 6. That alllaws or parts of laws in conflictwith
this act are repealed .
§ 7. That thisactshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 4,1890.

CHAPTER 564 .
AN ACT to amend and reduce into one the act incorporating the
Marion and New LibertyTurnpike Road Company ,in Owen county.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the act, entitled"An act to incorporate
theMarion and New Liberty Turnpike Road Com-
pany," approved March third, one thousand eight
hundred and sixty eight, be, and the same ishereby,
amended , and the charter of said company reduced
into one act as follows :
§2. That the said company shallcontinue under its
presentcorporate name , to wit : The Marion and New
LibertyTurnpike Road Company ,with perpetualsuc-
cession, and with the right in said name to contract
andbe contractedwith,sueand be sued,pleadand
be impleaded ; to have a common seal,and all other
rightsand powers incidentto similarcorporations.
§3 . That the businessof said company shallbe to
build,manage and controla gradedmacadamized road
from Marion to New Liberty, in Owen county ; and
itmay acquire the rightof way forsaidroad,and the
• necessaryland for toll-houses, and stone,gravel, tim-
berand other material for constructingand repairing
thesame , by private agreement or by condemnation
under a writ of ad quod damnum pursuant to chapter

CHAPTER 566 .
AN ACT to authorizethe countycourtof Casey county,Kentucky ,to
take stock in turnpike roads,and forother purposes.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the county court for the county of Casey ,
a majority of the justicesof the peace forsaid county
being present and concurring therein,are hereby au-
thorized to take stock to the amount of five hundred
dollars to the mile in the Liberty and Rolling Fork
Turnpike Road Company , and in the Liberty and
Casey's Creek Turnpike Road Company .
§ 2. In order to carry out the provisionsof section
one of this act, the county court of Casey county ,
Kentucky , a majorityof the justicesof the peace for
said county being presentand concurring therein, are
hereby authorized to levy an ad valorem tax, not to
exceed twenty -fivecents on each one hundred dollars '
worth of taxableestatein said county liableto taxа-
tion for State revenue in any one year, and a per
capita tax, not to exceed twenty -fivecents on each
voter in any one year,which tax shallbe used by said
county court to pay for stock subscribed on roads
mentioned in section one of this act ; said tax shall
be collected in the same manner and at the same time
the State revenue is collected .
§ 3. This actshalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 4,1890 .

CHAPTER 568 .
AN ACT to incorporate the Burlington and Union Turnpike Road
Company .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That Joseph F. Weaver ,E. H. Blankenleeker ,
L. E. Clore, James M. Barlow , J. A. Huey , Senior,
W. F. McKein and Ezra Blankenleeker be,and they
are hereby,incorporatedas a body -politicand corpo-
rate, under the name and style of the Burlington and
Union Turnpike Road Company , and by said name
and styleshallhave power to contractand be con-
tracted with, sue and be sued, and are hereby in-
vested with all the powers incident and usual to such
corporations, for the purpose of building a turnpike
road in Boone county,Kentucky , from a point at or
near E. W. Weaver's blacksmith shop , on the Flor-
ence and Union Turnpike ,to Jeffersonstreetin the
town of Burlington, upon the most practicalroute
between the two points as may be determined by the
board of directors .
§2 . The capital stock of said company shall be
divided into shares of twenty -fivedollars each,and
the entirecapitalshallnot exceed the amount neces-
sary to construct said road and toll-houses for the
same .
§ 3. The persons named in this act are hereby de-
clared to be the present board of directorsof said
company , and shallhold their officeuntil theirsuc-
cessorsare duly electedand qualified. Said directors
shall elect one of their number president, one treas-
urer, and one secretaryof said company , and allsaid
persons are appointed commissioners, and any of said
persons are hereby appointed and authorized to re-
ceive subscriptions to the capital stock of said com-
pany .
§ 4. That for the purpose of constructingsaidroad

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 167
thedirectors may receivegrants of right of way and
materialfor the constructionand operation of said
road, and in the event they can not agree with the
ownersof the soiland material,they may proceed
and have the same condemned , as provided by law.
Forthe purpose of locatingsaid road it shallbe law-
fulforthe president and directorsto enter with their
surveyor, chain carriersand engineers upon the land
and inclosures and county roads, through which the
intendedroad passes, to examine and survey grounds
suitable, and to examine quarries,gravel and other
materials suitable for the construction of said road .
$ 5. Said company shall have the right to erect a
toll-gate as soon as ithas completed two milesof con-
secutiveroad , and upon itscompletion may erectone
gate for each five miles, or, if they desire, may es-
tablishhalf -fare gates for each two and one -half miles
I ofroad completed ; but the ratesof tollshallat no
timebe greater than is now providedby theGeneral
Statutesof Kentucky.
§6 . The annual election of officersfor said com-
pany shall be held on the firstSaturday in May , and
thetreasurerelectedshallgive bond with good secur-
ityfor the faithfulperformance of his duties, and
thathe will pay out and account for allmoney that
may come to his hands as treasurer, which bond shall
be acceptedand approved by the board of directors.
7. That the width of said road shall not exceed
fortyfeet; that the width of gravel or stoneon said
roadshall not exceed sixteen feet nor lessthan ten
feet,and such thicknessas may be prescribed by the
board of directors, and the average grade of said road
shallnot exceed fivedegrees.
8. That the provisions of chapter one hundred
and ten of the General Statutes of Kentucky , and
| theamendments thereto,so faras they are not incon-
sistentwith this act, are made part of thisact.
$ 9. This act to be enforced from itspassage.
Approved April 4, 1890.

one. The said partiesnamed as trusteesshallcause

and corporate ,by the name and styleof the Board of

Trusteesof the Town of Croppers,and by that name
may sue and be sued,plead and be impleaded , con-
tractand be contractedwith, in allcourtsand places
in this Commonwealth ; may have and use a corpo-
rate seal,and alterthe same at pleasure. The pru-
dential and financial affairs of said town shall be

alty; they shallhave charge of the streetsand alleys.
and public property, as designated by the plat here-
inafterprovided for, or which may hereafterbe estab-
lished .

shal, and in allcasesshallbe in the name of the Com-

teringupon the dischargeof theirduties.

ceeding twenty -five cents on the one hundred dol-

collected by the town marshal ; and with a view of

ulationsas may be necessaryfor the peace and good
order of the town .

to the same fees as a justiceof the peace forsimilar
services, and the town marshal shallbe entitledto the
same fees as a constable for similar services .
§ 9. The board of trusteesshall cause a survey and
plat of saidtown to be made , which shallbe recorded
in the officeof the Shelby County Court, and shall
pay for same out of town treasury .
§ 10. The board of trustees may appoint a treas-
urer, who shall execute bonds , with surety, to be
approved by the board,and whose term of officeshall
be for one year, and may be removed by the board at
pleasure .

tees for municipal purposes, and choses in action

taxation .

False Registration .
§ 14. Any person who shall cause himself to be
registeredin more than one election ward , other-
wise than is provided in sectioneleven of this act,
or more than once in the same ward , or who shall
cause himself to be registered, knowing that he is
not lawfully entitledto registration, and any per-
son who shall aid or abet in the commission of any
of said acts, shall be deemed guilty of a misde-
meanor , and shallbe imprisoned in the county jail
not less than one nor more than twelve months .
False Registration Books .
§ 15. Any officerof registrationor other person,
who shallunlawfully alterany registrationbook , or
add any name thereto,or who shall willfullysecrete,
suppress, or destroy any such book , or who shall
make or aid in making any falseor fraudulent regis-
trationbook , shall be deemed guilty of felony, and

shallbe confined in the penitentiarynot less than one
nor more than five years,and shall forfeitany office
he then holds , and shallforeverbe disqualifiedfrom
holding office.
Perjury .
§16 . Any person who shall willfullymake any
falsestatement , under any oath lawfullyadministered
under this act, shall be deemed guilty of perjury,
and confined in the penitentiarynot lessthan one nor
more than five years .
Obstructing Registration.
§17 . Any person who , by himself or in aid of
others, shallforciblybreak up or attempt to break up
a registrationheld under this act, or shall forcibly
prevent or attempt to prevent any person from ap-
proaching or entering a place of registrationfor the
purpose of registering, shall be fined not less than
one hundred nor more than five hundred dollars, or
imprisoned not less than six nor more than twelve
months in the county jail,or both .
§18 . No witness shall be excused from testifying
before any grand jury, or in any prosecution or in-
dictment under this act,on the ground that his testi-
mony may criminate himself; but no such testimony
shall be used against him in any prosecution,except
forperjury in giving the same .
Fees .

shall receive the following fees, and no other : For
copying the registrylistsrequiredto be kept in his
office, or to be used insupplyinglostbooks,the sum
of two cents for each voter whose name isso copied ;
for his services under section twelve of this act, the
sum of twenty -fivecents for the name of each voter
stricken off the lists; for receiptingto clerksof regis-
tration and electionfor registrationbooks , the sum
of five cents for each book receiptedfor. The mar

shal shallreceiveforservingthe noticesprovided for
in sectiontwo the sum of twenty-fivecents for each
actual service. Officersof registrationshall receive-
two dollarsper day for each day employed in attend .

trationand electionshallnot be entitledto any pay ,
except upon the production of the cityclerk'sreceipt
for theirrespectiveregistrationbooks , which receipt
shall be the voucher of the officerpaying them . The
cityof Maysville shallpay allfees and expenses in-
curred under thisact for registration.
§ 20. A copy of this act shall be printed in each
registrationbook made under itsprovisions.
§ 21. This act shall take effect and be in force from .
and after itspassage .

CHAPTER 585 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Peewee Valley Hotel Company .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Peewee Valley Hotel Company be ,
and ishereby,authorizedto increaseitscapitalstock
from fiftythousand to two hundred thousand dol-
lars, and that said company have perpetual succes-
sion .
§2 . That the articlesof incorporationof said com-
pany are hereby declared legal and valid, as if the
same had been published within the time prescribed
by chapter fifty-six of the General Statutes,said pub-
licationhaving been in fact made ,but not within the
period prescribedby saidchapter.
§ 3. This act shall take effectand be in force from
itspassage.
Approved April 7,1890.

CHAPTER 586 .
AN ACT toprohibitthe saleofspirituous,vinousor malt liquorswithin
two and one -half miles of the Methodist Church house or common
school-house in Campton ,in Wolfe county.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful for any person to sell
orvend spirituous, vinous or malt liquors, or a mix
ture of either , at or within two and one -half miles of
the Methodist Church house or the common school-
house in Campton , Wolfe county.
§2 . That any person who shallsellor vend , in any
manner whatever , any spirituous, vinous or malt
liquors, or the mixture of either,at or within two and
one -half miles of the Methodist Church house or com-
mon school-house inCampton , Wolfe county,shallbe
deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and for each offense
shallbe fined not lessthan twenty nor more than five
hundred dollars .
§3 . Prosecutions fora violationof the provisionsof
thisact shall be by indictment in the Wolfe Circuit
Court .
§4 . This act to take effectand be in force from its
passage .
Approved April 7,1890.
CHAPTER 587 .
AN ACT to authorizeand empower the Wolfe County Court to levy
and collectan ad valorem and pollor capitationtax toraisefundsto
build a jailand poor-house.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Wolfe County Court,a majorityof the
justicesof thepeaceofsaidWolfe countybeingpresent

202 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
and concurringtherein,shallhave power tolevyand
collectan ad valorem tax of not exceeding twenty -five
cents on each one hundred dollars' worth of personal
and realproperty in saidcounty, and a pollor capita-
tiontax of not exceeding two dollarsupon each legal
voterin saidcounty ,each year for fiveyears from the
passage of thisact,in additionto the tax now allowed
by law.
§ 2. That the sheriffof Wolfe county shallcollect
the taxes authorized by this act,and be responsible
forthe collectionand the accounting for the same as
now provided by law for the collectionof the county
levy .
§3 That the firstlevy made under this act shall be
at the October court of claims for saidcounty in one
thousand eighthundred and ninety,and at the annual
term of said court in each year thereafter, if neces-
sary,untila sum necessaryislevied,not, however , for
more than four years afterfirstlevy ismade .
§ 4. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 7,1890.
CHAPTER 588 .
AN ACT making it unlawful for stock to run at large on certain
turnpikes in Spencer county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of theCom-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That itshallbe unlawful for any hogs or swine
to run at large upon the Taylorsvilleand Snider's
Turnpike , in Spencer county,at any point thereon be-
tween Taylorsvilleand LittleMount in said county ,
or on the Taylorsvilleand Waterford Turnpike in
said county, at any point thereon between Taylors-
villeand Waterford , or on the Louisvilleand Tay-
lorsvilleTurnpike on any point within said Spencer
county .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 203
2. The owner of any stockfound runningatlarge
on said turnpikes , described in the firstsection of
thisact, shall, upon conviction,be finedin any sum
not exceeding ten dollarsfor each offense, to be re-
coverableby warrant in the name of the Common-
wealthbefore any justiceof thepeaceof saidSpencer
county .
§3 . This act to take effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 7,1890.

CHAPTER 589 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof thecityofLouisvillein regardtothe
salary of the assessorof the cityof Louisville.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That hereafterthe salaryof the assessorof the
cityof Louisville shall be such sum , not exceeding
threethousand dollarsperannum ,as may be fixedby
thegeneral council; said salaryto be paid monthly .
§2 . This act to take effect from and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 7, 1890.

fiftycents, and fortwo horsesand scraper,one dollar
and fiftycents; and it is further ordered, that no

them from the delinquent hands on their road

thecourt of claims ; and he shallalsocertifyfor what

therefore ,

§ 4. It shallbe the duty of the county treasurer
herein provided for to receive, take charge of, and
account for allmoney that he may be directedby the
Harrison County Court to do , and to pay over said
money as he may be directedby saidcourton what-
ever claims or debts he may be so directed to pay
same on . He shallkeep a book ,to be furnished and

partof the records thereof, and shallat alltimes be

§5 . It shall be the duty of the county judge and
county attorney of said county to make annual settle-
ments with the county treasurer, and the same shall
be reported to the court of claims at itsApril térm
for confirmation ; and if no exceptions be taken at
the aforesaid court , it shall be confirmed and said
report ordered to record .
§ 6. At the expiration of the term of officeof the
county treasurer, he shallsettlehis accounts as above
stated, and shall pay and deliverto his successorall
county funds , officebooks and accounts in his cus-
tody .
§7 . If a vacancy occursby deathor resignation,the
county judge is empowered to fillthe vacancy until
the next regular electionas above provided for.
$ 8. The county court shallprovide for the compen-
sation of the county treasurer; but the compensation
in no event shall exceed two hundred and fiftydol-
lars.
§9 . This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved April7,1890.

CHAPTER 607 .
AN ACT for thebenefitof the countycourtof Breckinridgecounty.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That it shall be lawful for the county court of
Breckinridge county ,composed of the presidingjudge
and a majority of the justicesof the peace for said
county, to levy a capitationtax for county purposes,
inany sum not exceeding threedollars.
§2 . That allactsin conflictherewith are hereby re-
pealed. But this act shall in no way affectthe pro-
visions of an act, entitled " An act for the benefit of
the county court of Breckinridge county," approved
December fifteenth, one thousand eight hundred and
seventy -three .
3. That thisact shalltake effectand be in force
from and after itspassage .
Approved April 7,1890.

CHAPTER 608 .
AN ACT to increasethejurisdictionof magistratesin Walton mag-
isterialdistrictin Boone county.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of theCom-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the jurisdictionof magistratesin Walton
magisterial districtshall be increased so as to give the
magistratesjurisdictionin allcaseswhere the amount
in controversy (exclusiveof interestand cost) does
not exceed the sum of two hundred dollars .
§2 . This act shall take effectand be in force from
and afteritspassage.
Approved April 7, 1890.
CHAPTER 609 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the Perryvilleand Mitchelsburg
Turnpike Road Company .
WHEREAS , The charter of the Perryville and Mit-
chelsburg Turnpike Road Company requires of the
directors of said road to erect and maintain a wooden
bridgeoverChaplin creek,acrosswhich saidroad runs,
and to keep the said bridge covered ; and whereas ,
the directors of said road have erected an iron bridge
over said stream in lieu of the wooden bridge pro-
vided for in said charter; therefore,
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the charterof the said turnpike road be ,
and the same is hereby , amended so that the directors
of said road may maintain an iron bridge over said
creek on said road without being covered, in lieu of
the covered wooden bridge provided for in the charter
of said road .
§ 2. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage . Approved April7,1890.

builda bridge acrossRough river,between Ohio and Grayson coun-
ties.

monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the court of claims of Ohio county is
hereby authorized and empowered to appropriatesuch
a sum of money as they may deem proper to aid in
the construction of a bridge acrossRough river, be-
tween Ohio and Grayson counties; and said court
shallhave power to levy and collectany additional
necessarytax for the purpose above-named .
2. This act shalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April7,1890.

one thousand eight hundred and seventy-six, as re-

When this act takes effect and becomes operative, the notes or other evidence of debt held by the board of sinking fund commissioners against the city of Henderson shall be delivered to the common council, and not paid by said city. At the expiration of said twenty years, for the purpose of paying off the said bonded debt, said board shall retain its resources and funds, and may loan out same, or invest same in good interest-bearing securities. After thirty years from the date of said bonds, said board may pay off such of said bonds as it may deem proper. The board of sinking fund commissioners shall continue as now constituted, that is, viz: The mayor of the city of Henderson, the president of the Henderson Branch of the Farmers' Bank of Kentucky, the president of the Henderson National Bank, and their successors, shall, *ex officio*, together with two persons to be appointed annually by the common council of the city of Henderson, constitute the board of the sinking fund commissioners.

## CHAPTER 625.

AN ACT to amend an act, entitled "An act to incorporate the town of Pellville, in Hancock county," approved February twenty-fifth, one thousand eight hundred and seventy.

*Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky:*

That said act to which this is an amendment be so amended:

§ 1. That said marshal shall be liable on his official bond for all taxes, dues and demands listed with him for collection in the same manner that sheriffs are under the laws of the State, and suit on his bond may be instituted in the name of "The Trustees of Pellville," in any court of competent jurisdiction.

§ 2. That said marshal shall have the same power to collect taxes due said town by garnishment as is now given to sheriffs of this Commonwealth by the provisions of sections one, two, three and four of article ten of chapter ninety-two, General Statutes. The notice prescribed by section one as aforesaid may summon the person or persons owing the tax or taxes before the Pellville Police Court, and said court shall have the same power to hear and fully determine any garnishment for taxes due said town as the county court now has to hear and determine such cases for taxes due the Commonwealth; and all sales for taxes shall be made at some public place in the corporate limits of said town, of which written or printed notices shall be posted at three public places in said town, stating the kind of property levied upon, the amount of tax due, and the amount of cost incurred in the collection of the same, for at least ten days previous to the day of sale by the marshal.

essarybuildingsand appliances.

ferableonly upon the books of saidcompany .

each day the same may remain after the expiration of said notice, unless obstructions are released by the officers of said road.

§ 8. That no person shall be eligible as president or director who is not the owner of one or more shares of stock in said road; and in the election of president and directors, each stockholder shall be entitled to one vote for each share of stock so owned, and may be cast in person or by written proxy.

§ 9. The annual election of officers for said company shall be held on the first Saturday in August, at such places as the officers may designate from year to year, who shall continue in office for one year, and until their successors are elected and qualified.

§ 10. The said company are also empowered to build and construct a bridge across Mud Lick creek, or may pave said creek, as the officers may deem best, and collect toll as provided in General Statutes.

§ 11. That all persons owning or occupying land adjoining said road, and having or opening road-ways or passways to and from said road, shall place culverts or bridges or stone gutters over and across the ditches of the turnpike road so as to avoid destroying or obstructing said ditches; and upon failure so to do, shall be liable to the company for such damage as may be caused thereby.

§ 12. Private property of the stockholders shall be exempt from all corporate debts and liabilities, except the amount subscribed for the building of the said road. The private property of the officers of the company shall be exempt from any damage that may occur to any one on or along said road.

§ 13. That this act take effect from its passage.

Approved April 7, 1890.

CHAPTER 629.

AN ACT to incorporate the Newton and Little Elkhorn Turnpike Company, in Scott county.

*Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky:*

§ 1. That Edwin Ward, Franklin Brock, Charles Hall, L. F. Ferguison, T. L. Carrick, W. O. Carrick, H. C. Funk and R. B. Wallace, are hereby created and constituted a body-politic and corporate, under the name and style of the Newton and Little Elkhorn Turnpike Road Company, with the powers usual and incident to such companies, not inconsistent with the laws of this State.

§ 2. The capital stock of said company shall not exceed ten thousand dollars, to be divided into shares of twenty-five dollars each; and upon the subscription of five hundred dollars, said above-named incorporators may convene the stockholders and elect a board of directors, and proceed to the transaction of the business of the company, the object of which is to build a turnpike road from Newton, Scott county, Kentucky, to an intersection of the Hame's Mill and Marion Church Turnpike, at or near the corner of the Burch farm, in said county.

§ 3. The Scott County Court is authorized and empowered to subscribe stock to any fractional part of a mile of said road, in the proportion of one thousand dollars to the mile, said county court being now empowered to subscribe one thousand dollars for every mile of turnpike road built in said county; and said court may also appropriate, in its discretion, such sums of money as it may deem necessary to construct a bridge over Boyd's run.

§ 4. The board of directors shall make such rules and by-laws for the management of the business of the company as the law requires.

§ 5. This act shall take effect from its passage.

Approved April 7, 1890.

AN ACT to fund the present bonded indebtednessof the town of Rus-
sellville,Kentucky .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the mayor and council of the town of
Russellvilleshallhave authorityto fund the present
bonded indebtedness of said town whenever it shall
become due by issuingcoupon bonds ,bearing six per
cent. interest, payable semi-annually; the bonds due
in thirtyyears from the date of their issuance, but
redeemable at the pleasure of the council after the
expirationof ten years, and by substitutingsame for

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 271
the present outstanding bonds . Said bonds shallbe
signed by the mayor and clerk.
§2 . The council shallhave the power to pledge the
revenue of said town forthe payment of interestand
principalof said bonds .
§3 . The council shall have power under the pro
visions of this act to issue bonds of the denomination
of one hundred dollars, fivehundred dollarsor one
thousand dollars, or all of said denominations, in
their discretion .
§4 . The council shall cause to be kept by the
town clerk, under their supervision,a bond register,
in which the exact financial condition of the town ,
atany time , may be ascertained.
§5 . This act shallbe in forcefrom itspassage.
Approved April 7,1890 .

dred and eighty-five, from the Henderson County
Court, a licenseto sell, by retail, spirituous, vinous
and malt liquors in the town of Cairo, Henderson
county , forone year from date, and paid thereforone
hundred dollars. At the time he procured said license
he had a license from the town of Cairo , Henderson
county,which expiredon the secondday of August ,
one thousand eight hundred and eighty-five. On ap-
plicationto the town of Cairo,Henderson county, said
town trustees, acting therefor, refused and now re-
fuse to issue to said A. Meahl a license to retail such
liquorsany longer; therefore,

CHAPTER 640 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof the suretiesof J. C. Holloway ,late sheriff
of Lyon county .
WHEREAS , On the sixth day of February, one
thousand eight hundred and seventy-eight, an act
was passed by the Legislatureof Kentucky,entitled
" An act for the benefitof the tax-payers of Lyon

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 273
county," under which act four levieswere laid upon
the property of the tax-payers of Lyon county for
thepurpose mentioned in said act; and whereas,said
J. C. Holloway , as sheriffof Lyon county, under-
took the collectionof said levies,but paid over only
a part of the money collected, and judgments were
recovered by the treasurerof Lyon county against
saidHolloway and his suretiesupon his bonds exe-
cuted for the collectionof said leviesupon the
amount he had failedthus to pay over ; and whereas ,
the whole of said J. C. Holloway's propertyhas been,
or soon will be, exhausted in satisfactionof saidjudg-
ments , and stilla considerableamount of money will
remain to be paid thereon by said sureties; and
whereas , if said money was to be paid by said sure
ties, it could not now be used for the purposes pro
vided in said act by reason of changes in the condition
of affairs in Lyon county since saidlevieswere laid.
Since the enforcement of the payment of the remain-
der of saidleviesby saidsuretiescould onlyresultin
the financialruin of severalgood citizensof Lyon
county ; therefore,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the suretiesof J. C. Holloway be, and
arehereby , releasedfrom any and allfurtherliability
upon the bond of said J. C. Holloway .
2. This act to take effectfrom and after itspas-
sage .
[Became a law April4,1890,withouttheapprovalof theGovernor.]

CHAPTER 660.

AN ACT regulating the making, altering and repairing county roads of Marion county

*Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky:*

§ 1. That before any county road surveyor shall have power to use, at the expense of the county, any wheel carriage or other implement for the purpose of making, altering or repairing any county road, he shall have the written permission of the nearest commissioned magistrate to said road, stating the amount of hauling or other work such surveyor may have done, and should such magistrate deem it necessary, in order to know the condition of the roads, to examine the same, he may do so, and shall have for his services one dollar per day for the time he may be actually engaged, not to exceed, however, five dollars for any one year.

travelingpublic, and not untilsuch road shallhave
been well graded and ditched .

the amount of his poll-tax for that year .
§ 10. All claims allowed under thisact shallbe paid
through the sheriffof the county, and the sheriff
shallnot pay out any money on such road claim for
the benefitof any person until all taxes due from
said person for the year in which the labor was per-
formed and the year next preceding said year shall
have been paid .
§ 11. Should any magistrate or county road sur-
veyor violateany provision of this act, he shall be
deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and ,upon convic-
tion, shall be fined in any sum not exceeding one
hundred dollars .
§12. This act shallapply only to Marion county ;
and all laws and parts of laws in conflictwith the
provisionsof this act are hereby repealed.
§ 13. This act shall take effectfrom and after the
firstday of June , one thousand eight hundred and
ninety .
Approved April 9,1890.

CHAPTER 680 .
AN ACT to regulatethe jurisdictionof justicesof the peace in the city
of Louisville .
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Whenever there is issued, by a justiceof the
peace, in the city of Louisville, an attachment for
rent,or a distresswarrant,and the amount thereof,
exclusive of interestand costs, does not exceed the
sum of one hundred dollars,said attachment or dis-
tresswarrant shallbe returned before the justice of
thepeacewho issuedthe same,togetherwithany and
allbonds which may be given concerning said attach-
ment or distresswarrant,and said justiceshall have
exclusivejurisdictionof all matterspertainingto
said proceedings, subject to the right of appeal or
change of venue as now provided.
§ 2. All laws, publicand private, inconsistenthere-
with, are hereby repealed.
§ 3. This law to take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April 10,1890 .

354 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 689 .
AN ACT to prevent stock from running at large on that portion of the
Mount Sterlingand Winchester Turnpike Road lying in Montgomery
county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be unlawful for live stock of any
kind to run at large on the Mount Sterling and Win-
chesterTurnpike Road lying in the county of Mont-
gomery .
§ 2. Any person or persons,being the owner or own-
ersof, or having the custody or controlof livestock
of any kind, who shallsufferor permit any of them
to run at largeon any portionof the lineof said turn-
pike road belonging to the Mount Sterlingand Win-
chesterTurnpike Road Company lying in the county
of Montgomery , shall be fined for each and every
offense not less than two nor more than ten dollars,
recoverableby warrant in the name of the Common-
wealth before any county or policejudge or justice
of the peace in Montgomery county .
§ 3. Unless said fineispaid or replevied, the stock
so running at large shall be subject to the fine and
costs, and an execution shall be levied upon the
same ,and the officershall proceed to collectthe fine
and costs as in case of other executions ; and none of
saidstockso running at largeupon saidturnpike road
shall be exempt from execution under this act ; the
fine so collected shall be paid to the trustee of the
jury fund of saidcounty, as other finesare paid .
§ 4. This act shall take effect and be in force fron
and afterthe firstday of May , one thousand eigh
hundred and ninety ; and notice of this act shall b
posted at three of the most public placeson the lin
of said turnpike road for two weeks before the da
this law takes effect.
Approved April 10, 1890 .

pany for any purpose whatever .

WHEREAS , Doubts have been raisedas to the legali-
tyof the electionof trusteesof the public schools of
Winchester , held in February , one thousand eight
hundred and ninety ; and whereas, those acting as
trustees are anxious to have these doubts put to rest ;
now , therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an electionbe held at the public school
building, on the north-west corner of Hickman and
Highland streets,in Winchester , on the fourth Satur-
day inApril , one thousand eighthundred and ninety,
forthe purpose of electingfivetrusteesof the public
schools of Winchester , who shallhave the qualifica-
tions now required by law of such trustees. Said
election shall be held as an election of councilmen of
Winchester is now required to be held, except that
thesuperintendent of common schoolsof Clark coun-
ty shall appoint the officersthereof. The fiveper-
sons receiving the highest number of votes at said
electionshall hold the officeof trusteesof said pub-
lic schools until the regular election of board of
councilmen of Winchester in one thousand eight
hundred and ninety-four, or until their successors
are duly elected and qualified. After the election
herein provided for, trusteesof said public schools
shall be elected when councilmen of Winchester are
chosen, and at electionsto be held at the same time
and place and beforethe same officers.
§2 . That at the electionprovided for in the forego-
ingsection there shallbe taken a vote,and an election
heldon thequestionof issuingbonds forthepurpose
ofbuilding a school house in Winchester ,as provided

for in section two of an act,entitled" An act to amend
an act, entitled'An act to provide for organizing and
establishinga system of public schools in Winches-
ter, " approved February fifth, one thousand eight
hundred and ninety; and if a majority of the votes
cast at said election shall be " For the School -house |
Tax," then the trusteeselected, as providedfor in
the firstsectionof this act, shall have the powers |
conferredon them with referenceto building a school-
house or school-houses in Winchester,issuingbonds ,
and levying taxes to dischargesame ,and allthe other
power and authority given them by said act of Feb-
ruary fifth, one thousand eight hundred and ninety ,
which shall, in all respects,apply as if the election
had been calledand held under and pursuant to said
act. If said electionshould result in a majority
againstthe school-house tax,then the trusteeselected,
as provided for in the firstsectionof this act, shall
have power to re-submit the question as authorized in
the second sectionof said act of February fifth, one
thousand eighthundred and ninety.
§ 3. That all acts or parts of acts inconsistent here-
with are hereby repealed.
§ 4. That this act shall go into effectfrom and after
itspassage .
Approved April 10,1890 .

CHAPTER 703 .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 387
tracted with, sue and be sued, implead and be im-
pleaded , make and have a common seal, and alter
and renew the same at pleasure; may acquireby pur-
chase, lease or otherwise, receive,hold and enjoy all
such estate,real,personaland mixed ,as may be neces-
sary or convenient for the purposes of the corpora-
tion, and the same , from time to time, may sell
convey , lease, mortgage , pledge or otherwise alien
and dispose of; may adopt and enforce such by-
laws, rules and regulations,as may be necessaryor
convenient for the maintenance of the corporation
not contrary to the constitutionallaws of this Com-
monwealth .
§2 . Said corporationshallhave power and author-
ityto mine ,bore for,develop,produce,utilize,refine,
prepare, transportand storeironore,coal,limestone,
fire-clay, salt, petroleumand otheroils, naturalgas,
and any other minerals upon lands owned or con-
trolled by said corporation; and as common carriers
to transport, in any usual or convenient manner ,any
such products ,and allmineral products,and convert
the same , or any product thereof, into such articles,
implements or materialsas may be deemed advisable.
To these ends the corporation may purchase, con-
struct, maintain, or otherwiseacquireand conduct,or
cause to be conducted, any such furnaces, foundries,
pipe lines, machinery, factories, offices, warehouses,
buildings,and other structuresand modes of transpor-
tation or communication which may be necessaryor
convenient for carrying into effectthe powers herein
granted . Said corporationshallhave power ,by con-
tractor arrangement with any person or persons, cor-
poration or corporations,toprovideforthebuilding,
construction,maintenance or management of any mine
or mines , furnaces, modes of transportation, or other
work or works hereinauthorized; to furnishin money
or by way of material,laboror otherwise,the whole
or any part of thecapitaldesiredby such personsor
corporations, eitherby way of loan or advances of

388 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
money or of credit, or by purchase of the bonds ,
stocks, notes or other securitiesof such persons or
corporations; and to cause the loans or advances to
be secured by mortgage ,pledge or otherwise, as may
be agreed upon between the contractingparties. Said
corporationmay likewiseindorse and guarantee the
payment of the notes or bonds and the performance
of the obligationsof other corporationsor persons ;
and assume , become responsible for, execute and
carry out any contracts, leasesor sub-leasesmade by
any corporationor persons with other persons or cor-
porations similarin characterto, and within the gen-
eral purview of, the purposes and objectsof thisact ;
but nothing in this act shall confer the power to
constructor operate a railroadas a common carrier.
§ 3. Said corporationshallhave power , in the man-
ner and on the terms prescribed, with reference to
condemnations by railroadand turnpike companies, in
an act of the General Assembly of Kentucky , ap-
proved April eleventh, one thousand eighthundred
and eighty-two, entitled"An act to prescribethe
mode of condemning land for the use of railroadand
turnpike companies," to condemn such lands as may
be necessary to the corporation for the purpose of
constructing, maintaining and operatingsuch conduit
or conduits, pipe line or pipe lines, and telegraph
lines used in connection therewith , and other works
necessary or convenient to itsbusinessas a common
carrier, as herein provided for. Said corporationshall
have the power to fixand regulatetollsor charges for
freightor property transportedby it, and for mes .
sages sent over itstelegraph lines,subject, however ,
to such regulationsin this respect as may be pre-
scribedby general or speciallaws of this Common-
wealth .
§ 4. Said corporationmay ,by contract, consolidate
its business, property and franchiseswith those of
any othercompany or companies locatedin thisState
or elsewhere, to such extent, and on such terms and

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 389 .
conditions as may be agreed upon : Provided , That
such consolidationshallbe authorizedor approved by
a vote in itsfavor of a majority of the entirecapital
stock of thiscorporation.
§ 5. The capitalstock of the company shallconsist
of ten thousand shares of one hundred dollars each ,
being one million dollars. The corporation may ,
from time to time, increase the same by a vote of
the holders of a majority of the capitalstock pres-
ent or represented at any annual or specialmeet-
ing to such an amount as may be deemed needful for
the business of the company . Until the electionof
the board of directors,as hereinafterprovided, said
capital'stock, or any part thereof, may be subscribed
for and paid in at the times and upon the terms to be
determined by the said corporatorsor a majority of
them ; but after said board of directors shall be
elected, the said capitalstock then unsubscribed, or
any increase thereof,as often as made ,shallbe sub-
scribed for and paid in at the times and upon the
terms , and be transferablein the manner to be deter-
mined by the board of directors. The board of direct-
ors, if thereto authorized by the stockholders, may
receive subscriptionsfor stock to be paid in property,
real, personal or mixed , or any other valuable thing
or service, upon terms to be agreed upon with sub-
scribers . When not less than one thousand shares
shallhave been subscribedthecorporationmay begin
business , and the share -holders shall elect not less
than three nor more than thirteen directors to serve
until the next annual election, or until their suc-
cessorsshallbe duly electedand qualified; and the
directors so elected and their successors may , and
they are hereby authorizedand empowered to have
and to exercise, in the name and behalf of this com-
pany , allthe rights,powers and privilegeswhich are
hereby given or intendedto be given,subjectonlyto
such liabilitiesas other share-holdersaresubjectto,
which liabilitiesare no more than forthe payment to

390 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the company of the sums due or to become due on the
sharesheld by them . Should the capitalstock at any
time be increased,the stockholders,atthe time of such
increase,shallbe entitledto subscribepro rata,accord-
ing to their respectiveholdings, for such increase,
within such time and upon such terms as may be fixed
by the orderallowingsuch increase;and inthe event of
a failure of the stockholders to subscribe for the whole
of such increase, the directorsmay dispose of the
same to otherpersons. Whenever an increaseof cap-
italstock is made , a certificatethereof, duly executed
under the corporate seal of the company , and signed
by the presidentand secretary,shallbe filedwith the
Secretaryof the Stateof Kentucky beforethe increase

§ 6. The principalofficesof the saidcompany shall
be in the cityof Louisville; but the directors, under
such rulesand regulationsas they may prescribe,may
establishbranches or agencies,or meet in other parts
of the State or elsewhere .
§ 7. After the year one thousand eighthundred and
ninety, the directorsshallbe electedannually by the

year ; and shallelectfrom their number , at the first
meetingof theboardaftertheirelection,a president,
and shallalsohave power to electfrom their number
or otherwise a vice-president,a treasurerand secre-
tary,and such otherofficers,clerks and agents as the
business of the company may require; all elections
fordirectorsshallbe by ballot, and every stockholder
shall be entitledto one vote, in person or by proxy ,
foreach share of stock held by him ; but no person

to the amount of ten shares .

in two newspapers published in the city of Louis-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 391
holders, one of whom shall act as judge, and the

§ 9. The board of directors shall make all by -laws

pany ; the said directorsshallhave power , by a vote

§ 10. The privateproperty of the incorporatorsand

§ 11. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .

mittedtodealin the same ; alsoinbillsof exchange ,

take personal and other securitytherefor,and dispose

WHEREAS , The board of trustees of the town of
Falmouth having been heretofore authorized to issue
bonds for the payment of six miles of turnpike owned
by said town ; and whereas, there is remaining and
unpaid thirteenthousand dollars of said indebted-
ness, due June , one thousand eight hundred and
ninety, and there not being sufficientmoney in the
treasuryof saidtown to pay saidbonds ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 423
Be it enactedby theGeneralAssembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :

mouth are authorized to issue bonds of said town in :
an amount sufficientto refund or pay offthe bonds
and interest due June , one thousand eight hundred
and ninety , the amount to be raised not to exceed
thirteen thousand dollars. Said bonds shall be pay-
able at the Falmouth Deposit Bank , at Falmouth ,
Kentucky , not lessthan two nor more than ten years
from the date of their issue, and shall not bear more
than six per cent . per annum interest. The bonds
issued under this act shall be in denomination not
less than one hundred nor more than one thousand
dollars ; and when negotiated,shallbe signed by the
policejudge of the town of Falmouth, and counter-
signed by the clerk of said town, in theirofficial
capacity ; and when so signed and deliveredto the

terest on said bonds shall be paid semi-annually at

denced by coupons attached to said bonds , each cou-
pon to be signed by said clerk of said town ,and rep-
resenting six months ' interest on the bond to which it
isattached. Presentmentofsaidcouponstosaidbank,
at or after maturity, shallauthorizepayment of same .
Said board of trusteesof said town ishereby author-
ized to levy annually an ad valorem tax on all real
and personal property in said town subject to taxa-
tion, not exceeding twenty -fivecentson each hundred
dollars' worth in any one year,which tax, when so
levied, shall be collectedby the collectorof said town
as other town taxes are collected, and he shall be
liable on his bond as collector for all taxes collected
under this act ; and said taxes,when collected,shall
be applied to the payment of the bonds and interest
issued under this act. It shallbe the duty of said
clerk to keep a record,showing the date of the issue
of said bonds ,theirrespectivenumbers ,when payable,

on his bond for same , and shall disburse same for the
purposes herein stated under the directionof said
board of trustees .
§ 2. All acts and parts of acts in conflictwith the
provisionsof thisact are hereby repealed.

CHAPTER 726 .
AN ACT to prescribethe liabilityof railroadcompanies to the own-
ersof stock killedor injuredby railroadcars or agents in the
counties of Ballard and Carlisle.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1. That all railroad companies owning or ope-
ratingany railroad in the counties of Ballard and
Carlisle, or that may hereafter own or operate
any railroad in said counties, shall pay fulldama-
ges to the owners of horses, mules, cattle, jacks
and jennets that they may killor damage by their
carsor agents along said roads or branches : Pro-
vided,however , That the owner has not intentionally
contributedto the killingor injuringof said stock :
And provided further, That whenever any railroad
company shall fence their track by a good fence of
wire,stone or plank ,four and one-half feethigh, and
shallkeep and maintain same in good repair, with
good and sufficientcattle-guards at allcrossings, then
such company so fencingand maintaining same shall
notbe liableforstock killedor injured by theircars
or agents, unless by the use of ordinary care they
couldhave avoided such killingor injuring.
§2 . This act shall not apply in any case where the
railroadcompany has paid the land owner for fencing
their track .
§3 . All actsin conflictwith this act are hereby re-
pealed, as far as they relateto the countiesof Ballard
and Carlisle .
§4 . This act to take effect and be in force from
and after its passage .

Approved April11,1890.

CHAPTER 734 .
AN ACT toamend an act,entitled"An acttoincorporatethe Lexing-
ton Charity Organization Society."
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act, entitled" An act to incorporate
the Lexington Charity Organization Society," ap-
proved March fifth, one thousand eight hundred and
ninety , be amended by adding the following words to
sectiontwo : " And any beggar who failsor refusesto
accept such employment or suitableassistancewhen
tendered, and isthereafterfound begging in the city
limits, shall be reported to the county judge,who
shall have such beggar arrested for vagrancy , and
brought before him ; and upon satisfactoryproof of
the offerof employment or other suitableassistance,
and of subsequent begging,shallsentence the offend-
er to the city work -house for not exceeding thirty
days : Provided , however, If the offenderbe a child
of tender years, it shall be the duty of the county
court to place such child in the custody of this so-
cietyuntil a home foritcan be found with some suit-
ableperson or charitableorganizationwhere itwillbe
reared in moral courses ; the court shall then bind it
out as now provided by law ."
§2 . That said act be further amended by adding
the following words to section four of the same :
"And the county court shallhave the power to place
orphan children, whose friendsor relativesfailor re-
fuse to provide for their necessary physical wants ,
and the children of parents of notoriouslybad moral
character, in the custody of this society, untilhomes
can be provided for them with some suitableperson
or charitableorganization. The courtshallthen bind
saidchildren out as now provided by law . The court
may , however , permit a child so taken to be returned
to itsparents without being bound out, if, in the

456 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
meantime , the said parents show abilityand willing-
ness to provide for itsnecessaryphysical wants , and
to rear it in moral courses ."
§ 3. Any person attempting clandestinelyor by
force to take a child from the legalcustody of this
societyshall, upon convictionby a jury in the county
court, be punished by a finenot exceeding twenty
dollars,and confinement in the city work -house not
more than sixty days , or both , in the discretion of the
court .
Approved April 11, 1890 .

fore,

CHAPTER 736 .

Turnpike and BridgeCompany ,inGreen county,Kentucky .

monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That W. F. Goran ,Alexander Milby , Edward
H. Scott, E. R. Vanmeter, E. H. Hobson, W. N.
Vaughan , W. W. Vaughn , Woodson Lewis, J. L.
Howell ,B. W. Allen, John J. Durham , Lucian Dur-
ham , Joseph Perry, Doctor W. M. Lewis, A. W.
Paxton , H. W. Wilson , Jordan O. Grove , L. Η .
Cabell and H. P. Buchanan , and their associates and
successors,be, and they are hereby, created a body-
politicand corporate, under the name and style of
" Greensburg , Summersville and Caney Fork Turn-
pike and Bridge Company ;" and by said name and
style shallhave power to contractand be contracted
with ,sue and be sued,plead and be impleaded , in all
the courts of this Commonwealth ; and to have and
use a common seal,and change and alterthe same at
pleasure .
§2 . That said company is hereby authorized to
build and construct a turnpike road from the town
of Summersville , in Green county , Kentucky , to
Greensburg ; and thence to the ford of Caney Fork
of Russell's creek , below the farm of J. O. Grove ,
or from Greensburg, Kentucky , to eitherof said
other two points, as said company in its discre-
tion may determine ; and also construct a bridge
across Green river, at or near Greensburg ,Kentucky ,
on the line of said turnpike, of such style and pat-
tern and material as the company may judge best
suited to the purpose ; and said company shallhave
the right to acquire, hold,use and enjoy, and have
titleto such realestate,earth, timber,gravelor stone,
or other property,as may be in or forthe construction
of said turnpike or bridge, or for eitheror both, and

458 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
may condemn land for the rightof way , and toll-
houses,gravel,stone and timber necessaryin the con-
structionof said road and bridge, or either, in the
same manner provided by law for the condemnation
of land by railroadcompanies when same can not be
obtainedby consent or purchase.
§ 3. The capitalstock of saidcompany shallnot ex-
ceed thirtythousand dollars,and shallbe divided into
shares of fiftydollarseach ; and any of the corpora-
torsnamed in this act may receivewritten subscrip-
tionsto the capitalstock of said company ,and each
stockholder shall be entitled to one vote for each
share of stock owned by him in said company at all
electionsof officers, which may be given in person
or by written proxy ; and said stock may be sub-
scribedforby individualsor corporations. The stock-
holdersshall,once in every year,electseven directors,
one of whom shallbe electedby them as president,
who may also electa treasurerand secretaryof the
company ,who shallcontinue in officeuntil their suc-
cessorsare electedand qualified. The treasurermust
givebond tothecompany forthefaithfulperformance
of his duties and for the proper payment of such
moneys as may come to his hands as treasurerafore-
said. Said president and board of directorsmay
adopt such by -laws fortheirgovernment and for the
government of their road and bridge as they may
think proper,not inconsistentwith generallaws ; and
may employ such agents as they may deem proper .
Said president and directorsmay provide for the
transferof stock when sold by a stockholder; but
said company has a lien on the respectiveshares of
stock forany unpaid subscriptionon same . Upon the
subscriptionof twenty-fiveper cent. of said capital
stock the organizationof the company may be com-
pleted and work commenced . Said directors may ,
after organization, make a call of twenty -five per
cent. of the stock subscribed, at intervalsof thirty

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 459
days, as they may desirethe same ,and each callmust
be met in twenty days .
§4. The tollscharged by saidcompany shallbe the
same as fixed by law for turnpike roads with gates
fivemiles apart ; and separate tollsmay be charged
forcrossing said bridge,at rates as may be fixed by
thedirectors,not to exceed fiftycents for four horse
team and vehicle, thirty cents for two -horse vehicle
and team , and twenty cents for single vehicle and
team, and fifteencents for horse and rider, and five
centsfor a singlefootman ;and saidcompany may put
up toll-gate and commence charging tollsso soon as
threeconsecutive miles of said road iscompleted, and
forthe bridge as soon as the same isready foruse.
§5 . Said company , through itsdirectors,is author-
ized to let out the building or making of said road
and bridge , or either, to contractors on such terms
and in such portionsas they may deem proper.
§ 6. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 11, 1890.
CHAPTER 737 .
AN ACT tocharterthe Mortonsvilleand ClearCreek Turnpike Road
Company , in Woodford county.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the Mortonsville and Clear Creek Turn-
pike Road Company be incorporatedfor the purpose
of constructing and operating a turnpike road from
Mortonsvilleto a point in the Versaillesand Mun-
day's Ferry Turnpike Road ,near G. Y. Oneal'sgate.
The exact locationof said road shall be fixedby the
commissioners hereinafter named on the best and most
practicalroute between saidpoints.
§2 . Such company , by the name designated, may
sue and be sued,contractand be contractedwith, and

460 LAWS OF KENTUCKY
buy and sell,hold and convey any real estateneeded
for the buildingor operation of said road, and do all
lawful acts in the premises.
§3 . That Samuel H. Shouse , G. Y. Oneal , L. D.
Carpenter, J. G. Boone and S. P. Oliver are hereby
appointed commissioners, and they, or any three of
them , may open books and receivesubscriptionsto
the capitalstock of said company at such times and
placesas they may choose. The capitalstock of said
company shallnot exceed six thousanddollars, and
shallbe divided intosharesof fiftydollarseach, and
the private property of the stockholdersshallbe ex-
empt from itscorporateliabilities.
§ 4. When the commissioners named , or a majority
of them , believe that enough stock has been sub-
scribed by individuals and the county court of Wood-
ford county to build said road according to such
specificationsas may be adopted by them and the
county court, then said commissioners shall, after ten
days' notice in writing of time and place, hold an
election of a board of five directors . The directors
shall hold officefor one year , and until their success-
ors are elected and qualified. Said directors shall an-
nually name one of their number president, and also
appoint a secretaryand treasurerfrom among the
stockholders. A majority of the directorsshall be a
quorum for the transactionof business of the com-
pany , and said board of directorsshall control the
affairsof the company .
§ 5. Similar elections for the election of directors
shallbe held annually, and ten days ' previous notice
of the same shall be given by the directors; the vot-
ing shallbe in person or by writtenproxy ,and each
share of stock shall be entitled to one vote .
§ 6. This act shall take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 11,1890.

CHAPTER 738 .

toxicatingliquorsin Bullittcounty," approved March twenty-first,

thereto .

sembly , entitled"An act to prohibitthe saleof intox-
icatingliquors in Bullittcounty," approved March
twenty-first,one thousand eight hundred and seventy-
one, and all amendments thereto, be, and the same
arehereby , repealed.
§ 2. This act, in as faras itprovides for the repeal
herein, shall not take effectand be in forceuntil the
same shall be approved and adopted by a majority of
the votes cast for and against same at the election
hereinafterprovided for in thisact.
§3 . The judge of the BullittCounty Court shall
make an order on the records of said court at least
sixtydays priorto the firstMonday in August,one
thousand eight hundred and ninety, directing the
sheriffof Bullittcounty to open a pollat each voting
place in each of the voting precinctsin saidcounty at
the regular August election,to be held on the first
Monday in August ,one thousand eight hundred and
ninety, to take thesenseof thequalifiedvotersof the
county as to whether they are foror againstrepeal-
ing an act to prohibitthesaleof intoxicatingliquors
in Bullitt county ,and amendments thereto. The clerk
of said court shall deliver to the sheriff of Bullitt
county a copy of said order at leastfortydays before
the firstMonday in August , one thousand eight hun-
dred and ninety .
§ 4. The sheriffof Bullittcounty shallpublish said
election for at least thirty days prior to the first
Monday in August ,one thousand eight hundred and

462 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ninety,by posting printedcopiesof said order of the
county courtin not lessthan fivepublicplacesin each
votingprecinctin said county .
§ 5. The clerk of the BullittCounty Court shall
prepare two columns on the pollbooks for each vot-
ing precinctin the county, one in which to record the
votes cast for the repeal, and the other those cast
againsttherepeal. The questionshallbe propounded
to each voter voting at said election: " Are you for
or againstrepealingthe act to prohibitthe saleof in-
toxicatingliquorsin Bullittcounty ?" and the clerk
of the election shall record the vote in accordance
with the answer . The vote shall be taken, certified
and returned, as required by the general law on the
subjectof elections. The examining board,whose duty
itisto examine and compare the poll-books in county
and State elections, shallcompare the vote cast for
and againstsaid repeal, and shall make and sign a
certificate of the number of votes cast for and the
number cast against said repeal in the county , and
shall deliver said certificateinside of five days from
the date of comparing said poll to the judge of the
BullittCounty Court,who shall safelykeep said cer-
tificateuntil the next regular term of said court ,
when he shallcause said certificateto be spread on
the order-book of saidcourt ; and if it shall appear
from said certificatethat a majority of the votes cast
for and against said repeal in the county have voted
forsaidrepeal,then thisact shallbe in forcein said
Bullittcounty from and afterthe firstday of Septem-
ber, one thousand eighthundred and ninety; but if it
shallappear thatlessthan a majorityof the votes cast
for and againstsaid repealdid not vote for the repeal ,
then this act shall not take effect or be in force .
§ 6. If it shall appear from the certificateof the
board of examiners of said vote that a majority of
the votes cast at said election were in favor of said re-
peal, then the general law prescribedin chapter one
hundred and six, General Statutes,shall be revived

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 463
and in fullforcein Bullittcounty from the firstday
of September ,one thousand eighthundred and ninety.
§ 7. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 11,1890 .

son Circuit Court for fines and forfeitures, which

number of the case; second. The stylethereof; third.
The nature of the offense ; fourth . The date of the
judgment ; fifth. The amount thereof; sixth. The
number of capias; seventh. The amount and cost
thereof; eighth. The credit, if any, thereon ; ninth.
When same is due ; tenth . Sheriff'sreturn on same ;
eleventh. Columns for remarks ; twelfth. Page on
cash-book ; thirteenth. Date of amount paid trustee.

ever .

cessorin office,and shall be evidencein any court or

amined at leastonce each month by the judge of each

quired, and see that each of said books agree with

CHAPTER 774 .
AN ACT inrelationto Bath countysubscriptiontoKentucky Midland
Railway Company .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the Kentucky Midland Railway Company
is hereby authorized to apply to the court of claims
of the county of Bath for extensions of the times
within which a certaincontract of subscriptionfor
the capitalstock of said company , made and entered
intowhile itwas transactingbusinessunder the name
of the Paris, Georgetown and Frankfort Railroad
Company , shall be complied with by said railway
company ; and said court,when applied to,may grant

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 535
the extensions applied for, or such modified exten-
sionsas they may deem prudent and advisable: Pro-
vided, The said county court shall not extend the
time within which the railway company shall com-
plete the work in that county, or any part thereof,
later than the firstday of January , one thousand
eighthundred and ninety-three; but if the railway
company shall comply with the conditions of the
extensions of times, if any shallbe made , then, to
the extent it shall so comply , it shallbe entitledto
demand and receive the bonds of the county as though
ithad complied literallywith itsoriginalundertaking
as to time .
§ 2. Upon the applicationof the railway company
to the county judge of said county , it shall be his
duty to associatewith himself the justicesof such
county , on a day to be named by him , not laterthan
ten days after the application; and if, on the day
named , a majority of the justicesfailto attend, he
shall adjourn the court,from day to day ,tillthe at-
tendance of such majority can be secured. It shall
then be the duty of the court thus made up to con-
sider and act upon such propositionsfor extension
of time as the railway company may submit. The
names of the members of the court voting upon any
such proposition shall be entered upon the order-
book ; and no order of extension shall be valid or
effectual unless the record shall show that it has re-
ceived votes equal in number to a majority of the
court, includingthe county judgeand allthe justices
of the county in commission ; but the county judge
shall vote and be counted as a member of the court
for the purposes of thisact.
§ 3. This actshalltake effectat itspassage.
Approved April 15,1890.
