CHAPTER 784 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled"An act to amend and reduce into
one allactsincorporatingthe MasonicSavingsInstitution."
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section two of an act, entitled " An act to
amend and reduce into one all acts incorporating the
Masonic Savings Institution," approved February
twentieth, one thousand eight hundred and sixty-
four, be,and the same hereby is, amended as follows:
" That from and afterthe passage of this act there
shall be seven directors instead of five as heretofore ."
§2 . That afterthe passage of this act, and at the
next annual election, the directorsshall elect from
their number , in addition to the other officershere-
toforeauthorized, a vice-president, who shall,during
the absence or inabilityof the president, perform all
his duties .
§ 3. This act shall take effectand be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved April 16, 1890 .
CHAPTER 786 .
AN ACT to provide for removing obstructions in water -courses in
Marshall county.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That whenever as many as twenty or more
citizensof Marshall county , owning lands adjacent
to or in the bottoms or valleysof any of the water-
courses in said county, shall petition the county
judge of saidcounty for that purpose,it shall be the
duty of saidcountyjudge,and he ishereby author-
izedand empowered ,to lay offany such water-course
in said county into divisionsor districts, as public

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 565
highways are laid off; and such county judge shall
thereupon appoint overseers over such divisions or
districtsalong such stream or streams so laid off.
§2 . It shallbe the duty of such overseersto sum-
mon all persons who are required to work upon pub-
licroads in their respectivedivisionor district, own-
inglands thereinadjacentto or in the bottom of such
water-courses,who shall cleanout and keep clearall
obstructions in said water -course that will prevent the
freeflow of the water in the channel thereof, or that
will cause an overflow in said stream , under the same
provisionsof the law that public highways are now
kept in order .
§3 . That any person owning lands adjacent to or in
thebottoms of any such water-courses,and not living
on the same , afternotificationto said owner or agent
inwriting,shallfailor refuse to furnish his part of
the labor, the land or lands of such persons shallbe
liablefor the payment of his pro rataof the expenses
in clearing and cleaning out such streams ; and said
overseershallhave a lienupon saidland for the pay-
ment of such sum , which may be enforced by a suit
inequity.
§ 4. Any person failingto attend with proper tools,
or to labor when in attendance without reasonable
excuse after being summoned , as herein provided,
shall be fined not less than two and not more than
fivedollars for each day he shall so fail, to be re-
covered by warrant beforea justiceof the peace.
§5 . Said overseershall not summons the persons
herein required to labor, or compel them to furnish
labor, more than threetimes a year ; and shallnot re-
quire such persons to labor or work more than five
days at one time when so summoned .
§ 6. This act shall not be construed to interferewith
any mill-dams acrossany of said streams ; and noth-
inghereinshallbe construedsoas to preventthecon-
structionof water-gates across any of said streams :

566 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Provided , Such gates are so constructed and main-
tained as not to obstructthe freepassage of water in
said stream .
§ 7. This act to take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April 16,1890.
CHAPTER 787 .
AN ACT to provide for the appointment of a clerk for the jury com-
missionersof Jeffersoncounty .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the judges of the JeffersonCircuitCourt ,
the Jefferson Court of Common Pleas , and the Louis-
villeLaw and Equity Court,shallappoint some com-
petent clerkfor the jury commissioners of Jefferson
county .
§ 2. That when the assessorof saidcounty shall fur-
nish the names of persons qualifiedfor jury services,
as required by an act approved February twenty-
first, one thousand eighthundred and ninety, entitled
" An act to secure a more uniform and equal distribu-
tionof jury serviceamong the citizensof the county
of Jeffersonand the cityof Louisville," that it shall
be the duty of said clerk to transcribesaid names
onto slipsof paper, under the directionof the said
commissioners; and for his servicesin making out
said slips, and for such other clericalduties which
may be requiredof him by saidcommissioners, said
clerk shallreceivea reasonable compensation, to be
fixedby saidcourts,payable jointlyby the said coun-
ty and cityas other expenses are paid.
§3 . That this act shall take effectfrom and after
its passage .
Approved April 16, 1890 .

CHAPTER 793 .
AN ACT toamend an act,entitled"An actto incorporatethe town of
Rosine,in Ohio county, Kentucky ," approved March sixteenth, one
thousand eight hundred and seventy-eight.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. The board of trustees of said town shall have
authorityto build and erecta safe and commodious
prison-house or lock-up in said town , of such form
and dimension as they may deem advisable, which
shallbe placed in charge of the town marshal, or such
other person as they may select; which shall be used

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 577
for the confinement of all offenderswho may be
consigned theretoby the police judge, and for the
safe-keeping of allsuch persons as may be consigned
to work on the streetsand alleys of said town when
not at work .
§ 2. If any person,against whom a fineis assessed
in the policecourt of said town ,shallfailto pay the
same , togetherwith the costsof the proceedings, said
police judge shall have authorityto direct the con-
finement of such person in the prison-house or lock-
up until such fineand costsbe paid, at the rateof one
dollar per day foreach day's confinement; or he may
directthat such person be confinedin the jailof Ohio
county untilsuch finebe paid,as now prescribedby
law ; or he may directthat such person be placed at
labor on the streets and alleys of said town , under
the direction of the town marshal or road overseer,
untilsuch fineand costsbe paid, at the rate of one
dollarper day for each day's labor.
§3 . The policejudge of saidtown is hereby vested
with the authority and power to cause any person
guiltyof violatingthe ordinancesof said town , or dis-
turbing the peace, to be confined in the prison-house
or lock up untilsuch person be sober enough for his
trial .
§ 4. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 16, 1890.

592 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 801 .
AN ACT to repairand keep in repairthe public roads of Mercer county.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the county court of Mercer county , a ma-
jorityof the members thereof concurring therein,be,
and it is hereby , authorized to levy annually upon
the taxableproperty of saidcounty an ad valorem tax
not exceeding ten cents on each one hundred dollars
of taxable property in said county ,except property
assessedfortown or citypurposes,and a poll-tax not
exceeding two dollarsper capita, to raisea sum to
pay for the working of the county roads in Mercer
county, and to pay fiftycents per day to every hand
working the county roads of saidcounty .
§ 2. That the tax so leviedshallbe collectedby the
sheriffof said county at the same time he collects the
State revenue,and account for them in the same way ,
and shallbe subjectto the same liabilityand penalties
forcollectionof same that he isnow subjectto by law
for the collection of the State revenue ; and he shall
have power to distrainas he is authorized in collecting
the State revenue .
§ 3. It shallbe the duty of all surveyors of public
roads in the said county to submit to the court of
claims annually,at its regular meeting, a list, prop-
erly subscribed and sworn to, of the names of all the
hands who have worked upon their respectiveroads
during the twelve months next preceding said term
of court, and the number of hours of labor per-
formed by each ; and the said court may allow said
claims to be paid as other claims against the said
county are paid,at the rate of fiftycents per day of
eight hours each ; and any surveyor who shall make
an untrue reportof said work shallbe guilty of a
misdemeanor, and,on conviction, shallbe fined any

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 593
sum, in the discretionof the jury trying the case, not
exceeding one hundred dollars.
§4. The sheriffshallnot pay out any money forthe
benefitof any person who has a claim for work upon
said roads , as herein mentioned , until all taxes due
from said person for the year inwhich the labor was
performed, and the year next precedingsaid year,are
paid .
§5 . This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved April 16,1890.
CHAPTER 802 .

the town of Owenton , in Owen county ."

monwealth of Kentucky :

creating a new charter for the town of Owenton , in
Owen county ," approved March ninth, one thousand
eight hundred and eighty eight, be, and the same is
hereby , so amended as to provide that the regular
terms of the Owenton PoliceCourt shallbe held quar-
terlyon the firstMondays inMarch,June,September
and December of each year .
§2 . That the provisionsof sectionseventeenof said
act shall not apply to females,so faras they are now
requiredby saidact towork upon thestreetsor pub-
lic works of said town .
§3 . That said board of trusteesof the town of
Owenton shall have no power to pass any ordinance
imposing a finefor the saleof liquorsin saidtown,
the same being now already prohibitedby the general
law in force in said county .
§ 4. This actshalltake effectfrom itspassage.
38 - LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 2 .
Approved April 16, 1890.

CHAPTER 808 .

CHAPTER 813 .

of the Independence and ColemansvilleTurnpike Road

" ten," wherever the same occurs in said section, be
strickenout, and the word " twenty " be inserted in
place thereof.
§2 . That the justicesof the peace, when calledon ,
as provided in said section, shall have examined the
road, and found the same out of repair, shall give
notice, in writing, to the presidentof said road , or
any receiver,that said road isout of repair; the no-
ticeshallspecifythe particularpoints at which said
road isout of repair, and notifysaidpresidentor re-
ceiverthat unlesssaidplacesdesignatedin said notice
are repairedwithin twenty days from the execution
of the notice on either of them , that the gate or gates

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 615
nearest the point or pointswhere said road isout of
repairwillbe thrown open ,and no tollshallbe taken.
If,however ,said road shallbe repairedwithin twenty
days from the serviceof saidnotice,then saidjustices
shall again examine the road, at the request of the
president or receiver; and , if found to be in repair
at the pointsdesignated in said notice,then said jus-
ticesshallgive notice,in writing,that the proceedings
have been dismissed . But if said road has not been
repairedwithin twenty days from the date of the no
tice, then the gate or gates nearestthe point at which
said road isout of repairshall be thrown open , and
no toll collecteduntilsaid road shallbe repaired at
the points setforthin said notice: Provided ,however,
That the travel on said road is not obstructed on said
road at the pointsspecifiedin saidnotice.
§3 . Whereas , sectionsixteen of said charter pro-
vides that gates shall not be erectedon said road in
less than five miles of each other, nor until five
continuous miles shall have been completed ; and
whereas , said company have constructed about six-
teen miles of road ; that said section sixteen be so
amended as to authorize said company , or any re-
ceiver appointed for said road,by and with the con-
sent of the court appointing said receiver,the order
soappointingto be enteredof recordin thecourtso
appointing, that said receivermay erect a toll-house
and gate at any point beyond the firstfifteenmilesof
said road ,and collecttollfor every mile or fraction
thereofof travelon saidroåd,atthesame rateof toll
as is now allowed by said charter. The proceedings
forthe purchase of any land,or condemning the same
by writ of ad quod damnum ,and for the acquiringof
not more than one acre of ground to erect a toll-house
and gate thereon, shallbe the same as provided in the
charter of said company ; that so much of said sec-
tionssixteenand twenty as conflictshereinare hereby
repealed .
§ 4. This act to take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April16,1890.

CHAPTER 822 .
AN ACT to incorporateGraham Lodge number two hundred and eight,
of Ancient York Masons,locatedin Scottsville,Allen county.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That J. D. Kelley, S. H. Welch and J. W.
Ham , as trustees, and their successorsin office, be,
and they are hereby, constituteda body -politicand
corporate, under the name and styleof Graham
Lodge number two hundred and eight, of Ancient
York Masons , and that the officersand members of
saidlodge, and theirsuccessors, shallso continue and
have perpetual succession; and the name and style
aforesaidare made capable in law to contractand be
contracted with, sue and be sued,plead and be im-
pleaded,and answer and be answered,in allthe courts
of this State and elsewhere .
§ 2. The said lodge shall have the right, through
said trustees and their successors, to take and hold by
purchase, gift, deviseor bequest,realand personal
property, or either, and dispose of and convey the
same for the benefitof saidlodge : Provided ,however,
Said lodge or company shall not own any more real
estatethan is necessary for the conduct of the busi-
ness of said lodge or company within the scope of
itsobject. Any real estatetaken for debt shall not
be held longer than fiveyears.
§3 . Be it further enacted, That the individual
propertyof the members of said lodge shallnot be
liablein any way for the debts or liabilitiesof said
lodge.
§ 4. This act to take effectfrom and after its pas-
sage .
Approved April16,1890.

CHAPTER 824 .
AN ACT to incorporateMiles Lodge, number three hundred and
forty-one, Free and Accepted Masons .

monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That James W. Ridgway , Henry Samuels and
J. W. Croan , and their successorsin office, be,and
they are hereby,constituteda body -politicand cor-
porate, under the name and style of " Miles Lodge ,
number three hundred and forty-one , of Free and
Accepted Masons ;" and that the officersand mem-
bers of said lodge,and theirsuccessors,shallso con-
tinue and have perpetual succession; and by the
name and styleaforesaid they are hereby made ca-
pable in law and empowered to contractand be con-
tracted with, to sue and be sued, answer and be
answered, to plead and be impleaded , in all of the
courtsof law and equity in this State or elsewhere ;
to make ,have and use a common seal, and the same
to alteror change at pleasure .
§ 2. The said corporation shall have the right to
acquire, take and hold,by donation,gift,purchase or
devise, real or personal estate, to an amount not ex-
ceeding ten thousand dollars; to dispose of, lease,
sell and convey the same at pleasure. They may
borrow or loan money , and take notes or mortgages
to securethe payment thereof, or may execute mort-
gages on the corporateproperty.
§ 3. The businessand management of the affairsof
said corporation shall be under the control of the
three incorporatorsof the lodge named herein until
theirsuccessorsare electedas hereinprovided. Three
trusteesshallbe electedannually from the members
of said lodge, at the firststated meeting in the month
of June , one thousand eighthundred and ninety, and
each year thereafter,and whose duties shallbe such

ulations,as may be necessaryfor the safe-keeping of
the property and other interestsof the lodge, not
inconsistent with the Constitution and the laws of
theUnited Statesand the Stateof Kentucky .
§ 5. This actshallbe in forcefrom itspassage.
Approved April 16,1890 .

tax ."

tion to propound to each individual who offersto
vote, " Are you for or against the road tax ? " and his
vote shall be recorded according to his answer . If it
shall appear from a comparison of the polls that a
majority of the votes cast at said electionare for the
road tax, then the board to examine the polis shall
make a certificateof the fact, which shall be filed
with the county clerk, who shallrecord the same in
his office, and then this act shall be in full force and
effect .
§ 29. Notice of said electionshallbe by written or
printed noticesposted by the sheriffat not lessthan
three publicplacesin each districtin saidcounty , for
twenty days beforesuch election.
§ 30. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage ; and allacts or parts of actsin conflictwith
the provisionsof thisact are hereby repealed.
Approved April 16,1890.
CHAPTER 827 .
AN ACT relatingto water-works and electriclightsin the town of
Russellville,Kentucky .
WHEREAS ,It is a question of doubt whether the
board of mayor and councilmen of the town of Rus-
sellvillehave the authority, under itscharter,to im-
pose a tax on the people of saidtown for the purpose
of erectingwater-works and electriclights,or either,
therein; and whereas, said board, if they have the
power to impose a tax for such a purpose,are unwill-
ing to do so ; and whereas,it isthe desireof some of
the inhabitants of said town to have water -works and
electriclightstherein; therefore,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 683
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the mayor of the town of Russellville,a
majority of the members of the council concurring,
shall have the authority to order an electionto be
held in said town once every two yearsfor the purpose
of ascertaining thereby whether or not a majority
of the voters in said town are in favor of or opposed
to the erectionof water-works and electriclights, or
either of them , therein.
§2 . The board of mayor and councilmen, on the
occasion of said election, shall furnish a poll-book ,
ruled in fivecolumns ,headed respectively, " Name ,"
"For Water -Works ," " Against Water -Works ,"
" For ElectricLights," " Against ElectricLights," in
which shallbe recorded,in theirappropriatecolumns ,
the name and vote of each voter . Said election shall
be held in the usual way , aftergiving the notice re-
quired by law forhelding electionsin said town .
§ 3. If, at saidelection,a majority of the votes cast
shall be in favor of water-works and electriclights,or
either of them ,the mayor and councilmen shallissue
the bonds of the town forthe purpose of raisingmoney
with which to erect said water -works and electric
lights,or said water-works or electriclights,accord-
ing as the vote shallbe.
§ 4. The mayor and councilmen shallnot have the
power hereunder to issue more than thirty-fivethou-
sand dollars of bonds for the erection of water -works ,
nor more than eight thousand dollarsfor the erection
of electriclights.
§ 5. The bonds issued hereunder shall bear interest
at six per cent. per annum , payable semi-annually.
They shall mature in thirtyyears, but shall be re-
deemable at the willof the councilin eightyears.
§ 6. The councilshallhave the power to pledge the
revenue of the town for the payment of these bonds ;
and if they think itnecessary, may increasethe poll-
tax one dollar and fiftycents,and the property tax

684 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
twenty -fivecents on the one hundred dollars' worth
of taxable property beyond the amount now allowed
by law .
§7. No one, whose taxes are in arrears, shall be
allowed to vote at the afore -named election .
§ 8. All laws and partsof laws in conflictwith any
portion of thisact are hereby repealed.
§ 9. This act shallbe in force from itspassage .
Approved April 16, 1890 .
CHAPTER 828 .
AN ACT to provide for holding an electionto take the sense of the
votersof Canton precinct,in Trigg county ,as to the regulationof the
liquortrafficor itsprohibitiontherein.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . The judge of the county court of Trigg county
shall,upon the writtenpetitionof not lessthan twen-
ty legalvoters of Canton precinct, in Trigg county ,
order an electiontobe held thereinfor the purpose of
taking the sense of the legalvoters of said district
upon the propositionwhether they are in favor of the
regulationof the trafficin liquorthereinby licenseor
prohibition, said electionto be held at such time as
shallbe mentioned in and requested by said petition,
other than the day of any other election, and except
as modified by this act,shall,in all respectsand in
every manner ,be held under and in conformity to the
provisionsof " An act to regulate the saleof spirit-
uous , vinous or malt liquorsin thisCommonwealth ,"
approved May twenty -sixth, one thousand eight hun-
dred and seventy-four; and allduties required of the
county judge and other officersin electionsheld under
saidact shallbe performed by such officerswith ref-
erence to the electionprovided for hereby , except as
hereby modified.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 685
§2 . At said electionthe electionofficersshallpro-
pound to each voter presenting himself to vote, the
question, " Are you in favor of the prohibitionor
the regulation by licenseof the sale of spirituous,
vinous or malt liquorsin thisprecinct?" and his vote
shallbe entered for the prohibitionof said trafficor
foritsregulationby license,as thevoterdirects.
§ 3. If itshallbe found that a majority of the legal
votes castat said electionwere given for the prohibi-
tionof the saleof liquor in said district, the examin-
ingboardshallsocertifytothecountycourt,and it
shallbe unlawful to sellsuch liquorin said precinct;
but if it shallbe found that a majority of the legal
votes castat said electionwere given for the regula-
ion of the saleof such liquorby license, then the said
board shallso certifyto the county court,which certif-
icate shall be entered in the order -book of said court
at its next regular term , and it shall then be lawful to
sellspirituous, vinous and malt liquorsin said pre-
cinct under such regulation as to licenseas may be
provided by law .
§ 4. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage ; and
all acts and parts of actsin conflictwith the provis-
ions of this act are, so far as the same conflict here-
with , repealed.
Approved April16,1890.
CHAPTER 829 .
AN ACT to provide for a systemof seweragein the cityof Paducah.
Be it enacted by theGeneral Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the cityof Paducah shallhave power to
issuebonds not exceeding forty thousand dollars, and
said bonds shallnot be issuedfora longerperiod than
thirtyyears,and redeemable at such time as the coun-
cilmay designate; and the rateof interestnot to ex

686 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ceed fiveper cent.per annum ,to be paid semi -annu-
allyat the citytreasurer'sofficein said city; and the
council may releasesaid bonds of all city taxation ;
and said bonds shall be known as sewer bonds ; and
the taxableproperty of Paducah shall be responsible
forthe payment of saidbonds and interest.
§ 2. The proceeds of the bonds described in section
one shallbe used exclusivelyfor building the main
sewers,pumps and pumping station,or so much there-
of as may be necessaryto buildsaid main sewers,
pumps and pumping -houses, flush-tanks and man-
holes, as the intention is to build only the main
sewers by generaltaxation.
§ 3. The councilshallhave power to locateall sew-
ers (both main and lateral), pumps , pump -houses ,
flush -tanks and man -holes .
§ 4. The council shall have power to lay offthe city
in such sizesewer districtsas it may see proper for
the purpose of building lateralor small sewers to
lead into the main sewers . And these lateral sewers
and connectionsare to be paid for by a specialtax on
realestatein each district, and proportioned against
each realestateowner pro rata according to the num-
ber of square or superficialfeetowned by them in said
sewer district, and such special tax shall be a lien
on the real estateand property so assessed for the
amount so assessed for said sewerage .
§ 5. After the completion of a districtsewer, and
connection with the main established, the council
shall have the power to compel all owners of lots
with buildingsor tenement on same , within said dis-
trict, to connect their premises with the lateral
sewers; and shouldany owner of lotfailto comply
with the above requirement, then the cityshall have
power to have such connectionmade at the expense of
the property -owner .
§6. That itshallrequiretwo-thirdsof allthecoun-
cilmen the cityof Paducah isentitledto elect, evi-
denced by a callof the yeas and nays voting in the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 687
affirmative,to adopt any one or allof the provisions
named in the foregoingsections.
§ 7. Before the bonds named in section one of this
actcan be issued,the questionof " whether or not the
voterswish a system of sewerage,and bonds issued
to pay for same ," shallbe submitted to the qualified
voters of said city by the council,two thirds of all
the councilmen -electof saidcityvoting in the affirm-
ative, may submit said question at some regularcity
electionheld in said city; and if a majority of the
qualifiedvoters of said city, as shown by the assess-
or'sbooks for saidyear,vote for the sewerageand
bonds , and then the council, by a two-thirds vote of
allthe councilmen of said city, shall have the right
to order said bonds issued, and sewers constructed,
upon such terms and conditions as named in section
one of this act; and saidbonds shallbe issued with
coupons attached, the bonds to be signed by the
mayor of said city, and witnessed by the council
clerk of said city, and the council clerk shall sign
all the coupons attached to each bond . Should a
majority of such qualifiedvoters, voting at saidelec-
tion, vote against the sewerage and issuance of said
bonds , then the council shallhave the power to re-
submit the same question of " whether or not the
voters wish a system of sewerage, and bonds issued
to pay for same ," at any future electionafter one
year from the lastsubmission.
§ 8. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 16, 1890 .

688 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 830 .
AN ACT to amend "An act to create the office of commissioner of
bonds for the cityof Louisville," approved March twenty-sixth, one
thousand eight hundred and ninety.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That sectionthree of an act, entitled"An act
to create the office of commissioner of bonds for the
city of Louisville," approved March twenty -sixth ,
one thousand eight hundred and ninety, be amended
by adding to saidsectionthe followingwords , to wit :
"And of all partieswho shall be required to give
bond for their appearance before the said citycourt
of Louisville ."
§ 2. This act shall take effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 16, 1890.
CHAPTER 831 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Bank of Benton, Marshallcounty.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That there is hereby establisheda saving and
depositbank inthe town ofBenton,Marshallcounty ,
Kentucky, with a capitalstock of twenty-fivethou-
sand dollars,with the privilegeto the stockholders of
increasingthe same not to exceed one hundred thou-
sand dollars,in shares of one hundred dollars each ,
to be subscribedand paid for as hereinafterprovided
for; and the subscribers, their associates, successors
and assigns,shallbe a body-politicand corporate, by
the name and style of the " Bank of Benton ," and
shall so continue for thirtyyears from itsorganiza-
tion; and shallhave allthe rightsand privileges of
a naturalperson in contractingand beingcontracted

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 689
i
i
1
with, of suing and being sued,of impleading and be-
ing impleaded , of answering and defending, in all
courts and places whatever . Said bank may have
and use a common seal,and change or renew the same
at pleasure .
§2 . Said bank shall be under the control and man-
agement of seven directors, each of whom shall be a
stockholderin his own right; and afterthe firstelec-
tion, shall be electedannually on the firstMonday in
January in each year,and hold officeuntil theirsuc-
cessorsare electedand qualified. They shallelect
one of their number president, and appoint such
officers, agents and servants as they may deem nec-
essary to conduct the business of the bank ,and pay
them such sums as wages and salaries,and take from
them such bond to securethe faithfulperformance of
their duties,as they shall think proper and reasona-
ble; and they shall hold regular meetings at such
times as may be fixed by the by -laws ; and other
meetings may be had whenever deemed necessary;
and in case of death or resignationof any director,
or a vacancy declaredfor cause,such vacancy shall
be filledby the board of directors. The directors
shall have power to receivesubscriptionfor so many
of the shares of stock of saidcompany remaining un-
sold as the stockholdersmay , at any time,directto
sell; to declare dividends of the profitsarisingout
of the business. The stock shallbe deemed personal
property ,and shallbe transferableonly on the books
of the bank by the stockholders,his agent or attor-
ney, under such rulesand regulationsas the directors
from time to time shall establish; but the corpora-
tion shall have a lien on the stock to secureany in-
debtedness to the bank for the unpaid portionof the
stock subscribed for .
§ 3. That R. W. Starks,Junior,J. H. Goheen,W.
F. G. Collie,J. L. Holland ,A. J. Cox ,J.H. Johnston,
D. R. H. Truitt, P. W. Calvert,T. H. Hall, J. A.
44 - LOCAL LAWS , VOL .2 .

690 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Woodall , J. T. Irvin, R. M. Pace ,W. M. Reed ,J. W.
Dycus , J. D. Peterson, W. T. Carr,Solon L. Palmer ,
M. B. Pace , F. T. Foust, T. W. Holland, E. G.
Thomas ,D. A. Lovett,J. H. Ivey, Baxter Kuyken-
dall and W. S. Ivey, of Marshall county , and J. R.
Smith , of McCracken county, Kentucky , any three of
whom may act,are hereby appointed commissioners
to open books in the town of Benton , and receivesub-
scriptionsto the capitalstock of saidbank ;and when
shares to the amount of twelve thousand five hundred
dollars shall have been subscribed , it shall be their
duty to give notice to the subscribingstockholders,
and appoint a day for the electionof a board of di-
rectors,who shallhold their officeuntil the ensuing
annual election, and theirsuccessorsduly elected and
qualified. The payment of sharessubscribedshall be
as follows, namely : Five dollars on each share at
the time of subscribing, and twenty dollarswithin
ten days after the election of the firstboard of direct-
ors, and the residue in installmentsof twenty -five
dollarson each shareevery thirtydays : Provided,
That after twelve thousand and five hundred dollars
shall have been paid , the board of directors shall have
power to prolong the time for the payment of the
residue ; and should any of the subscribers fail to
pay for theirsubscriptionof stock as herein pro-
vided, the directorsmay , aftergiving due notice to
said delinquent stockholder, proceed to sue such de-
linquent stockholder in the name of the corporation
for the recovery of same ; or the directorsmay , in
their discretion, enter an order upon the books of
the company forfeitingthe unpaid stock, and recall
and cancel the subscriptiontherefor; and in such
case the stock shall be held and owned to the extent
and forthe amount paid thereon.
§ 4. Said bank may commence business as soon
as twelve thousand and five hundred dollars shall
have been paid in, and the presidentand directors
firstelected shall have made oath to the fact of such

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 691
1
payment before some officerauthorized to administer
oaths : Provided , Fifty per cent.of allthe stock sub-
scribed for shall be paid in before the bank com-
mences business: Provided further, The entirestock
subscribed for shall be paid in full within twelve
months after the date of the commencement of busi-
nessby the bank : And it is further provided, That
should the board of directorsat any time increasethe
capitalstock, as provided in sectionone, at leastfifty
percent.of said additionalstockshallbe paidinat
the time said subscriptionismade , and the remainder
within twelve months from that date : And it is fur-
therprovided , That in the event the stock isnot paid
in full at the expiration of twelve months from the
date of commencement of business by the bank , or
within twelve months from the date of subscriptionof
increasedstock,then the capitalof said bank shallbe
reduced to the amount of stock paid in full.
§5 . Said bank may receivedeposits of gold and
silver, bank notes and other noteswhich may be law-
fully circulatingas money , and repay the same in
such manner and at such times as may be agreed on
with the depositorsby specialor general contract;
may deal in the loaning of money and discounting
bills; may buy and sellbillsof exchange,promissory
notes, uncurrent money , stocks, bonds , mortgages,
gold and silvercoin, securitiesof any kind,and any
evidences of debt whatever ; take personal and other
securityfor the payment of same , and upon the non-
payment of the debt on demand , aftermaturity,
may sellsaidsecuritiesin accordancewith the pro-
visions of an act, entitled"An act for the benefitof
the incorporatedbanks of Kentucky," approved
March sixteenth, one thousand eight hundred and
seventy-one . All promissory notes made negotiable
and payable at saidbank,or any bank incorporated
under any law of the Commonwealth , or organized
in this Commonwealth under any law of the United
States,when discountedby thisbank are herebyput

692 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
upon the footing of foreign billsof exchange , and
like remedy may be had thereon, jointlyand sever-
ally, againstthe makers ,drawers, indorsersor parties
thereto .
§ 6. That said bank may receive deposits from
minors and married women ,and the receiptand ac-
quittancesof such minors and married women shall
be valid .
§ 7. That saidbank may acquire,hold, possess, use,
enjoy and occupy allsuch realestate,goods and chat-
tels, as may be necessary for the transactionof its
business, or which may be conveyed to it as surety
forany debt,or purchased in satisfactionof any debt ,
judgment or decree, and may buy itsown stock in
payment of a debt,judgment or decree, and selland
convey same ; but it shall not hold any real estate
purchased by itin satisfaction, in whole or in part, of
any debt,judgment or decree,for a longer periodthan
fiveyears. A conveyance, duly executed by the pres-
ident and board of directors,shall pass the titleof
saidcorporationto any realestateitmay want to dis-
pose of .
§ 8. That at all meetings of the stockholders, and
at allelectionsunder this charter, each share-holder
shall be entitledto one vote for every share held
in his own right. Any stockholder entitledto vote
may do so in person or by written proxy , such proxy
being granted to a stockholder.
§ 9. It shall not be lawful for said bank to issue
any note or billto circulateas money .
§ 10. The board of directors shall make all neces-
sary rules, regulationsand by -laws for the govern-
ment and management of the business of said bank ,
not inconsistent with the Constitution and laws of
this State or of the United States .
§ 11.The number of directorsand officersmay be
increased or diminished at the pleasure and by a
majority vote of the stockholders.
§ 12. The private property of the stockholders of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 693
said bank shall not be liable for the debts of the cor-
poration .
§ 13. The president and directorsof this bank are
authorized and empowered to wind up the affairsof
said bank whenever two -thirds of the stockholders
representedby stock shall determine that the com-
munity and the interestof the company do not
require its continuance.
§ 14. Every stockholder shallbe individuallyliable
to the creditors of said bank to the full amount of
stock subscribed by him and remaining unpaid ; but
when the same shallhave been paid for in fullby him ,
there shall be no further individualliabilityto the
stockholder .
§ 15. That thisact shalltake effectfrom itspassage,
and continue in forcethirtyyears.
Approved April 16,1890.
CHAPTER 832 .
AN ACT toincorporatethecityof Pineville, inBellcounty.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That so much of the county of Bell as is in-
cluded in the limits of the town of Pineville as now
fixed by law,and ,in additionthereto, commencing on
the north-west lineof saidlimitsat a point threehun-
dred yards north of the centerlineof the Louisville
and NashvilleRailroad Company's rightof way ;
thence west, and parallelwith said line, to the west
line of the land conveyed by Vincent Boreing to the
Bell County Coke and Improvement Company ; and
thence with said line to Cumberland river; and thence
east with the meanders of said river to the said west
line of the town of Pineville ; and also extending the
south line of saidlimitswhere it joinsthe land con

694 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
veyed by R. M. Moss,etcetera,tothe Pine Mountain
Ironand Coal Company ,so as to includesaid land, and
the persons residingwithin saidlimits,are hereby cre-
ated a body -politicand corporate, by the name " City
of Pineville;" and by that name shallhave perpetual
succession, with power to govern themselves by such
ordinances and regulationsand resolutionsas they
may deem proper, pursuant to the terms of this
charter : Provided , The same be not in conflict with
the Constitution or laws of this State or of the United
States; and be capable of suing and being sued ,
pleading and being impleaded , defending and being
defended , in all courts ; may contract and be con-
tracted with ; may acquire and hold property, for
municipal purposes, by dedication or condemnation
or otherwise; and may use, manage , improve , sell,
convey, rent or leasesuch property ; may have a com-
mon seal, and altersame at pleasure .
§ 2. And said city is invested with all the lands ,
tenements, and hereditaments, and all the goods,
chattels, moneys , revenues, rights and demands in
possession or expectancy of the town of Pineville,
and shallbe bound by allcontracts, and responsi-
ble for all debts, demands and liabilities, of what-
soever kind , against said town ; and it shall suc-
ceed to allthe rights, privilegesand franchisesof
said town ; and all privateactsheretoforegranted in
referenceto streetrailwaysin said town , or any fran-
chisetherein,shallbecome applicableto the said city
in the same manner and to the same extent as before
the adoption of this act.
§3 .Allstreets,alleys,roadsand lanesnow open to
the publictravel, and now establishedin said city,
are hereby declared to be public ways , and as such
shallbe under the controlof the city of Pineville.
ARTICLE II .
§1 . The corporatepowers of said cityof Pineville
shall be divided into three distinctand separate de

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 695
partments, viz: The legislative, executive and judi-
ciary.
ARTICLE III .
§ 1. The legislativepowers of the city of Pineville
shallbe vested in fivecouncilmen,to be styled The
Board of Councilmen of the City of Pineville, mem-
bers of which shallbe qualifiedvoters,owners of real
estatein said city,bona fide residentsthereof for at
leasttwelve months prior to theirelection, and shall
not be directlyor indirectlyinterestedin any place
where intoxicatingliquorof any kind is sold, nor be
directlyor indirectlyinterestedin his own behalf, or
inthe behalf of any other individual, in any contract
with said cityor any applicationtherefor, nor be in
arrears to said city for money collectedfor and with-
held from said city without settlement or quietus
therefor; and if, after his election, any member of
saidboard of councilmen shalldie or resign as coun-
cilman,or remove from the city,or engage in any bus-
iness, or do any act, the holding or doing of which
would have rendered him ineligibleas a member of
the board of councilmen, his seat, as such member ,
shall thereby be vacated , and the remaining members
of said board of councilmen shallso declare,and pro-
ceed immediately , or as soon thereafteras is practica-
ble, to fillthe vacancy .
§ 2. Stockholders in other corporationsmay be eli-
gible as a member of the board of councilmen ; but
shallnot vote or interfere, directlyor indirectly, with
any matter or questionaffectingany contractbetween
such company or corporationand the city of Pine-
ville .
§3 . The board of councilmen shall be electedby
the qualifiedvoters of said cityon the second Tues-
day in May ,one thousand eight hundred and ninety,
and biennially thereafter; and each member thereof
shall hold his officefor a term of two years .
§4 . The board of councilmen shall, as soon as prac-
ticable, electone of itsmembers mayor of the cityof

696 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
Pineville, who shall also be chairman of the board ;
and in his absence a mayor and chairman pro tempore
shallbe electedfrom the members by the members ;
but such person shallnot perform the functionsof the
officeof mayor ,except when the regular mayor has
been absent from Bell county forat leastthree days .
Three members shallbe a quorum of the board , with
power to act. In the absence of a quorum two mem-
bers shallhave power to adjourn from time to time
untila quorum issecured. The board shallhave power
tofillany vacancieswhich may occur in the board,and
upon a tievote, the person forwhom the mayor and
one councilman may vote shall be elected,and shall
hold officeuntil the next regular municipal elec-
tion. The board shall meet at such times and place
as they shall,by resolutionor ordinance,agree upon .
Itshalljudge of the eligibilityand electionreturns of
itsmembers , adopt rulesfor itsproceedings and gov-
ernment ,and enforce the same by appropriate fines,
not exceeding ten dollarsfor each offense; and four
members voting affirmativelymay , for cause, expel
any member .
§ 5. Before entering upon the execution of the
duties of their office, the several members of the
board of councilmen , and all other city officers, shall
take the following oath or affirmation: " I do sol-
emnly swear (or affirm)that I willfaithfullyand with-
out fear, favor or affection, discharge the duties of
the office of (as the officemay be) of the
city of Pinevilleaccording to law," which oath or
affirmation shall be administered by the clerk of the
Bell County Court, or any officerauthorizedto admin-
isteroaths, and a certificatethereof shallbe filedon
the journaland filedwith the recordsof the city.
§ 6. The board of councilmenshallelecta clerk,
who shallkeep a complete record of all itsproceed-
ings; and allrecords and officialproceedings of the
board of councilmen , and of the board of trustees of
the town of Pineville under former charters of the

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 697
1
town, are hereby declared " public records," and as
such shall be preserved and entitledto all the faith
and credit of " public records," and officialcopies
thereof may be read in allcourtswith like effectas
public records. Every ordinance passed, or resolu-
tionadopted ,shallbe published at leastonce in some
newspaper published in Pineville,or by printed hand-
billsposted innot lessthan threepublic placesin said
city; and it shall be the duty of the clerk of said
board to have such publication made as soon as
practicable, and to preserve a copy of said news-
paper or handbillin which published,and such pub-
licationshall be made before any ordinance is en-
forced ; and such copies of an ordinance or resolution
shall be prima facie evidence of its having been
published as requiredherein.
§ 7. Any member of saidboard of councilmen may
callfor the yeas and nays on any vote, which shall
be entered on the journalof proceedings.
Powers of the Board of Councilmen .
The board of councilmen, in additionto other pow-
ers hereinbeforegranted,shallhave power within the
city of Pineville, by ordinance:
First . To levy and collectfor municipal purposes
an annual ad valorem tax, not exceeding fiftycents
on every one hundred dollarsof real estateand per-
sonal property made taxable by law for State pur
poses, and not exceeding fiftycents on every one
hundred dollarsof such property to meet the princi-
pal and interest of any bonded debt hereinafter
authorized .
Second . To levy and collectannually upon every
male residentof said citytwenty -one years of age or
over, a head or personal tax of not exceeding two
dollars .
Third . To borrow money upon the credit of the
city,and issuecoupon bonds therefor,payment thereof
to be secured by a pledge or mortgage of the revenues

698 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of the city; such bonds to be payable in not exceed-
ing thirtyyears, and to bear interest,payable semi-
annually,at a rate not exceeding six per cent. per
annum ; said bonds and the interest thereon may be
made payable at such place or placesas saidboard of
councilmen may designate in the ordinance authoriz
ing the issue of such bonds . But the debt so created
shallnot exceed fiftythousand dollarsat any time.
Fourth . To appropriate money and provide for the
payment of the debts and the expenses of the city.
Fifth . To make regulationsto prevent the intro-
duction or spreading of contagiousor infectiousdis-
eases in the city; to pass quarantine laws for that
purpose , and to enforce the same within one mile of
the boundary of the city; to establishand regulate
hospitalsor pest-houses in or outsideof the city,by
contract ; to make all regulations necessary to secure
the general health of the inhabitantsof the city, and
to regulateand provide forthe burialof the dead .
Sixth. To prevent,abate and remove nuisances at
the expense of the owner of or occupant of the prem-
iseswhere such nuisance exists; to define and declare ,
by ordinance, what shall be a nuisance within the
limitsof the city, and to punish by fine any person
for causing or permitting a nuisance ; such fine shall
not exceed twenty-fivedollarsfor each day such nui-
sance is continued after notice, and shall be recovera-
ble in the Pineville City Court .
Seventh. To prohibitthe erectionof wooden build-
ings, and regulatethe height of same in any part
of said city; to have repaired, removed or to change
any chimneys,fluesorstove-pipeswhich,intheirjudg-
ment ,threatensthe securityof other property. The
power of the board of councilmen in regard to the
erectionof wooden buildingsand the height thereof
in saidcity,may be enforcedby such fineor penalty
as may be prescribedby ordinance of said city; any
repairs, removal or change in any chimneys , flues,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 699
stove-pipes or fire-places,shall be at the cost of the
property -owner .
Eighth . To make all necessarycontractswith any
person or corporationto provide the citywith water ,
and, for such purpose, to erect hydrants, cisterns,
fire-plugs and pumps in the streetswithin or beyond
the limits of said city.
Ninth . To contractforand maintain sewers,bridges
and culverts, and regulate the use of the same ; to
clean and keep in repair streets, alleys,public ways
and public grounds ,and to drain the streetsand pub-
licplaces; and to condemn land for draining and
sewerage purposes, in the same manner and under the
same proceedings as required for condemning lands
forthe opening or extending of streets.
Tenth . To provide, by ordinance or contract, for
the lightingof streets, market -houses and other pub-
licbuildings, rooms and offices, with gas or other
means or agencies .
Eleventh . To erect market -houses, and to provide
for the government and management thereof.
Twelfth . To provide for public buildings for the
city, and inclosingand improving allpublic grounds ;
to license, tax and regulate auctioneers, merchants,
peddlers, brokers, pawnbrokers, tobacco factories,
public halls, lectures, concerts, and other places of
amusement ; wagons , drays, hacks, and all kinds
of vehicles, and livery stables, pork -houses, ex-
change offices, telegraph offices, insurance offices,
or the agents keeping the same ; express officesor
agents for same ; real estateagents, hotels, tav-
erns, restaurants, publicboarding-houses, entertain-
ing transientguests.
Thirteenth. To license, tax, regulate, suppress or
prohibitlectures,theatricalor otherexhibitions,cir-
cuses,shows,billiard-tablesand pooltables,shooting-
galleries, skating-rinks, ten-pin alleysand bowling-
saloons, and other amusements .
Fourteenth . To pass ordinancesinflictingfines, and

700 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
provide forthe enforcementfor same, fordrunken-
ness, disorderlyconduct, for keeping or permitting
gaming -houses or disorderlyhouses of any kind .
Fifteenth. The board of councilmen shall provide
by ordinance the amount of tax for any license au-
thorizedby thischarter and not herein fixed,at any
sum they may deem proper in their discretion, not
exceeding two hundred and fiftydollars; and said
board of councilmen shall have power and authority
to prescribeby ordinance such penaltiesas they may
deem proper, in their discretion, against those who
may failto obtain a licensein any case wherein a
licenseis required by this charter, not exceeding a
fine of five hundred dollars .
Sixteenth. The board of councilmen shallhave pow-
er toprovide forthe preventionand extinguishment of
fires,and to regulateand establishfirecompanies ; to
provide foror electfiremen, prescribetheirduties and
fix their compensation ; and the volunteer members
of such companies, who receiveno pay ,shall be ex-
empt from serving on juriesin municipal courts, and
from paying polltax, so long as they remain active
members of such companies ; also to regulate and
provide by ordinance for the sweeping and cleaning
of chimneys ; to regulateand restrainthe erectionof
wooden buildings,and to provide for the removal of
same when built contrary to the ordinances of the
city; to provide forthe condemnation and removal of
unsafe walls,and to requirethat allnew buildings, as
to theirsafety,be subjectto the approval of the city
engineer .
Seventeenth. To regulatethe storageof gunpowder ,
rosin, tar,pitch, cotton, oil, and all other explosive
and combustible material,and to appoint some suit-
able person or persons, at seasonable times,to enter
and examine such houses as they may designate, in
order to ascertainwhether any of such houses are in
dangerous condition with reference to fires, and to
cause such as are in dangerous condition to be im-

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 701
i
mediately put in a safe order or conditionat the cost
and expense of the occupants or owners thereof.
Eighteenth . To erect and keep in repair accurate
public scales, and to appoint a public weigher or
weighers to attend to the same ,and fix the fees and
compensation for his or their services; to establish
standard weights and measures ; to regulate the
weights and measures to be used in the city in all
caseswhere the same isnot provided by law.
Nineteenth. To provide for the measurement of
lumber and all other building material .
Twentieth . To provide forthe inspection, weighing
and measuring of hay ,stone-coal,charcoal, fire-wood ,
beef, pork , bacon , meal, flour, whisky in barrels,
hogsheads or other vessels, and coal-oil: Provided ,
however , Nothing in thisact shallbe so construed as
to authorizethe inspectionor weighing of any of the
articlesenumerated which are to be shipped beyond
the limits of the city, except at the request of the
owners thereof, or the persons having charge of the
same .
Troenty-first. To pass ordinances requiringallcoal
and wood ,when sold and deliveredin the city, to be
weighed or measured by the public weigher, and to
fix adequate penaltiesfor the violationof same ; to
requireallilluminatingoils,beforebeingsoldin the
city, to be inspectedby thepublicinspector.
Twenty -second. To control,regulateand determine
the location of butcher-shops, slaughter-houses and
glue factories.
Thoenty-third. To provide for and regulatethe in-
spectionof butter,lardand otherprovisions,and the
vending of meat and all other articlesof food for
consumption withinthecitylimits.
Twenty -fourth. To erect a work -house, poor-house,
station-house and house of correction, or any or
either of them , and to provide for the maintenance,
government and regulationthereof, and of the per
1

702 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
sons confined therein, and to provide, maintain and
regulatea public burying-ground .
Twenty fifth. To exact of and compel persons ar-
rested and held under a capias pro fine from the
PinevilleCity Court,and persons finedby said court,
if the fineand costs of prosecution are not paid at
once, to perform such reasonablework as may not be
detrimental to health, either in any work -house or
on the streetsof said city, or in preparing curbing,
rock or other material for the same , or to do any
other useful labor on public property ; to regulate
such labor, and fix the wages to be allowed therefor
inextinguishment of finesand costs.
Twenty -sixth. To provide for the appointment of
all officers, agents and servants of said city, not
otherwise provided for herein,and fixthe compensa-
tion for their services; to fix the fees of witnesses in
municipal courts and others for services rendered
under thisact, or under any ordinance of said city.
Twenty -seventh. To fix, by ordinance, the penalty
for the violationof any provisionof thischarter, and
enforce the same .
Twenty -eighth. The board of councilmen may grant
the right of way over the public streetsor the other
publicgrounds of saidcity, to any railroadcompany
or streetrailroadcompany forsuch time and in such
manner and upon such conditionsas to them may
seem proper and expedient for the good of the city ;
and shallhave a supervisingcontrolover the same ,
and regulatethe speed with which cars shall be run ,
and signals. And ,under like conditions, may grant
the right of way that may be necessary for the
properoperationof gas companies,water companies,
electriclight companies, telephone companies , tele-
graph companies,or any similarcompanies .
Twenty -ninth. The board of councilmen shallhave
the power to define,by ordinance, what shall consti-
tutevagrancy,and the punishment thereof.
§ 8. The board of councilmen,aside from the bond

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 703
indebtedness hereinbefore authorized , shall have no
power to incur any indebtedness on behalf of the
citywhich can not be met out of the revenue of the
cityfor the currentfiscalyear ; and in the event that
said city shall be held liablefor any such indebt-
edness by a court of lastresort,the members of the
board of councilmen voting to create or authorize
such indebtedness , their heirs, executors and admin-
istrators,shallbe liablethereforto said city.
§ 9. The board of councilmen shallhave power ,by
ordinance, to prescribethe punishment by finenot ex-
ceedingone thousanddollars,or imprisonmentin the
county jailor the work -house not exceeding one year,
of any person who shall molest, damage or interfere
with any system of water-works laid in said city, or
the pipe , mains , hydrants or any part thereof; and
may ,subjecttothe rulesofany watercompany which
may establishsuch system, selectwhat persons shall
have the right to open ,tap or make connectionwith
such pipe or mains in the streets, alleysand public
ways of said city.
Police Force .
§1 . The board of councilmen shall have power to
elect a policeforce; the number ,grades and allreg-
ulations thereof to be as provided by ordinance from
time to time,whose term of officeshall expire two
years from the date of election.
§ 2. No person shallbe eligibleas a policeman who
is not at the time of his appointment a citizenof the
United States, a residentof Pinevillefor at leastsix
months next preceding his appointment , and at least
twenty -four years of age,or who is not moral, sober
and sagacious, or who has been convictedof a felony,
or who can not read and write the English language
intelligibly. No policeman shall,afterhiselection,or
while a member of the policeforce, interferein any
election further than to vote; a violationof thispro-
vision shall be a sufficient reason for his removal from
the force .

704 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 3. Every policeman shallbe commissioned by the
mayor ;but beforereceivinghiscommission shalltake
an oath faithfullyto perform the duties of his office,
and that he possessesthe severalqualificationsre-
quired in this charter,and that he willnot, while he
remains a policeman, interferein any electionfurther
than to vote .
§ 4. The chief of policeand every policeman shall
have power to execute warrants of arrest, process,
subpœnas and attachments forwitnesses, whether the
same be directedto them or not. They ,and each of
them , shall have power of arrest, with or without
warrants, for any offense against the ordinances or
municipal regulationsof Pineville, and the same
power of arrestforany offenseagainstthe laws of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky that the sheriffhas ; for
any servicesunder thissectionunder the laws of the
Statethey shallbe entitledto the feesallowed by law
to the sheriffsand other officersfor such services;and
shall be entitled to the same remedies for the collec-
tion thereof .
§ 5. The policejurisdictionof the cityof Pineville
shallextend to any point in Bell county within two
miles of the boundary of said city.
§ 6. Every policeman, before he is commissioned ,
shall give bond , with surety,before the mayor , and
to be approved by the mayor of the cityof Pineville,
in the sum of fivehundred dollars, for the faithful
performance of the duties of his office; and for any
unlawful arrest,and for any cruel beating or assault
inmaking an arrest,he and his surety shallbe liable
to the person so injuredon saidbond .
§ 7. Persons arrestedfor any bailableoffenseshall
be placed in the station-house or jailforsafe-keeping
untiltaken before the citycourt fortrial: Provided ,
however, That the judge of the city court may , at
chambers , releasesaidperson upon bail.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 705
