ARTICLE IV .
§1 . The mayor of Pinevilleshallhold his officefor
a term of two years,and untilhis successoriselected
and qualified. He shall be the chief executive officer
of the cityof Pineville, and the presiding officerof
the board of councilmen, decide all points of order ;
and shallhave the power ,during the sessionof said
board of councilmen , to enforce good order and de-
corum , and to punish contempt by finein any sum
from one to five dollars .
§ 2. He shall perform and dischargesuch dutiesas
may be prescribed from time to time by ordinance,
not inconsistentwith thischarter; he may callspecial
meetings of the board of councilmen whenever , in his
judgment , it may be necessary or to the interestof
the city ; he may require information in writing from
any or allof the officersof the cityupon any subject
relating to the duties of their respective office; it
shallbe his duty to see that the laws and ordinances
of the city,and that contractsmade with the city, are
duly enforced, observed and faithfullyexecuted.
§3 . The mayor shallbe the head of the police of
the city, and may command them in the performance
of theirduties; and shallhave fullpower to suspend
or dismiss any policeman from the force for any cause
he may deem sufficient; he shallbe a conservatorof
the public peace ; he shall, when deemed necessary
by him to enforcethe laws of the city, to save lifeor
property, or to quell riotsor mobs , have power to
summon into serviceany of the citizensof said city,
either civilor military ; and in such cases he must be
present and command in person ; and the board of
councilmen shallby ordinance prescribesuitablefines
and penaltiesfor disobedienceof the mayor's orders
and summons .
§ 4. It shallbe the duty of the mayor to sign the
proceedings of every meeting of the board of council-
men when the same shallhave been writtenup by the
45 - LOCAL LAWS , VOL . 2 .

706 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
clerkin a journal to be kept for such purpose . He
shall sign all ordinances and resolutionspassed by
the board of councilmen. He shall give the casting
vote whenever the board of councilmen are equally
divided on any question,ordinance or resolution. He
shall have power to administer oaths .
§ 5. In case of the death , resignation, removal from
office, inabilityor refusalto act of the mayor , the
members of the board of councilmen shall elect one
of theirmembers mayor pro tempore, who shall exer-
cise the powers and discharge the duties of mayor
untilthedisabilitiesbe removed,or,incaseof a per-
manent vacancy, until the next regular municipal
election .
§ 6. The compensation of the mayor shall be fixed
by the board of councilmen,but he shallnot preside
while his salaryis under considerationor being fixed
by the board of councilmen .
ARTICLE V.
Chief of Police .
§1 . The board of councilmen shall, as soon as it is
organizedunder thischarter, at itsfirstmeeting, or as
soon thereafteras may be, and bienniallythereafter,
electa chief of police, who shallhold his officefor
a term of two years, subject to the power of re-
moval for good cause (which shallbe judged of by
the board of councilmen exclusively), at any time by
the board of councilmen .
§ 2. The chiefof police, before entering upon the
officialduties of his office, shall take the oath of office
hereinbeforeprescribed, and execute bond , payable
to the city of Pineville, with surety, in a sum not
lessthan one thousand dollars, the same to be ap-
proved by the board of councilmen,conditioned that
he willwell and faithfullyperform allthe duties of
his office, and pay over and account for all money
collectedby him upon capias pro fines,or otherwise

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 707
onpenalproceedingsas chiefof police, to the city
treasurer,or such persons as may be entitledthereto,
withinten days after the same has been collected;
andupon payment to the citytreasurer, he shall take
hisreceiptfor the same and reportsaid collectionto
theboard of councilmen at their next ensuing meet-
ing;and in matters relatingto his officehe shallbe
governedby the laws and ordinancesof said city,the
resolutionsof saidboard of councilmen, and the pro-
visionsof this charter .
§3. The chiefof policeshallbe second in command
tothemayor of the policeforceof the city. He shall
attendall sessions of the board of councilmen ; exe-
cutethe orders thereof, and preserve order thereat.
He shallattend the sessionsof the citycourt of Pine-
ville,and act as the marshal of saidcourt. It shall
behisduty to execute alltheprocesses,ordersand
judgments of said court,and he shallbe entitledto
thesame fees forlike services, and to the same reme-
diesforcollectinghis feesthat a sheriffisentitledto.
Itshallbe lawful for the board of councilmen to pay
saidchiefof policea salary, in additionto the feesof
hisoffice,of not exceedingfivehundred dollarsper
annum, the same to be paid monthly out of the city
treasury,and to terminatewhen he ceasesto hold
office.
§4. Said chief of policemay , by and with the
adviceand consent of the board of councilmen,
appointa deputy , who shallhave the same powers ,
and perform all the duties required of the chief of
police,except that he shallnot have any command
overthe police force of the city, except during the
absencefrom the cityor disabilityof the chiefof
police: Provided, however, That saiddeputy shall,
beforeenteringupon the dutiesof saidoffice, take
theoath of officehereinbeforeprescribedfor police-
men, and execute a bond , payable to the cityof Pine-
ville, with surety,in a sum of not lessthan one
thousanddollars, the same to be approved by the

708 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
board of councilmen , conditioned in like manner as
that of the chief of police.
§ 5. Itshallbe the duty of the chiefof police,either
in person or by deputy , to see that the cityis propр-
erly patroled at all hours,and to prevent,as far as
possible, all crimes and offenses, and to arrest all
criminalsand offenders; and he may have the power
of the city to assisthim in the performance of his
duties, and may orallysummon as many persons as
he may deem necessaryto aid in making arrests; and
all persons failingto obey his summons , without a
reasonableexcuse, shallbe guiltyof a misdemeanor ,
and be punished by a finenot exceeding fiftydollars,
or imprisoned not exceeding thirtydays ,or both so
finedand imprisoned, as the citycourt may adjudge .
§ 6. It shallbe the duty of the chiefof policeor his
deputy, iftherebe one, to cause proceedingsto be
institutedagainst any person carrying on any busi-
ness, or doing any act for which a licenseisrequired ,
without paying such license: Provided ,That no per-
son permanently residingor doing business in Pine-
ville shall be convicted of such offense without at least
threedays ' notice given in writing by said chief of
police,by himself or his deputy , if there be one , to
pay said license.
ARTICLE VI .
Concerning the City Attorney.
§ 1. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof city
attorneyunlesshe be at leasttwenty-four years of age ,
and shallhave been a practicinglawyer for atleasttwo
years,and shallhave been a residentof Pinevillefor at
least six months before his election. Before entering
upon the duties of his office,he shall take the oath
required by a Commonwealth attorney, modified so
faras may be necessaryto conform to his office.
§ 2. The board of councilmen,as soon as they are
organizedunder thischarter,or as soon thereafteras
practicable, and bienniallythereafter, shall elect a
cityattorney, who shallhold his officefor a term of

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . $709
i
two years,and untilhissuccessoriselectedand quali-
fied .
§3 . It shallbe the duty of the cityattorneyto give
legaladvice to the board of councilmen when called
upon , and to be present at the meeting of said board
of councilmen ifrequested; and when required, shall
furnishwrittenopinions upon the subjectssubmitted
to him by the board of councilmen ; to prosecute all
persons in the citycourt charged with the violationof
the criminal or penal laws or ordinancesof the cityof
Pinevilleor the Stateof Kentucky ; to institutepro-
ceedings for the enforcement of the forfeituresof re-
cognizanceand bail bonds in saidcitycourt, and the
enforcement of alljudgments againstoffenders. The
cityattorney shallhave,for hisservices,a taxed feein
each case where there is a judgment againstthe de-
fendant,of one dollar,and thirtyper cent.of such fines
and forfeituresas by law go to and are for the benefit
of said cityof Pineville; said feeof one dollar to be
taxed as cost and paid by the defendant : Provided ,
That if such attorney'sper cent.of such fineand for-
feituresamounts to fivedollars, then said attorney's
fee of one dollar shall not be taxed .
§ 4. In all civilactionsin which the cityis either
plaintiffor defendant, it shall be the duty of said
cityattorneytoappearon behalfof thecityand pros-
ecute or defend said suitor suits, as directedby the
board of councilmen .
§ 5. The board of councilmen may pay the city
attorney a salarynot to exceed threehundred dollars
per annum , in additionto the feesof his office, the
same to be paid in monthly installmentsout of the
city treasury .
ARTICLE VII .
Judicial Department .
§ 1. The judicialpower of thiscorporationshallbe
vested in and exercisedby a court,to be styledthe
Pineville City Court,the judge thereof to be elected
by the qualifiedvotersof the cityof Pinevilleon the

710 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
second Tuesday in May , one thousand eight hundred
and ninety,and every two years thereafter; and such
judge shall hold his officefor two years, and until
his successoriselectedand qualified: Provided , The
present judge of the police court of the town of
Pinevilleshall be the judge of said court until the
firstregularmunicipal election, as provided herein.
§ 2. No person shall be eligibleto the officeof said
city court unless he be a male citizenof said city,
at least twenty -five years of age, and has resided
within the limitsof the said cityat leastsix months
next precedinghis election.
§3 . When , from any cause,the judge of the city
court failsto attend, or, if in attendance, can not
properly presidein any action or specialproceeding
or prosecution pending in said court, or if either
party shallfilehis affidavitthat said judge will not
give him a fairand impartialtrial, then the attorneys
of the courtwho are presentshallelectone of the mem-
bers of the bar then in attendance,who shallpossess
the same qualificationsprescribedby this charter for
the regularjudge, to hold the court for the occasion;
and he shallaccordinglypresideand adjudicate,hav-
ing firsttaken the oath as prescribedfor the regular
judge by this charter. The electionshallbe held by
the clerk(if therebe one) of said court,and in case
of a tie,he shallgive the castingvote; and if there
be no clerk of said court, then such election shall be
held by the cityattorney; and if he be not present,
then by any member of the bar of that court whom
the members of the bar present may select. The per-
son thus selected,during the period that he acts,shall
have allthe power and be liableto allthe responsi-
bilitiesof the regularjudge of saidcourt.
§ 4. Whenever a vacancy shall occur in the officeof
judge of the PinevilleCity Court itshall be filledby
appointment by the board of councilmen until the
next regular electionfor said office,or until a suc-
cessoriselectedand qualified. Before a judge (other

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 711
than a special judge elected as hereinbefore pro-
vided) of said court shallenterupon the dischargeof
his officialduties,he shallreceivefrom the Governor
of Kentucky a commission as such, which shall be
granted to him upon his furnishing a certificateof
his election, or a certificateof his appointment from
the city clerk, whereupon he shalltake the same oath
of officethat circuitjudges are required by law to
take .
§ 5. The judge of the PinevilleCity Court shallbe
a conservator of the peace, and may issuewarrants
of arrestforalloffensesagainstthe laws of the State
or ordinances of the city, when from his own per-
sonal knowledge , or from information from the offi-
cers of the city, or from the oath of any person,he
shall be satisfiedthat there are reasonable grounds
to believe that an offense has been committed ;
and for those offensescommitted in his presence
may order an arrestwithout a warrant. He shall
have power to summarily fineand imprison for con-
tempt to his court: Provided , The finedoes not ex-
ceed ten dollars, nor the imprisonment twenty -four
hours . He shall have power to order the chief of
police to summon a jury in any case cognizable
before him , when the jury would be required be-
fore a circuitcourt or a justiceof the peace,and
to issue subpœnas for witnesses in any case pend-
ing before him ; and,upon theirfailureto obey the
summons ,he may award compulsory processto com-
pel theirattendance. Alltrialsheld and had before
the judge of said court shallbe within the limitsof
the city of Pineville, in a building or room suitable
therefor, to be provided by the board of councilmen at
the expense of said city.
§6 The said judge shallhave power and authority
to administer oaths,grant injunctionsand issue at-
tachments under the same rulesand regulations, and
in the same cases, that county judges are now and
may hereafterbe authorizedby law. Saidjudgeshall

712 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
be clerk of his own court,but may appoint a deputy
clerk, who , afterhe shallhave taken the same oath of
officeas requiredby law of a deputy clerkof a court,
may perform allof the dutiesof clerk of said court ;
the judge of said court, as clerk thereof, being re-
sponsibleforthe actsof his deputy clerk. He shall
keep a docket, order-book and execution-book , and
such other books as may be necessary(which books
shall be furnished by the board of councilmen at the
expense of said city), and shall always keep them
open for the inspection of the board of councilmen
and othercityofficers, or any other person or persons
who may be interestedin the same or entriestherein.
Such books shall be public records.
§ 7. The Pineville City Court shall have exclusive
originaljurisdictionin allproceedingsor prosecutions
for the violationof ordinances of the city, and con-
current jurisdictionwith the Bell CircuitCourt , but
exclusiveof justicesof the peace and of the Bell
county judge,of allviolationsof the penal laws of this
Commonwealth arisingwithin the limitsof the city of
Pineville,except casesof felony; and said court shall
have power and authority to take recognizancesand
bail bonds from persons charged with offensescogni-
zablebefore said court, for appearance in said court,
and take recognizancesand bail bonds for good be-
havior and to keep the peace ; and upon a violation
of any of the conditions of such recognizances or
bailbonds,saidcourt shallhave power to adjudge the
same as forfeited,pronouncejudgment thereon, and
enforce fullcompliance therewith; said court shall
have the power of a justiceof the peace and of a
county judge combined , as an examining court, and
shall have originalconcurrent jurisdictionwithout
the limitsof the city of Pinevilleand co-extensive
with thelimitsofBellcounty,withinthejurisdiction
of justicesof the peace, and shall have power and
authorityto take recognizancesand bail bonds , in
casesof felonyand misdemeanor, for appearance in

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 713
!
the Bell CircuitCourt . Appeals can be taken from
the judgment of said court to the Bell CircuitCourt
inallcases of the violationof the penal laws of this
Commonwealth , where the fine exceeds twenty-five
dollars, or the judgment of imprisonment is for ex-
ceeding twenty -fivedays .
§ 8. The PinevilleCity Court shall have original
jurisdiction,co-extensivewith Bell county,in allcivil
actionsin which the amount in controversy, exclusive
of interests and costs, does not exceed two hundred
dollars, which jurisdictionshall be concurrent with
that of any other court in Bell county having juris-
diction within the limits before named ; but the said
court shallhave no jurisdictionof casesinvolvingthe
title of real estate or the enforcement of liens there-
on ; and in all actions begun in said court,if itap-
pearsthatan adjudicationupon the questionof title
to real estateor the enforcement of a lien upon real
estate is necessary to a final settlement of a contro-
versy, said court shall at once make an order trans-
ferringthe actionto the Bell Common Pleas Court,or
such court as may have at the time likeciviljuris-
diction, and shall forthwith deliver or cause to be de-
liveredthe originalpapers,and certifiedcopiesof all
orders made in the action, to the clerk of the court to
which such action may be transferred, who shall
docket the same , and the proceedings shall be had
thereon as though the action had originallybeen
brought in that court.
§ 9. In allcivilactionsan appeal shalllieto the
Bell Common Pleas Court, or any court having like
civiljurisdiction,where the amount involvedexceeds
or isequal to the amount which authorizesan appeal
to a circuitcourt from a court of inferiorjurisdiction,
and under the rulesand regulationsprescribedby law
for such appeal .
§ 10. The PinevilleCity Court shall be a court of
record; and have a seal,which shallbe provided by
the board of councilmen,and kept in the custody of

714 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
the judge of said court or his deputy clerk (if there
be such deputy clerk), if he shall so direct; and
copiesof the recordsof said court,certifiedover the
signatureof the judge,clerk or deputy clerk of said
court,and under the seal of said court,shall be evi-
dence in any court of thisCommonwealth .
§ 11. The PinevilleCity Court, so far as it is a po-
licecourt or courtof inquiry,shallalways be open for
the hearing and the determining of such cases as are
within itsjurisdiction; and for the trialof civilcases
thereshallbe a monthly term,to be held on the third
Monday in each month , and continuing as many days
as the businessmay require.
§12. The said court shall, by rule, fix a monthly
return day of executions, and shall have power to
prescriberulesgoverning itsproceedings, and forthe
speedy and convenient administrationof justice; and
shall collecttax on suits, where the amount in con-
troversyin civilcases is fiftydollarsor over, under
the same conditionsand regulationsas are required
of circuit court clerks .
§ 13. The proceedingsin saidcourt shall be in the
name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , when the
offense charged is a violation of the laws of the State ,
and in the name of the city of Pineville, when the
offense charged is a violation of an ordinance of the
city. The process shallbe in the name of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky ;shallbe made returnable to
the PinevilleCity Court ; shall be directed to and
executed by the chief of police, unless, for special
reason, the judge of said court shall cause it to be
directed to another , when it shall be directed to
any sheriff, constable or policeman The process
may go to any county in the Commonwealth , and
the said chief of police may execute it anywhere
in the State, or he may ,by indorsement on the pro-
cess,appoint a specialbailiffto execute it, who shall
have allthe right to execute it, and be entitled to all
the feesthereforthat the chief of policehas by law.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 715
The returnon such processby a specialbailiffshall
be made under oath .
§ 11. The judge of the PinevilleCity Court shallbe
entitledto a feeof one dollarfor a peace warrant, or
for a warrant for any misdemeanor or breach of the
peace,or fora breach of any ofthe laws and ordinances
of the cityof Pineville; he shallbe entitledto a fee
of twenty-five cents for each originaljudgment ;
twenty -five cents for recording the same ; twenty -five
cents for each subpœna ; twenty -fivecentsfor a cer-
tificate, and forissuingeach execution or capias pro
fine, twenty -five cents. He shall be entitledto the
same feesin allother cases as are allowed justicesof
the peace by law for likeservices; and his feesforall
servicesnot herein provided for, in any case where
the jurisdictionis concurrent with the circuitcourt,
shall be the same as allowed by law to circuit
clerks. The board of councilmen shall have power
by ordinance to pay said judge a salaryin addition
to the fees of his office, not to exceed five hundred
dollars per annum .
§ 15. Itshallbe the duty of saidjudge, on the first
judicialday of each month , to furnish the citytreas-
urer a statement, under oath, of allfines and for-
feiturespaid to or collectedby the chief of police
during the month preceding, as may appear from the
records of his court,and no salaryshall be received
by said judge untilsuch reportismade .
§ 16. The fines and forfeitures recovered in said
court shall be collectedby the chief of police or
other officer to whom execution of fieri facias or
capias pro fineisdirected; and such finesand forfeit-
ures as, by law, are for the benefitof the city of
Pineville,shallbe paid by him to the citytreasurer;
and such finesand forfeituresas, by law,are for the
benefit of the State, shall be paid by him to the
trustee of the jury fund forBell county,for the use
of the State. But before the issualof finalprocess,
the defendant may pay the finesor forfeituresto the

716 LAWS OF KENTUCKY
chief of police, who shall pay it over to the city
treasureror the trustee of the jury fund , as the
cityor Statemay be entitledthereto.
§ 17. The defendant shallhave the rightto replevy,
for three months , any judgment for any fine or for-
feiturein saidcitycourt, and any capias pro fine or
fierifacias issuedthereon,by giving bond with good
security. A return of " no property found " on a
writ of fierifacias,or of " not found " on a capias
pro fine, issuedon any judgment of the city court,
shallauthorizean attachment in equity in favor of
the Commonwealth or the city of Pineville against
the choses in action or effects of the defendant or
defendants , in the same manner that a return of " no
property found " authorizesan attachment in equity
on judgments in the circuitcourt.
§ 18. That allpersons adjudged to pay finesby said
citycourt,who do not pay or replevy the same , may
be confinedin the citywork -house (if therebe one)
or Bell county jailuntil the finesand costsof prose-
cution assessed against them are discharged at the
rate of one dollarfor each day's confinement : Pro-
vided, That all able-bodied male persons confined in
said work -house or jail,for a failureto pay or replevy
finesimposed by saidcitycourt, or on a judgment of
fineand imprisonment by said court,shall work out
said fine and costs and judgment of imprisonment ,
under the supervisionof the warden or keeper of the
work -house, chiefof police,or other person appointed
by the board of councilmen, on any of the streets,
alleysor public grounds of said city; and such per-
sonsshallbe creditedon saidfine,or fineand imprison-
ment , at the rateof one dollarper day foreach day's
work : Provided also, That in case of a judgment of
imprisonment againstan able-bodied male person,
without a finebeing imposed , such person shall be re-
quired to work during the day for such period of
imprisonmentupon any of the streets,alleysor pub-
licgrounds of said city,under such supervisionas is

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 717
hereinbeforeprovided. In allcaseswhere the defend-
ant is committed to the citywork -house (if there be
one) or jail, the officerto whom costs are due shall be
allowed the same out of the citytreasury.
§ 19. Witnesses in said city court shall not be allow-
ed any fees for attendance on the court, unless they
livebeyond the limitsof the cityof Pineville, when
they shall be allowed the fees allowed for witnesses
in circuit court .
§ 20. In civilcases, where the amount involved is
within the jurisdictionof circuitcourts,allproceed-
ings shallbe governed by the provisionsof the law
governing proceedingsin circuitcourts. In all other
cases the proceedings shallbe governed by the pro-
visionsof the law governing policecourts and courts
inferior to circuit courts .
§ 21. In all cases where persons are presented to
said court charged with an offensewithin itsjurisdic-
tion,said court may hear and determine the case; and
in cases , the punishment of which is limited to a fine
of twenty dollarsor less, may impose such finewith-
out the interventionof a jury ; in allother cases,he
shall cause a jury to be empaneled , unless a jury be
waived by the party charged.
ARTICLE VIII .
Concerning the City Clerk.
§ 1. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof city
clerk who isnot at the time of his electiona qualified
voter of the city.
§ 2. The cityclerkshall,beforehe entersupon the
discharge of the officialduties of his office,execute a
bond to thecityofPineville,withgood security,tobe
approved by theboard ofcouncilmen,conditionedfor
the faithfulperformance of his officialduty .
§ 3. It shallbe his duty to make a fulland com-
plete record of allthe acts and doings of the board
of councilmen in a book kept for that purpose ; and

718 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
each law and regulation,or ordinanceor resolution,
passed by saidboard of councilmen,shallbe spread in
fullby him on said record-book ; and said act, ordi-
nance,order,regulationor resolutionshallbe copied
by him for publication whenever required by the
board of councilmen ; and he shall make out suitable
poll-books for the officersof the election for elections
of cityofficersunder thisact.
§ 4. Upon the resignation, removal or expirationof
his term of office, the cityclerk shall, upon applica-

recordsbelonging to the city,and upon failureto do

monwealth .
§ 5. The cityclerkshallhold his officeat the pleas-
ure of the board of councilmen , and shall receive such
compensation for hisservicesas the board of council-
men may allow him .
Concerning the City Treasurer.
§ 1. No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof city
treasurer who is not , at the time of his election or
appointment, a qualifiedvoter of the city of Pine-
ville. The board of councilmen of saidcity shall, at
theirfirstmeeting under thischarter,and biennially
thereafter,electa city treasurer, who shall hold his
officefora term of two years,and untilhis successor
iselectedor appointedand qualified, subjectto re-
moval for good cause (of which the board of council-
men shallbe solejudges) at any time.
§2 . The city treasurer, before entering upon the
discharge of the duties of his office, shall take an
oath faithfullyto perform allthe dutiesof his office,
and shallexecute bond ,payable to the cityof Pine-
ville,with security,in the sum of not lessthan five
thousand dollars, to be approved by the board of
councilmen, conditionedthat he will well and faith-

fullyperform the duties of his office,and willkeep

cilmen by warrant drawn on the treasurer, signed by

§5 . He shall be the custodian of the city funds ,
and shall keep a true and faithfulitemized account

cilmen at the expiration of each and every month in

ished by expulsion from officeby resolutionof the
board of councilmen .
§ 6. It shall be the duty of the treasurer to issue
all licensesrequired under this charter, and collect

as the board of councilmen may prescribe.
§ 7. The treasurershallreceivesuch compensation
forhisservicesasthe board of councilmenmay allow

ARTICLE IX .
Concerning the Assessor .
§ 1. The board of councilmen shall, at their first
meeting under this charter, or as soon thereafter as
possible, and bienniallythereafter, shall electa city
assessor, who shallhold his officefor two years, or

fied. Such assessorshall, before entering upon the

board of councilmen , conditioned that he will well
and faithfullydischarge the dutiesof his office. He
shall be controlled in his duties, in the assessment of
persons and property,by the provisionsof this char-
ter, and by such regulationsas may be prescribed by
the city ordinances .
§2 . The board of councilmen shall, by ordinance ,
fix the compensation of the assessor.
§3 . No person shallbe eligibleto the officeof as-
sessor unless he be at least twenty -four years of
age,and a qualifiedvoterof the cityof Pineville.
§ 4. Itshallbe the duty of the assessorto assessthe
taxablepropertyin Pinevilleforeach year as of the first

asland,and every thingattachedtheretoorbuiltthere-

mentioned in section four of this article. Before tak-
ing each list, he shallrequire the person giving said

just valuation of every speciesof propertybelonging

§ 9. An assessment of any lands or improvements
thereon shallnot be invalidforbeing made erroneously
in the name of a person other than the true owner ,nor

:
for the collectionof the tax may then proceed,sub-
jectto the right of such owner to have the valuation

thereofmay be found . His failureso to do shallnot

ments may devolve upon others. He is directednot

thus : as " A. B.'s unknown heirs " or "A. B.'s un-

Supervisors of Taxes .
§1 . The board of councilmenshall, in everyyear,
appoint threediscreetmen as supervisorsof taxes,
and fix theircompensation. The said supervisors
shallqualifyby takingon oath faithfullyand impar-
tiallytoperformthedutiesof theiroffice; and they,or
any two ofthem,shallmeet intheofficeofthe assessor
on the Tuesday following the second Monday in De-
cember in each year, and shallexamine with care the
assessor'sbooks and correctany errorsof the assess

or that may appear to them ,whether in the names of
the parties assessedor in the listsor value of the
property, or otherwise. They shallselecta chairman
by vote or lot,and he, or eitherof said supervisors,

the whole truth concerning his taxable property to

ed. The saidsupervisorsshallhear the complaints of
any tax -payer in person or by agent or attorney ; they

The said supervisorsmay adjourn from day to day
until their work is completed, not exceeding one
week ; any failureor informalityin the electionof
said supervisors, or in their meeting or proceedings,
shall not affect the validity of the taxes .

Concerning Collector of Taxes .
§1 . It shall be the duty of the board of council-
men , on or before the thirdMonday in each Decem-
ber, or as speedilythereafteras possiblein each and
every year thereafter, to electsome competent person
tobe collectorof taxes for the cityof Pineville, who
shallhold his officefor one year, subjectto removal
atany time by saidboard for good cause. The chief
of police may be electedcollectorof taxes. It shall
be the duty of the collectorto collectallthe ad va

lorem and head taxes assessed under the provisions
of thischarter. He shallgive a bond to the cityof

a new bond , or additionalsureties,at any time ; and

ARTICLE XIII .
Concerning City Engineer .
It shall be lawful for the board of councilmen to
electa cityengineer, to hold his officefor a term of
two years. The time of his election, his salary and
his duties,shallbe as may be fixedby ordinance. He
shall have the directionand supervisionof the im-
provements, repairsofpublicways,making of sewers
and drains and other public works ; he shall establish
the grades of all public ways , and keep a record
thereof ; make and keep correct maps of said city,
and perform whatever other duties that may be im-
posed upon him by ordinance. The board of council-
men shallpass allneedfulordinancesto give efficiency
to this department .

§ 3. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and afterthe twelfthday of May , one thousand eight
hundred and ninety .

CHAPTER 835 .
AN ACT to provide for sewerage in the city of Dayton .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the board of councilmen of the city of
Dayton shalldivide the said city into sewerage dis-
tricts, having in view the economical construction of a
system of sewerage adequate to the needs of the city,
and the water -sheds of the territoryincluded therein .
They shallemploy engineersskillfulin such matters,
who , togetherwith the city engineer,shall report to
the said board one or more such divisions, and also
one or more schemes for sewering same , showing di-
mensions of the severalsewers in the district, and
such other data as they deem proper, together with
the estimatesof the cost of the systems reported in
each district, and estimates of the rate of tax on the
one hundred dollarvaluation, according to the then
assessedvalue of real estate in the severaldistricts,
it will requireeach year for twenty years to pay in-
terestat the rateof fiveper cent.on the unpaid part ,
and one-twentiethof the whole cost,according to the
severalschemes . The cityengineer,by and with the
consent of the board of councilmen , as to number
and compensation, may employ such assistantsas

necesary to be done and performed in the conduct of

inexecution such by -laws, rules and regulations, for

dient and proper ; and the same may be amended or

than thirty days previous: Provided , Such by -laws,

§ 2. The capitalstock of the corporation shall be Capitalstock.
fixedby the company ,but shallnot exceed two hun-
dred thousand dollars, and shall be divided into

been paid and discharged.

CHAPTER 851 .
AN ACT toincorporatetheCentralBank .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That there is hereby established a bank of de-
posit, and for other purposes hereinafternamed , in
the cityof Lexington, in Fayette county, Kentucky ,
with a capitalstock of not exceeding five hundred
thousand dollars, in shares of one hundred dollars
each, to be subscribed and paid for as hereinafter
specified, and the subscribers, their associates, suc-
cessorsand assigns,shallbe a body -politicand corpo-
rate, by the name a styleof the Central Bank , and
shall so continue for thirty years from its organiza-
tion; and shallhave allthe rightsand privilegesof a
natural person in contractingand being contracted
with, in suing and being sued, and of impleading
and being impleaded, answeringand defending, in
all courts and places whatsoever. Said bank may
have and use a corporateseal,and change the same at
pleasure .
§ 2. Said bank shall be under the control and man-
agement of nine directors,each of whom shall be a
holder of ten shares of stock ; said directors shall
hold their offices until their successors are elected and
qualified, and said directors, except as hereinafter
provided,shall be electedon the second Tuesday of
January of each year, or as soon thereafter as is
practicallyconvenient; and in case of a vacancy , the
remaining directorsshallhave the power to appoint a
qualifiedstockholderto fillsuch vacancy . The said
directorsshall electone of their own number presi-
dent, and anothervice-president. The presidentshall
presideat the meetings of the board of directors, and
performsuch otherdutiesasmay be assignedhim by
thischarterand the by laws of saidcorporation; and

shallhave the same powers and dutiesas the presi-

sellany of the stock unsold, and declare dividends

pointsuch officers,agents and employes as they may
deem necessaryto conduct the businessof said bank ,
and to provideforthepayment of saidofficers,agents
and employes ; to takefrom thepresident,vice-presi-
dent, cashier and other officersand employes such

and reasonable to secure a faithful performance of

such by -laws not to be contraryto the Constitution
or laws of the Stateof Kentucky or the United States.
The stock of saidbank shallbe deemed personalprop-
erty, and shall be transferableonly upon the books
of the bank by the stockholder,his agent or attor-
ney , under such rulesand regulationsas the board of
directorsshall,from time to time, establish; but the
corporation shallhave a priorlienupon allstock for

CHAPTER 854 .
AN AUT toincorporatethe Richmond Brick-making Company .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That Sam Rice, A. R. Burnam , J. Stone
Walker , O. H. Chenault , W. T. Harris , H. B. Dil .
lingham and Charles S. Powell, and theirassociates,
successorsand assigns,be, and they are hereby,con-
stituteda body -corporate,under the name and styleof
the Richmond Brick Company , and by that name
shallhave power to contractand be contracted with,
sue and be sued ; have and use a common seal, and
change the same at pleasure.
§2 . That the capitalstock of said company shall
be ten thousand dollars, of shares of one hundred
dollarseach ; but the capitalstock may be increased
to any sum not exceeding twenty -fivethousand dol-
lars, whenever, in the opinion of the company , as

784 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
expressed by the board of directors, the business
of the company may require it.
§3 . Any three of the persons hereinbefore-named
may open books for the subscriptionof stock, and
whenever ten thousand dollars of stock shall have been
subscribed, and ten per centum thereon has been paid
in, the stockholders shall meet, and shall then, and
on the firstTuesday of April of each year thereafter,
electa board of fivedirectors, who shall manage the
affairsof the company . The board of directorsshall
electa president, secretaryand treasurerfrom their
number ,and may employ a superintendent and such
otheragents as may be necessaryto carry on the busi-
ness, and may take a bond from any one or all of
them for a faithfulperformance of duties; any two
of the above positionsmay be filledby one person .
Said directors and officersshall hold their offices for
one year, and untiltheirsuccessorsare elected.
§ 4. The officeand chiefplace of business of said
company shallbe inRichmond ,Kentucky. The com-
pany shallhave power to acquire and hold,by pur-
chase or lease, allthe real and personal estateneces-
sary, in the opinion of the board of directors, for
carryingon the company's business; make , burn and
sellcommon and pressed brick; make contracts to
erect buildings, walls or any other kind of brick-
work .
§ 5. The private and individual property of the
stockholders shall not be liable for the debts or lia-
bilitiesof the corporation .
§ 6. Assessments on the capitalstock shall not be
collected,except as the business of the company re-
quires; but should any stockholderfailto pay his
assessmentfortendays afteritiscalledfor,the com-
pany shallhave a lienon his stockfor the unpaid
part,and may enforcepayment by a saleofhisstock,
afterhaving advertiseditfor twenty days .
§ 7. This act shall take effect from and after its
passage Approved April 17, 1890 .

§ 3. The followingcommissioners are appointed to

or as much as may be necessary to construct all or a

agement of six directors, one of whom shallbe chosen
president by saiddirectors.
§ 5. That J. W. Beatty, A. W. Skinner, W. L.
Spears, W. W. Muir , R. L. Henderson and R. B.
Hutchcraft are hereby appointed and constituteda
board of directorsto control and manage the affairs

ninety -one .

stockholders on the firstMonday in January , and

ors are elected and qualified. At allelectionsthe
stockholders may vote by proxy or in person,casting
one vote for each share of stock owned .

794 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
§ 7. That said company may contract and be con-
tracted with, sue and be sued, plead and be im-
pleaded, and in alllitigationsshallbe dealt with as
a naturalperson.
§ 8. When said road is completed, said company
may erecta toll-gate and charge tollfor the distance

stock .

Approved April 17,1890.
CHAPTER 861 .
AN ACT to amend , revise and reduce into one the several acts in
relationto the town of Mount Washington,in Bullittcounty.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
Boundary .
§1 . The boundary of the town of Mount Wash-
ington, in Bullittcounty,is hereby declared to be as
follows: Beginning at T. C. Long's house on the
Mans Lick road , one -fourth of a mile west of the
old town limits, including said house ; thence north
on a straightline to a stake,corner in W. P. Barne's
land ; thence at right-angleseast on a straightlineto
a stake on R. Griggsby's land, one-fourth of a mile
east of the old town limits; thence south at right-
angles on a straightline to a stake on J. W. Showal-
ter'sland ; thence west at right-angles on a straight
lineto a stake on W. S. McFarland's land,one fourth
of a mile west of the old town limits ; thence north
atright-angleson a straightlinetothepointofbegin-
ning .
§ 2. It shallbe the duty of the trusteesof said town ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 795
:
tees .
as soon as may be afterthe firstelectionunder thisact,Dutiesoftrus-
to cause a survey and plot of said town to be made
and prepared according to the boundary specifiedin
the firstsection,and to cause permanent monuments
to be erected at the place of beginning and at the
various corners, which plot shall show the various
subdivisionsof said town intolots,streetsand alleys.
The names of the streetsand alleys,and the numbers
of the lots as heretoforeestablished, shall, as far as
practicable,be preserved;and such land as may be in-
cluded in said boundary , not heretoforeembraced in
the corporate limitsof said town ,shall be designated
on said plotby the names of the owners. The trustees
shallcause saidplot to be recordedin the county court
clerk's officeof Bullittcounty, and a certifiedcopy
thereof,under the hand of the clerk,shallbe admitted
in evidence in all courts .
§3 . The municipal government of said town shall
consist of fivetrustees, one of whom shall be selected
by the board to presideover theirmeetings, and shall
be styled Chairman of the Board of Trusteesof Mount
Washington .
§ 4. Said trustees and their successors in officeshall Body-corporate.
be a body -politicand corporate, under the name and
- style of the trusteesof Mount Washington ; and by
that name they may , for and on behalf of said town ,
contract and be contracted with, sue and be sued,
plead and be impleaded,defendand be defended,and
in all courts and places do all thingswhich a body-
corporatewith perpetualsuccessionmay lawfullyand

§ 5. The other officersof said town shallbe a police
judge, marshal, attorney, clerk, treasurer, assessor
✓ and collector,and such otherofficersas the trustees
may , from to time, directand appoint.
Elections .
§ 6. An electionshallbe held in said town on the
firstSaturday in June ,in the year one thousand eight

796 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
hundred and ninety,and on the firstMonday inApril,
one thousand eight hundred and ninety-two , and on
the firstMonday in April every two years thereafter,
at the regularplace of holding county and State elec-
tions in said town , of which previous public notice
shall be given by written or printed notices posted in
at leastfour public places in said town by the town
clerk ; but the firstelection under this act shall be
ordered and held as provided in the next succeeding
section of this act .
§ 7. The firstelectionof officersunder thisact, the
judge of the BullittCounty Court shall appoint two
judges, a clerk and sheriff, to hold the same , who
shall be legal voters of said town : Notice of said
electionshallbe given by the sheriffof Bullittcounty ,
by written or printed notices posted up at four or
more public places in said town for ten days before
the said election, which shall be held from eight
o'clock in the forenoon until six o'clock afternoon .
§ 8. The poll-books of the firstelectionheld under
this act shallbe returned by the sheriffof the elec-
tion within three days to the clerk of the Bullitt
County Court,who shall safelykeep the same in his
cffice. He shallexamine and compare said pollsand
issuecertificatesof electionto the persons receiving
the greatest number of votes ; the poll-books of all
subsequentelectionsshallbe returnedby thesheriffof
electionto the clerk of the town of Mount Washing-
ton, who shallperform , in regard to the same , the
dutiesthatdevolve upon the county examining board
in county elections; but the certificatesof election
issuedby the town clerk,beforebeing delivered, shall
be approved by and spreadupon the recordsof the
board of trustees .
§ 9. At the biennial election there shall be elected
by the qualifiedvotersof said town fivetrustees, a
policejudge and a marshalforthetown,and the per-
sons receivingthe highest number of votes for any of
said offices shall be declared elected .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 797
§ 10. The officerselectedby the people under this
act shall hold their respectiveofficestwo years, the
firstterm from the date of their qualification; and
after the firstterm under this act they shall hold their
officesfor two years from the firstday of May after
their election and qualification. All other officers
shallhold theirofficesat the pleasureof the trustees.
§ 11. No person shallbe,eligibleto any officein said
town who isnot a citizenthereofand a qualifiedvoter
under the laws of the State ; and no one shall be eli-
gible to the officeof policejudge, trustee, marshal or
attorney who has not, at the of his election,residedin
said town at leastsix months preceding such election.
§ 12. Every person elected or appointed to office
under the provisionsof thisact,beforeenteringupon
the duties thereof, shall take and subscribe before
some authorized officerthe oath of officeprescribed
by the Constitutionof this Commonwealth , and file
the same,duly certifiedby the officerbeforewhom it

§ 13. Whenever a vacancy shallhappen inany office
filledby election, itshall be filledby appointment of

§ 14. When two or more candidatesfor any elective
officeshall have an equal number of votes, the person

the trusteesmay direct, in the presence of the trus-
tees and at least three other persons .
§ 15. The trustees,beforeevery biennialtown elec-

sation of all officersshall be fixed by the trustees;
but their liabilitiesshall be the same as that of offi-
cersof State electionsunder the general laws of this
Commonwealth .
§ 16. The electionherein provided forshallbe held
between the hours of eight o'clock in the forenoon
and five o'clock in the afternoon ; and all laws in

force in thisCommonwealth punishing illegalvoting
at the State electionsshallapply to illegalvoting at

scribingpenaltiesin generalelectionsfor briberyshall
apply to electionsheld under thisact, and the same

§ 17. All qualifiedvoters under the laws of this
Commonwealth who shall, at the time of offeringto
vote, have resided in said town sixty days previous
thereto, shall be qualifiedvoters at any electionfor
said town .
§ 18. The board of trusteesshalljudge of the quali-
fications, electionsand returns of its members ; and
casesof contestedelections,for any town officerselec-
tiveby the people, shallbe heard and determined by
the trustees,under such rulesand regulationsas they ,

§19 . The out-going town clerk shall certifyto the
Secretaryof State the name of the person elected to

ficer .
§20 . The poll-books of all electionsheld in said

of the electionto the clerkof the town ,who shallpre-
servethem as partof the recordsof his office, examine
and compare the same , and issue certificatesof elec-
tion to the officerselected,which certificates, before
being delivered, shall be approved by , and spread
upon the records of the board of trustees.
Powers and Duties of Officers.
§ 21. The clerk shall keep the corporate seal, if
there be one, and keep a complete record of the pro-
ceedings of the board of trustees, whose meetings it
shallbe his duty to attend ; and transcriptsfrom the
recordsof the proceedings certifiedby him shall be
evidence in all courts of this Commonwealth . He

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 799
shall draw all warrants on the treasurydirected by
the board of trustees, and shallcountersignthe same ,
and keep an accurate account thereof in a book to be
provided for the purpose. He shall issue licenses,

§ 22. The treasurershallreceiveallmoney belonging
to the town , and keep an accurateaccount of receipts
and expenditures. He shallpay out money only on
the order of the board of trustees,and countersigned
- by the clerk,and the ordersshallspecifythe purpose
for which the amount named therein is paid. The
treasurer shall exhibit to the board of trustees, at

- receiptsand expenditures afterthe date of the last
annual report, and shall state the condition of the

of the board of trustees .
§ 23. The marshal shall attend all sessions of the
policecourt, and give the town attorneynoticeof any
proceedings in saidcourtwhich itishisduty to attend.
He shallexecute and make due return of allprocess
directed to him by the Mount Washington Police
Court , or by any other court of this Commonwealth .
He shall possess all the powers and authority of a
constable,and his powers forperforming the dutiesof
his office shall be co -extensive with the limits of Bul-
litt county . He shall be entitled to the same fees
allowed by law to sheriffsand constablesfor similar
services, to be due and collectedin the same manner .
He may or may not be appointed as collectorfor said
town .
§ 24. The collectormust collectall taxes and assess-
ments which may be leviedby the board of trustees,
and for that purpose he shallhave all the powers of
a sheriffto make salesof personaland realestate; he
shall pay over to the treasurer all taxes and other

800 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
money which may come intohis hands as the board
of trusteesmay direct When the tax listis placed
in his hands ,he shall proceed at once to collectthe
same ; and to enforce payment may levy on and
sell personal property after advertising the same
by written or printed noticesposted up in at least
three public places in the town for ten days .
Within three months from the time he receives said
list, he shall make out and return to the board of
trusteesa report showing the amount collected, the
names of those who have paid and those who are
delinquent; he shall not be entitledto credit in his
settlementwith the board of trusteesby the listof
any person who owns personal property in said town
out of which the taxes can be made . Upon the re-
turn of the collector'sreport, as above provided, the
board of trusteesshall,by order duly entered of rec-
ord,directa warrant to be made out, signed by the

ing the collectorto levy upon and sellso much of the

as may be necessary to pay the taxes due and the

shall proeeed to make leviesas thereby commanded ,
and shallsellthe propertyleviedon forcash at public

estateso purchased, unlessthe same be redeemed as

tees a full and complete return of the manner in
which he has executed said warrant , which return

shallbe spread upon the recordsof the board . The

who shall not,within ten days afternotificationand

erty, papers and effectsof every description in his

Police Court .
§31.Thereisherebyestablishedinsaidtown a court,
to be styled the Mount Washington Police Court .
Said court shallbe held by one judge , who shall be
styledthe " Judge of the Mount Washington Police
Court," who shallbe electedand qualifiedas herein-
before provided .
§ 32. The Mount Washington Police Court shall
have exclusivejurisdictionof all prosecutionsfor all
violationsof theby-lawsand ordinancesofsaidtown ,

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 803
and concurrent jurisdictionwith the other courts of

rants and all other process in the name of the Com-
monwealth to compel the attendanceof witnesses; to

swer the same , and the same power to declare the

cuit courts have . Itshallhave power to enterjudg-
ment and award , and to issuewritsfor the purpose of

shall be a conservator of the peace ; he shall have

§ 33. The policecourt shallbe held at such place as
the trusteesshall designate, or they failingto desig-
nate and provide a suitableplace, at such place in

§ 34. Said court shall have no power to empannel a
grand jury,nor shallany indictmentbe necessaryto
the exercise of the jurisdictionhereby conferred on
said court. It may punish contempt by a fineof not

ing twenty -four hours. No person shall be triedin

more than twenty dollarswithout the interventionof
a jury, unlesshe shallwaive the privilegeof such
trialby jury.
§35 . Said court shallbe a court of record, and the
judge thereofshallact as clerk; and copiesof the

804 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
records thereof, over the signatureof the judge ,shall
be evidencein any court in thisCommonwealth .
§ 36. The jurisdictionand dutiesof the policejudge
in holding examining trialsshall be such as are now
prescribedby the generallaws of thisCommonwealth

to that of two justicesof the peace in such cases.
§ 37. All process from said police court shall be in
the name of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and
may be directedto any officerauthorized by law to
execute such process.
§ 38. All finesand forfeituresrecovered in said court
for breaches of the by -laws or town ordinances are
hereby granted to the town of Mount Washington ,
and shallbe paid by the officercollectingthe same to
the treasurerof the town . Before the issuing of any
executionor finalprocesson a judgment of said court
for a fine or forfeiture, the defendant may pay the
same to thejudgeof thecourt. The defendantmay
also,beforeor afterthe issuingof any execution or
capiaspro fine,replevyfor threemonths any judg-
ment ,fineor forfeitureof the policecourt,in the same
manner as is allowed by law in similarcases within
the jurisdictionof othercourtsof thisCommonwealth .
All finesimposed in said court for any violationof
any of the generallaws of the Stateshallbe accounted
for to the Commonwealth as in courts of justicesof
the peace .
§ 39. A returnof not found on a capias pro fine,
and of no property found on a writ of fierifacias ,
issuedon any judgment of the policecourt,shall au-
thorizean equitableproceeding in favor of the trus-
tees of Mount Washington against the defendant for
the enforcement of satisfactionof such judgment in
the same manner as the return of no property found
authorizessuch proceedingsin civilcases.
§ 40. Upon alljudgments forfinesrendered by said

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 805
court,and which shallnot be paid or repleviedat the
time of rendering the same , an execution against the
estate of the defendant may be issued, or a capias
pro fine may be issued, requiringthe imprisonment of
the defendant in the county or town jailor work-
house until the same shall be paid ; but the imprison-
ment shall not exceed one day for each dollar of such

discretion, assign such defendant so fined to hard

the town untilhe shall pay the fineadjudged against
him at the rateof one dollar per day ; and he shall
not be required to labor more than eight hours per
day . While at labor, such defendant shallbe under
the controlof the town marshal or surveyor of roads,
each of whom shallbe responsiblefor any negligence
in allowing his escape. When not at labor, such de-
fendant shallbe imprisoned as provided in this sec-
tion or as the court may directby itsorder; and for
the purpose of putting him in jail or the work-
house , or taking him out to labor on the streets
or roads, a certifiedcopy of the judgment assigning
him out to labor shall be sufficientauthority to the
marshal , jailer, surveyor of roads or keeper of the
work house . All persons sentenced to imprisonment
by the judgment of said court may be assigned to
labor in like manner as those fined. When persons
imprisoned, as contemplated by this section, or con-
finedin the county jail, the jailer'sfeesshall be paid
by the town .
§ 41. The Mount Washington Police Court shall
have jurisdictionof civilcases for the recovery of
money or personal property when the amount in con-
troversy, exclusiveof interestand costs,does not ex-
ceed one hundred dollars. The proceedingsas to
pleadings and filingstatements and exhibitsin said
court shallbe regulatedby theprovisionsoftheCivil
Code of Practiceand laws governing the proceedings
in justices' courts. The judge of said court shall

806 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
have power tograntordersofattachment,injunctions
and restrainingorders, orders of delivery, writs of
habeas corpus,distresswarrants and writs of forcible
entry and detainer, under the same circumstances,
rulesand regulationsas justicesof the peace or courts
of like jurisdictionand powers . Monthly terms of

order entered upon the order-book of said court.
The time for holding such terms shall be on the
fourth Saturdays in each month until the same are
changed in the manner provided in this section. The
trusteesof Mount Washington shallprovide the judge
of said court with an order -book , execution -book and
docket-book ;and itshallbe hisduty to keep a record
of all proceedings in his court, in the same manner
and to the same extent as isrequiredof judges of the
quarterlycourtsby the laws of this Commonwealth .
He shallpreserveallpapers and recordspertaining to
his office, and deliverthem to his successorin good
order. His jurisdictionshallbe at law and equity of
one hundred dollars .
§42. Appeals shallliefrom decisionsand judgments
of the Mount Washington PoliceCourt to the Bullitt
Circuit Court in criminal and penal cases when the
fineexceeds ten dollarsor the imprisonment exceeds
fivedays ; but no judgment shall be suspended by
such appeal unlessthe defendant, in fivedays after
the renditionthereof,shallfilea copy of said judg-
ment with the clerk of the circuitcourt, and execute
bond , as requiredby sectionthreehundred and sixty-
four of the Criminal Code of Practice .
§ 43. Appeals shallliefrom the judgments of said
police court to the BullittCircuitCourt in civilcases
when the amount in controversy,exclusiveof interest
and costs,exceeds ten dollars; and in writsof forcible
entry and forcibledetainer,and other specialproceed-
ings,the appeal and proceedingsthereon shallbe the

same as in similarproceedingsin courtsof justicesof
the peace .
§ 44. The manner of taking appeals, and all pro-
ceedings thereon, except as otherwise herein indi-
cated , shall conform to the provisions of the Civil and
Criminal Codes of Practice of this Commonwealth .
§ 45. The feesof the policejudge shallbe the same
in all cases as are now or may be hereaftergiven by

policejudge shallbe subjectto the same responsibility
and regulations, as to taxing and collectingcosts and

Taxation .
§ 46. The trustees of Mount Washington shall have
power , subject to the exceptions hereinaftermen-
tioned, to annually levy and collecttaxes, not more
than twenty -five cents on each one hundred dollars'
worth of property in said town ,includingevery thing
that is now or may be hereaftersubjectby the State
for revenue purposes;but personal property not situ-
ated within the town ,and chosesin actionowned by
citizensof the town , are not to be taxed. They may
also levy a head or poll-tax not exceeding one dollar
and fiftycents upon every male inhabitant of said
town over twenty -one years of age , and a tax of not
more than one dollarupon every dog that any person
may keep or sufferto be kept on his or her premises
in said town . They may also levy a road tax not ex-
ceeding ten cents on each one hundred dollars' worth
of property taxable in said town for State revenue,
and a poll-tax upon each male over twenty -one and
not over fiftyyears of age : Provided, That the town
trustees, the chairman of trusteesof common schools,
licensedministersof the gospel,and persons unable to
perform manual labor, shallbe exempt from the poll-
tax for working roads .
§ 47. There shall be exempt from taxation in said

808 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
town allpropertyexempted from taxationby the gen-
eral laws of this Commonwealth .
§ 48. All taxes imposed by said trusteesunder this
act shallbe a lien upon the property, real and per-
sonal, againstwhich the same has been assessed, until
the taxes are paid, and no sale or transferof such
property shallaffectthe lienthereon.
§49 . The right of redemption in all casesof sales
of real estate for taxes shall exist to the owner or his
heirsand devisees, or personal representative, for two
years from the date of the sale, on payment of the
amount for which the same was sold, with ten per
cent.thereon, and lawful interestadded ,and all the
taxes accruing subsequent to the sale. If the owner
at the time of the sale be an infant, married woman ,
or of unsound mind , the right to redeem shall exist
for two years afterthe removal of the disability. If
not redeemed within the time herein provided, the
board of trusteesshall,on presentationof the certifi-
cateof sale,convey such real estateto the purchaser.
§ 50. The trustees shall make such rules and regu-
lationsconcerning the assessmentof property in said
town as they may see proper ; and also for the re-
vising and correctingof the listsreturned by the
assessor .
§ 51. All fines, forfeituresand assessments herein
contemplated and provided for, except for finesim-
posed for a violationof the generallaws of the State,
shallbe appropriatedexclusivelyfor the use and ben-
efit of said town .
General Powers and Duties of the Trustees .
§ 52. The board of trustees shall hold stated meet-
ings, and the chairman, or in his absence the town
clerk, may call meetings by notice to each of the
members . A majority of the members elected shall
constitute a quorum . In the absence of the chair-
man any member of the board may be selected to
discharge his duties for the time .

CHAPTER 863 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe town of Mount Savage,in Cartercounty.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the town of Mount Savage, in Carter
county,isherebyincorporated,and the limitsof said
town shall be one -fourth of a mile in all directions
from the railroaddepot as now locatedin said town .
§2 . That John W ..Harris, O. F. L. Beckette and
John C. Humphreys are hereby appointed trusteesof
saidtown ,who shallchoose one of theirbody as chair-
man ;and a majorityshallconstitutea quorum to do
business. F. L. Porter isby thisact appointed police
judge,and L. W. Harris town marshal for said town .
Termsofoffice.All the town officershereby appointed shallremain in
officeuntil the firstSaturday in May , one thousand
eight hundred and ninety-one,and upon which day ,
and upon the firstSaturday in May bienniallythere-
after, the male citizensof said town entitled to vote
for Representative of the General Assembly , of the
age of twenty-one years, shallmeet at such place as
shallbe designated by the trusteesof said town , and
choose personsto serveas policejudge, town marshal
and trustees,by vote viva voce, to serve as officers of
saidtown fortwo years,and untiltheirsuccessorsare
electedand qualified.
§3 . That all citizensliving in the town who are
qualifiedto vote and hold officeunder the Constitu-
tion and laws of Kentucky are entitled to vote and
hold office in said town .
§ 4. Said officers, including the trustees, shall, be-
forethey enterupon thedutiesof theiroffice,take an
oathprescribedby law ;and the marshalshallexecute
bond inthecountycourtwith approvedsecurity,con-
ditionedforthefaithfuldischargeof his duty.
§ 5. Saidpolicejudge shallhave concurrentjuris

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 819
dictionwith justicesof the peace in Carter county in
allcivil, criminal and penal cases,and to charge the
same feesfor servicesas justicesof the peace are al-
lowed to charge. The marshal shallhave concurrent
jurisdictionwith constables of Carter county, and
charge the same fees for theirservices.
§ 6. The board of trustees created under this act
shallprovide,priortothefirstelection,properofficers
for holding the same ; and they shallgive ten days '
noticeof the time and placeof holding said election
by written noticeposted in said town ; and the trus-
teesare empowered to pass such by -laws and enact
such regulationsas may be necessaryfor the govern-
ment and well-being of saidtown ,not in contravention
with the Constitutionand laws of the Commonwealth ,
and to fillany vacancy in any officecreated by this
act that may occur .
§7 . All finesand forfeitures inflictedand collected
under the ordinancesand by -laws of said town shall
be paid to the treasurer of the town for the use and
benefit of the corporation .
§8 . The board of trustees shall consist of three
members , one of the members to act as chairman and
one as clerk; and they shallkeep a record of their
proceedings,and such proceedingsshallbe subject to
theinspectionof the citizensat any time ; and allby-
laws or ordinances passed by the trusteesshallbe
posted up by them in not lessthan three publicplaces
in said town , and not lessthan ten days before the
same shall take effect.
§ 9. The said trusteesmay sue and be sued in their
corporate capacity in the name of the trustees of the
town of Mount Savage .
§ 10. This act shall take effect from . and after its
passage .
Approved April17,1890.

CHAPTER 864 .
AN ACT toamend an act to incorporatetheDepositBank of Bourbon
county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That an act to incorporatethe Deposit Bank of
Paris, approved March seventh, one thousand eight
hundred and fifty-one , be amended as follows : That
section two be so amended as to read : " Said bank
may receivedeposits of gold and silver, bank notes,
and other notes which may be lawfullycirculatingas
money ,and repay the same in such manner and at
such time as may be agreed upon by the depositors
by specialor generalcontract; may deal in loaning
of money , exchange , promissory notes, and other
evidencesof debt ; take personal and other securities
forthe payment thereof,and dispose of the same as

spectsas naturalpersons may do under the law ; and
thepromissorynotesmade negotiableand payable at

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 821
CHAPTER 865 .
AN ACT to amend an act,entitled" An act to reduce intoone the
severalactsin relationto the town of Shelbyville," approvedMarch
twenty-second,one thousandeighthundred and seventy-three.
Be itenactedby theGeneralAssembly of theCom-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That sectionstwenty-eight, twenty-nine, thirty
and thirty-four of said act,entitled"An act toreduce
into one the several acts in relation to the town of
Shelbyville," approved March twenty , one thousand
eight hundred and seventy-three, be, and the same
are hereby , repealed, and the following sectionsare
substituted therefor :
§ 2. In lieuof sectiontwenty -eight of said act, the
following : The said board of trustees are hereby
authorized and empowered to charge and collectany
sum , not lessthan fivehundred dollarsnor more than
one thousand dollarsper year,for each tavernlicense,
with the privilegeof sellingintoxicatingliquorsby
retail, to be drunk on the premises where sold.
§ 3. In lieu of sectiontwenty -nine of said act, the
following : The said board of trustees shall have
power and authorityto charge and collectany sum ,
not less than three hundred dollars nor more than six
hundred dollarsper year, for each " grocery license."
And a " grocery license," within the meaning of this
act, shallonly authorizethe saleof spirituous,vinous
or malt liquorsin quantitiesnot lessthan a quart, not
to be drunk on the premises where sold or adjacent
thereto .
§ 4. In lieuof sectionthirtyof said act,the follow-
ing : They shall have power and authorityto charge
and collectany sum , not lessthan three hundred dol-
larsnor more than six hundred dollarsper year, for
each druggist license,which shallonly authorize the
saleof spirituous, vinous or malt liquorsby retailfor

822 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
medical purposes, and not tobe drunk on the premises
where sold or adjacentthereto.
§ 5. In lieu of sectionthirty-four of said act, the
following: No person shall be a qualifiedelector for
the municipality of Shelbyvillewho shallnot, at the
time he offersto vote,have paid all the taxes due by
him to said town at leastthirty days prior to said
election .
§ 6. Said board of trustees shall not have power to
renew any tavern,grocery or drug license, as defined
in the preceding sections,to any holder of same , to
sell liquor thereunder at any place within said town
until the licenseof said person previously granted
shallhave expired. Nor shallthey grant any of said
licensesto any person to sellliquor in any house or
building in saidtown in which liquorisbeing sold at
the time said licenseis applied for, or in which a
licensehas theretoforebeen granted to sellliquor,
until the expiration of the current year for which
said licenseshall have been granted .
§ 7. All actsand partsof acts in conflictwith this
act are hereby repealed.
§ 8. This act to take effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 18, 1890 .

CHAPTER 867 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the cityof Ludlow ,and procure &
water supply for said cityand itsinhabitants.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the cityof Ludlow is hereby authorized
topurchase from the cityof Covington, and the city
of Covington is hereby authorized to sell and furnish
to the cityof Ludlow a supply of water to said city of
Ludlow ,and the inhabitantsthereof, and other per-
sons or corporationsdoing business therein, for such
length of time,and upon such terms and conditions,
as the proper municipal authoritiesof said citiesmay
agree upon .
§2. For thepurposesofacquiringlandsnecessary
for the constructionand maintaining the pipes and
mains connectingthe cityof Ludlow with the water-
pipe system of the city of Covington, the citycoun-
cilof the cityof Ludlow ,in the name of, and for and
in behalf of the city of Ludlow , may acquire said
land by purchase,gift,or by condemnation proceed-
ings in any court of competent jurisdictionin the
county of Kenton ,in the manner provided in an act
of theGeneral Assembly ,entitled"An act to prescribe
the mode of condemning land for the use of railroad
and turnpike companies," which was approved April
eleventh, one thousand eight hundred and eighty-
two ; and for the purpose aforesaid may use and
occupy any road, street,alleyor other public ground
upon such terms and conditions as may be agreed
upon between the citycouncilof the cityof Ludlow
and the municipal corporations, or privatecorpora-
tions,persons or individualsowning or having charge
of the same ; and if no terms and conditions can be
agreed upon for the use and occupation of such street,
road,alley or other public ground , the said city of
Ludlow may use and occupy the same ,paying therefor

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 825
to the municipal or other corporation, person or indi-
vidual owning or having charge thereof,such damages
as may accrue to such for the use and occupation there-
of for the purposes aforesaid,the damage to be as-
certained and determined in the mode provided by
law for the condemnation of land for the use of rail-
road and turnpike companies above referred to.

CHAPTER 874 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Louisvilleand DanvilleRailroad Com-
pany .
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That M. J. Durham , Charles Schiff, C. H.
Rodes,C. B. Simralland Alfred Mack ,or those of
them who shall act, their associates, successors or
assigns,be,and theyare hereby,createda body -cor-
porate, with perpetualsuccession, under the name
and styleof " The Louisvilleand Danville Railroad
Company ;" and in such name to have fullpower to
contractand be contractedwith, sue and be sued ;
have a common seal, and alteror change the same at
pleasure;construct,operate,equipand manage a rail-
road,with singleor double tracksand appendages,
and linesof telegraphashereinafterprovided.
§ 2. Said company shallhave an authorized capital
stock of fivemilliondollars,which may be increased
at any time by a vote of the majorityof itsstock-
holders to not exceeding ten million dollars, and
which shall be divided into shares of the par value
of one hundred dollars each .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 837
§ 3. The businessof said company shallbe the con-
struction, operation, equipment and management of
a railroad,with single or double tracks,and lines of
telegraph and allthe necessary, usual or convenient
real estate, personal property, appendages , fixtures
and equipment now necessary,usual or convenient to
railroads, or which may become necessary,usual or
convenient for the operation or management of the
same , from a point at or near Louisville, in the
county of Jefferson, to or near a point on the Cin-
cinnati Southern Railway ,at or near Danville, in the
county of Boyle , and passing through the countiesof
Boyle,Mercer,Washington,Anderson,Nelson,Spen-
cer, Shelby , Bullittand Jefferson, or such of them ,
or such parts of them , as may be determined by the
board of directorsof said company .
§ 4. For the purpose of constructingand maintaining
said railroad and itsappendages , said company may
acquire, by purchase or gift,so much land as may be
or become necessarytoconstruct,maintainand operate
and complete said railroadand itsappendages : Pro-
vided, That ifthe necessarylands forthe construction,
operation and maintenance of said railway and its
appendages can not be obtained by agreement with
the persons, corporations or authoritiesowning or
controllingthe same ,the said company may proceed
to condemn the same in the manner prescribed by
law for the condemnation of lands for railroad and
turnpike purposes.
§ 5. Said company may , for the purpose of con-
structing and maintaining itsrailroadand append-
ages, occupy or use any turnpike road, street or
public way or grounds ,or any parť thereof,upon such
terms and conditionsas may be agreed upon between
said company and the municipal or other corpora-
tions, persons or publicauthoritiesowning or having
charge thereof; full power and authorityto make
such agreement being hereby conferred on such
municipal or other corporations, persons or public

838 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
authorities owning or having charge thereof. If no
agreement can be made for the rightto use or occupy
such turnpike road,ground ,or any part thereof, the
said company may take and appropriate said rights
in the manner provided by law for the condemnation
of lands forrailway and turnpike purposes.
§ 6. Authority is hereby given said company to
cross the tracks of any other company , when neces-
sary for the constructionand operationof its road ,
by paying a fair compensation forsuch use ; and in
the event a mutual agreement can not be effected with
such othercompany for such use, then the same may
be condemned in the manner provided by law for the
condemnation of lands for railway and turnpike pur-
poses .
§7. Saidcompany shallhave fullpower and authority
to borrow money and execute itsnotes,bonds or other
evidencesof indebtedness for the same , bearing such
rateof interestand payable at such times and places as
itsboard of directors may determine , and selland dis-
pose ofthesame asitsboard ofdirectorsmay deem best;
and itmay securethe payment of any indebtedness of
the company by mortgage or pledge of the whole or
any partof itsproperty,income or franchises,rightsor
privileges. Said company may sellor leaseitsrailway,
or any part thereof,or itmay operatethe same in such
manner as itsboard of directors may determine : Pro-
vided, That any sale or leaseshall be ratifiedor con-
curred in by a vote of two- thirdsof the stockholders
of such company before the same shallbe valid or
binding. Said company may purchase or lease any
railroad, or part thereofconnectingwith said railroad
or any branch or branches thereof,either within or
without the stateof Kentucky , or itmay construct a
branch or brancnes of itsline of railway so as to con-
nect with other linesof railway; and forsuch purpose
said company shallhave the same power and author-
ityforcondemning rightsofway as isconferredupon
it by the precedingsectionshereof.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 839
§ 8. The affairsof said company shallbe managed
and controlledby a board of directors,consistingof
not lessthan fivestockholders,and which may be in-
creased by a majority vote of the stockholdersat any
time to not exceeding thirteen. Said board of direct-
ors may establishsuch by -laws as itmay deem forthe
best interestof the company : Provided ,The same are
not inconsistent with the provisions of this act or the
laws or Constitution of the United States or of the
State of Kentucky . The board of directorsshall
electone of itsnumber president, and shallelectsuch
other officersas may be deemed advisable. Said board
of directorsshallhave fullpower and authority to do
allactsand things proper or convenient for carrying
into effectthe powers hereingranted,or to accomplish
the purposes forwhich said company isorganized.
§ 9. Each share of stock shall entitle the holder
thereof to one vote, eitherin person or by proxy .
§ 10. The first board of directors shall be elected at
such time as the incorporatorsmay provide; and there-
after said board shall be electedas provided in the
by -laws of saidcompany . The incorporatorsmay open
books for subscriptionto the capitalstock of said
company at such times or places as they may deter-
mine . Payment of subscriptionsshallbe at such time
or times as the board of directorsmay determine ; and
said board shallhave power to issuepaid-up and non-
assessablecapitalstockof saidcompany in payment
for lands,propertyor otherrightsnecessaryforsaid
railroad .
§ 11.The privateor individualproperty of the
stockholders of said company shallnot be liablefor,
or subjectto, the corporatedebtsor liabilities.
§ 12. Provided, That saidcompany shallin good
faith commence the construction of said road within
two yearsfrom and afterthisbillshallbe approved
by theGovernor,and shallcompletethesame within
fiveyears from saiddate.
§ 13. This act shalltake effectand be in forcefrom
and after itspassage . Approved April 18, 1890.

CHAPTER 875 .
AN ACT regulatingthe electionof directors, trusteesor managers of
the institutionsor corporationsof the cityof Louisville, whose di-
rectors, trusteesor managers are, by law, electedby the general
councilof the cityof Louisville.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That whenever a vacancy occurs in the board of
directors, managers or trusteesof any of the institu-
tions or corporations, whose directors, managers or
trusteesare, by law, electedby the general council
of the cityof Louisville, such vacancy shallbe filled
by the general council of said city, and not other-
wise .
§2. If, at the time for the regularelectionof such
boards of directors, managers or trusteesfixedby the
chartersof such institutionsand corporations, the
general councilshould failto hold the election, then
said institutionsor corporationsshallnot be dissolved,
but the old boards shall hold over until the general
council shall hold a new election, which they may do
at any time .
§ 3. All laws or parts of laws in conflictor incon-
sistent herewith are hereby repealed: Provided ,
Nothing in thisact shallbe construed to repeal any
part of an act, entitled"An act concerning the board
of commissioners of the sinking fund of the city of
Louisville ."
§ 4. This act to take effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
Approved April18,1890.

CHAPTER 885 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof Washington county,by which the Wash-
ingtonCounty Courtisnot allowedtoappropriatea greatersum than
fivehundred dollarsper miletoaidinconstructingturnpikeroads.
WHEREAS , The Washington County Court has , for
many years,been making large appropriationsto aid
in constructing turnpike roads in said county , by
which allthe main thoroughfares in every sectionof
said county has been turnpiked ; and whereas , there
is now an outstanding indebtedness of over sixty
thousand dollars against said county on account of
such appropriations; therefore,
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That hereafterit shall not be lawful for the
Washington County Court to make any appropria-
tion to or subscribe for stock in any turnpike com-
pany greaterthan fivehundred dollarsper mile ; nor
shallsaidcounty court make any appropriation until
there has been a sufficient amount of solvent bona
fide subscriptionforthe stock of such company suffi-
cient to build the road with the said county aid .
§ 2. All laws or parts of laws in conflictwith this
act are hereby repealed .
§ 3. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and after itspassage .
Approved April 18, 1890 .

CHAPTER 896 .
