LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1273
CHAPTER 997 .
AN ACT forthe benefitof the University Club of Louisville.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the UniversityClub ,locatedin Louisville,
• Jeffersoncounty, Kentucky ,incorporated under and
- in accordance with the laws of this State providing
- for the incorporationof such companies ,and whose
articlesof incorporationhave been recorded in the
Jeffersoncounty clerk'soffice, be, and the same is
herebydeclaredto be, a body-corporate,with power
to sue and be sued,contractand be contractedwith,
and adopt and change a corporateseal, make , adopt
and alterby -laws,and with such other powers and
_ duties as are incident to corporations .
§2 . The capitalstock of the said company shallbe
fiftythousand dollars, and which may be increased
from time to time, at any regularor specialmeeting of
the stockholders,but to an amount not exceeding one
hundred thousand dollars. The capitalstock shallbe
divided intosharesof one hundred dollarseach ; and
any person who is now or shallbecome a member of
the club, and no other, shall be entitled to one share
of stock, which shall be paid by his initiationfee;
said stock shallnot be transferableby actof the party
or by operation of law , and shall revert absolutely
to the club on such person ceasing to be a member ,
whether by death or resignation, expulsion or other-
wise . No person shallhold more than one share of
stock , nor be entitledto record more than one vote .
§ 3. The members of the club shall be those who
are such at the time this act shall take effect.
Second . Those therefore elected as prescribed by
the by -laws who are qualifiedunder the followingsec-
tions .
Third . Any male person shallbe eligibleto mem

1274 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
bershipwho shallhave resided at any reputable uni-
versityor college, or the United States Military or
Naval Academy , forat leastone session, or who shall
at least have receiveda degree from any reputable
professionalschool.
Fourth . Any other person may be elected as pre-
scribedby the by -laws : Provided ,That there shallnot
be atany one time more than fortyper centum of allthe
members who shallnot be qualifiedunder the forego-
ing section three .
§ 4. The initiationfee shall not be less than one
hundred dollars,and the monthly dues shall not ex-
ceed fivedollars,but the board of directors may in-
crease the amount of initiation fee or decrease that of
the monthly dues.
§ 5. The clubshallhold at leastone general meeting
annually, to be held on the third Saturday in the
month of March ineach year.
§ 6. The officersof the club shallconsistof a presi
dent, vice-president, a secretaryand treasurer, and
seven directors,each to serve one year, and such offi-
cers and directorsshall compose the board of direct-
ors, and shall be electedby the club at the annual
meeting in March of each year ; and the officersand
directorsin officeat the time of the passage of thisact
shall continue until the next annual election .
§ 7. The said corporation shall have the right to
acquire,hold and dispose of such real and personal
estateastheymay deem prudentforthe purposes of
the club ;and itshallhave authoritytoborrow money ,
and to issueand sellitsnegotiablebonds to an amount
not exceeding one hundred thousand dollars, and
bearing interestat a ratenot exceeding six per cent.
per annum , and may secure the payment thereof by
any deed or deeds of trust or mortgage as the board
of directors of the said club may determine ; and the
privatepropertyof the members shallbe exempt from
the corporatedebts.
§ 8. The board of directorsshallbe the governing

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1275
body of the club ; shall have power to fillvacancies
of officersor directorsfor the unexpired term, and to
make and enforceallnecessaryrulesand regulations,
includingthe fixingand enforcingof fines,penalties,
forfeituresand expulsions ; but it shall provide for an
appeal to the club on the part of any member whom
itmay decide to expel,and itsdecisionson such ques-
tions shallbe subjectto a reversalby a majority vote
of the members present at the meeting which con-
siders it.
§ 9. Besides such meetings as may be calledby the
board of directors, the secretaryshall, at the written
request of fivemembers ,designatingthe objectof the
meeting , calla meeting of the club ; and at such meet-
ing any rule or regulationestablishedby the board of
directorsmay be vacatedby a vote of a majorityof the
members present; or any officeror directornamed in
the callmay be removed from officeby a vote of two-
thirdsof the members present, and the place supplied
by a vote of a majority of those present. A notice
posted by the secretary in a conspicuous place in the
club for ten days , designating the time, place and
object of a meeting, shallbe a validcalltherefor.
§ 10. No treatingof one member by another shall
be allowed in the club-house ,except in the company
of an invited guest .
§ 11. No bettingor playing of games for money or
other consideration shall be allowed in the club -house .
§ 12. This act shalltake effectfrom itspassage.
Approved April 22,1890.

CHAPTER 998 .
AN ACT to amend the charter of the town of Slaughtersville, in
Webster county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That the charterof the town of Slaughtersville,
in Webster county , be, and same is hereby , amend-
ed as follows: That the policecourt of said town shall
have jurisdictionconcurrentwith the county court
and courts of justicesof the peace for trial of of-
fensesagainstthe provisionsof an act,entitled " An
act to prohibitthe sale, barter and trafficin spirit-
uous, vinous or malt liquors in Webster county,"
approved April thirteenth, one thousand eight hun-
dred and eighty-six.
§ 2. That when any fineshall be imposed in said
court for violationof any of the provisions of said,
or of any by -law or ordinance of said town , and
thepersonupon whom the finemay be imposed shall
failto pay or replevysame and costs,he shall work
on the streets,or other publicworks of said town ,one
day for each dollar of such fine and costs.
§3 . This actshallbe in forcefrom itspassage .
Approved April 22,1890.

CHAPTER 1001 .
AN ACT to levy a tax to pay the floatingdebt of the city of Owensboro .
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That for the purpose of raising means to pay
off the floatingdebt of the cityof Owensboro , the
common councilof saidcity,at the time when by law
they are authorizedto levy other taxes of said city,
they shallhave the power and authority to levy a tax
of twenty-fivecents on each one hundred dollars of
taxablepropertyin saidcityeach year,for a period of
three years,which fund so collectedshall be for the
purpose of paying said floatingdebt only ,and to be
used for no otherpurpose. Said actconferring said
authorityonlywithinthepresentlimitsof said city.
§ 2. This act to take effectand be in force from and
afteritspassage; but itsprovisionsshall not be car-
ried out as to the levy and collectionof taxes until
submitted to and approved by the qualified voters of
said city.
Approved April 22,1890.

CHAPTER 1004 .
AN ACT to empower Daveisscounty to disposeof itspauper grave-
yard, and to acquireotherland for such use.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of theCom-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the county of Daveiss be,and itishereby ,
empowered and authorized to sell, or exchange for
other suitablelands,the lands owned by it and used
as a pauper grave-yard ; and a deed signed and ac-
knowledged by the judge of the Daveiss County Court
shallpass the county'stitleto saidland. Said county
may acquire by purchase,gift, or in exchange , other
suitable lands for a pauper grave-yard, and take a
deed to Daveiss county therefor.
§ 2. Itshallbe the duty of the county court to pro-
vide for the removal of the bodies buried in its old
grave -yard to the lands which itmay acquirefor that
use .
§ 3. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and afteritspassage.
Approved April 22, 1890 .

CHAPTER 1006 .
AN ACT to amend the charterof the city of Owensboro , so as to em-
power saidcitytorecovertaxesby suit.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That section forty-seven of the charter of the
city of Owensboro shall be amended as follows, to
wit : That hereafter the city of Owensboro shall have
the right, when the tax collectorof said city shall
have returned any listof taxes assessed against any
person liableto taxation in said city, " no property
found," to institutean actionin equity in any of the

the discoveryof money ,property or choses in action,
and for thatpurpose may have the same remedies , by
attachment or otherwise,as provided by law in actions
broughtby individualsinthecollectionof debts.
§ 2. This act,as to provisionalremedies and discov-
ery of money ,propertyor chosesin action,shallap-
ply toany actionsheretoforeinstituted, and thatmay
stillbe pending in favorof said city.
§ 3. This act to take effectand be in force from and
after itspassage .

poses .

CHAPTER 1026 .
AN ACT authorizingthecommissionersof Lawrence countytoapply
the countytax on Ohio and Big Sandy RailroadCompany's roadand
propertyinpayment ofexpendituresfor rightof way .
WHEREAS , The people of Lawrence county are
deeply interestedin having the Ohio and Big Sandy
Railroad extended from Louisa up both the Tug and
Lavisa Forks of the Big Sandy river;and to aid there-
in,and induce the company to make such extension,it
is proposed to secure for the company the right of
way up whichever stream the company may determine
to build, or up both streams ifbuiltup both ; but as
this can not be done in allcasesby voluntarygrant of
land -owners, as some will refuse,others may be non-
residents or under disability; and inasmuch as neither
the people nor any authorityof the county can cause
the condemnationof lands,and in orderto provide;
therefore
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the commissioners of the county of Law-
rence be,and are hereby, empowered to enter intoa
contractwith the Ohio and Big Sandy Railroad Com-
pany to the effectthat forsuch rightsof way as can
not be procured by voluntarygrant,the saidrailroad
company may procure by purchase and condemnation
such rightsof way as may be required by the com-
pany for itsroad-way in Lawrence county ; thatsaid
commissioners may agree with the saidcompany , and
so enter upon itsorder-book ,that the county tax upon
the road and property within the county of Lawrence
shall be appliedto the payment of amounts expended
and the interestthereon, not, however , exceeding in
the aggregate the amount so paid by the company ;
that for the amount paid for such right of way , in-
cluding the costof condemnation and interestthereon,
the county commissioners of said county are author

1366 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
izedto ascertainthe amount so paid,and the respect-
ive dates when paid,and to enter upon their order-
book the amount so ascertained .
§ 2. The agreement and undertaking of the county
of Lawrence with the Ohio and Big Sandy Railroad
Company , hereinbeforereferredto, may be by an
entry upon the county order-book of said commission-
ers,and such contractso made shall be binding upon
saidcounty ; and itshallbe the duty of said commis-
sioners,or other authorityof the county , to have the
county taxes againstsaid railroadset apart and ap-
pliedas provided by the contract.
§ 3. This act takes effectfrom itspassage .
Approved April 24 , 1890 .

obstructionsinthe water-coursesof Calloway county," approved the
eighthday ofMarch,one thousandeighthundred and eighty-six.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That sectionone and two of said act ishereby
repealed .
§ 2. That upon the petitionof ten persons owning
land upon any water course in said county , the
county judge of saidcounty is hereby authorized and
empowered tolay offsaidwater-courseor courses,as
petitionedfor,into districts, and appoint overseers
in each of saiddistrictsalong saidwater -courses, who
shall summon all male persons over the age of sixteen
who own land along saidstreams or adjacent thereto.
§ 3. The overseers so appointed in said districts shall
warn ,by writtenor verbalnoticeto the hands owning
lands within the bounds of his district, of the time
and place of work ,and clean out alltrees,drift-wood ,
logsof any characterthat may be insidethe banks of
said streams,and keep the same clearof such ; upon
his failureso to do ,he shallbe finedfivedollars.
§ 4. Any person liableto work under this act, who
shall failto be at the time and place, after having
threedays' notice, and do good work , shall be fined
two dollarsand fiftycents by action in the name of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky before a justice of
the peace .

before a justiceof the peace.

sage .

urer of said town .

CHAPTER 1050 .
AN ACT toincorporatethe Citizens'DepositBank of Booneville.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . There is herebyestablisheda bank of depositcapital.
in the town of Booneville, Owsley county,Kentucky ,
with a capitalstock of not less than fiftythousand
dollars, but which may , at the pleasureof the direct-
ors , be increasedto one hundred thousand,in shares of
one hundred dollarseach,to be subscribedand paid
for as hereinafter provided . And the subscribers to
said stock, their associates, successors and assigns,
shall be a body -politicand corporate, by the name
and styleof Citizens'Deposit Bank ofBooneville,Ken- Name.
tucky , and shall so continue forthe period of thirty
years from itsorganization;and shallbe capableof
contracting and being contracted with, suing and
being sued, of pleading and being impleaded, of
answering and defending, in allthe courtsand places
whatsoever ;itmay have a common seal,which itmay
change or renew at pleasure.
§ 2. Said bank shall be under the control of a board
of seven directors,each of whom shallbe stockhold-
ers, who ,afterthe firstelection,shall be elected on
the firstMonday in February in each year, or as soon
as practicablethereafter,and who shallhold officefor

AN ACT relatingto depositionstaken to be read in the McCracken
Court of Common Pleas .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That any deposition or depositions taken on
any day of the sittingsof the McCracken Court of
Common Pleas,at any term thereof,shallnot be read
as evidence in any cause to be triedat the term dur-
ing which the same was taken,except by agreement
of all parties to the cause whose rights are to be
affectedthereby.
§ 2. The refusalto allow any depositionor deposi-
tions taken on any day of the sittingsof said court
to be read,shallnot be ground forcontinuance of any
cause , unless it be shown by affidavitthat due dili-
gence had been used to take the same beforethe term
commence .
§ 3. This act shall take effect and be in force from
and afteritspassage.
[Became a law April26,1890.withoutthe approvalof theGovernor.]
CHAPTER 1052..
AN ACT to prohibit the sale of spirituous, vinous, malt liquors, ale
or beer, within three miles of Salem Baptist Church , in Christian
county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That itshallbe unlawful forany person or per-
sons to sell or vend any spirituous, vinous, malt
liquors , ale or beer, or a mixture of either, within
three miles of Salem Baptist Church , in Christian
county , Kentucky .

CHAPTER 1066 .
AN ACT toincorporateSaintXavier'sCollege,ofLouisville,Kentucky.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That an institutionto educate boys and young
men in the higher branches of learningbe,and the
same ishereby,incorporatedand establishedat Louis-
ville, Jeffersoncounty, Kentucky , to be known by
the name of Saint Xavier's College .
§ 2. That John Baptist Vander Wee , Peter Lucas
Adolph Sommer , Bernard Hagemann and James
Hahnel , and such personsas they hereaftermay elect
as their associates and successors from the brother-
hood known as the Xaverian Brothers of the Roman
Catholic Church ,be,and they are, incorporatedas a
body -corporate, under the name and style of Saint
Xavier's College, and by that name they shall have
perpetual succession; and they may contractand be
- contracted with, sue and be sued, and have all the
powers hereinaftergiven; and may have and use a
common seal, and alter the same at pleasure; and
they may adopt by -laws for the transactionof their
business and for the government of said college.
Said corporation may acquire, receiveand hold, and
-n every way use and enjoy, lands, tenements and
hereditaments,money ,goods and choses in action,by
gifts, donations,devise, subscription, contractor pur-
chase , to the value of one hundred thousand dollars,
and may sell,exchange and convey said real or per-
sonal estateas may be deemed best for said corpora-
ion .
- § 3. Said corporationmay employ as many profess-
ors or teachers as may be deemed necessaryfor the
nstruction of itspupils in the artsand sciences,and
nay regulatethe conduct and courseof study of its
pupils, and may conferupon the graduatesof said
college suchdiplomas,honorsand degreesas evidence

1468 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
of scholarshipand skillas are usual in institutions
and collegesof this State.
§ 4. Said corporationmay establish its collegein
Louisville, or elsewhere in thisState, and may estab-
lish branch schools or branch colleges in other parts
of the State .
§5 . The management and controlof the corporation
shallbe vested in a board of trustees, consistingof
not less than three nor more than thirteen members ,
who shallbe selectedby the members of the corpora-
tion, and who shallbe subjectto the by -lawsand
rules adopted by the corporation. The members of
saidboard of trusteesshall, subject to the limitations
above, be determined by the by -laws. The Brother
Provincialof the Xaverian Brothers in America ,and
his successorin officefor the time being, shallalways
be the presidentof thiscorporation.
§ 6. The board of trusteesmay elect a vice-presi-
dent,secretaryand treasurer,and any other suitable
officers, at such times and in such manner and on
such terms and for such length of time as the by-
laws prescribe. All papers to be executed , or actsto
be performed by the presidentof said corporation.
may be done by the vice-president in the absence,
sicknessor disabilityof the president. The by-laws
may prescribehow vacancies in the officesmay be
filled, and how and for what causes officers or mem-
bersof saidcorporationmay be removed,suspended
or expelled . Said board of trustees shall have the
power at any time,by a two-thirdsvote, to expelaby
member of said board of trusteesfor a good cause.
The presidentshall always have a veto upon itsacts.
and in his absence the vice-presidentshall have such
a veto .
§7 . This act shall take effect from and after its
passage .
Approved April 25,1890

corporation for its said railway can not agree with

then the same may be condemned accordingto law :
Provided , That before any of the streetsof the town
I of Irvine shallbe run over or across, the consent of
the trustees of said town shall be firstobtained .
§ 3. The capitalstock of saidcompany shallbe ten
thousand dollars, divided into shares of twenty-five
dollars each,with power to extend its stock to any
amount not exceeding twenty -five thousand dol-
lars, in like shares. When one hundred shares of
said stock are subscribed, the incorporatorsherein
named , or one or more of them , shall call the stock-
holders together at some suitableplace in Irvine,
Kentucky , by notice mailed or delivered to each
stockholder ten days beforesaid meeting, forthe pur-
pose of organizing and electingdirectorsas herein
provided . At said meeting, and at all meetings of
the stockholders,each share of stock shallentitlethe
holder to one vote, in person or by proxy , upon all
questions to be voted upon by the stockholders. Said
incorporators may designate one or more of their
number to receivesaid subscriptions.

ers at their annual meeting on the third Tuesday in

until his successor is chosen at the succeeding regular

and such officers,agents,employes, as are engagedby

formance of theirduties if required. The directors
shall have power to appoint such agents and em-
ployes,and prescribetheirdutiesand compensation.

receive and collect, for transportation and travelupon
itsrailway, reasonablefreightsand passengerrates.

passage .

§3 . It shall be the duty of each person in said
county ,when called on by the assessoreach year for
the purpose of taking his listfor State and county
assessment and levies, to give to the assessora verbal
statement, under oath, to be administered by the
assessor, showing the number of dogs and bitches
owned or kept or harbored by such person, or any
member of his family residingwith him , on the fif-
teenth day of September for the year saidlistisbeing
taken . The person giving saiddogs or bitches shall
be liablefor the tax againstthe same ,and same shall
be collectedfrom him as herein provided in thisact.
Any person refusingto give in such listto the assessor
shallbe reportedby him tothecounty court,in writ

1494 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
ing,and saidcourtshallcause him to be summoned to
its next term to show cause for his failure or refusal :
and ifhe failto respond or to show good cause ,he shall
be fined ten dollarsby the court: said person shall
then give his listto the court for taxation , and the
sheriffshall collectthe tax thereon. Any dog or
bitch listedfor taxation under thisact, belonging to
a residentof Fayette county , is hereby declared to
be personal property, and the owner thereof may
maintain a civilaction and proceeding for any in-
jury or damage done thereto, the same as other prop-
erty .
§ 4. That the assessor shall be allowed five cents for
each dog and bitch assessed by him under this act,
and properly reported by him on his list; and the
same shallbe paid out of the dog tax on the order
of the county court, which order may be made any
time before the distributionof the fund , and paid
out of said fund on said order .
§5 . That the assessor shall be fined not exceeding
fivedollarsfor each dog or bitch he failsor neglects
to list, and a like sum for each person he fails to
swear to his list; the said fines may be recovered
beforea justiceof the peace or the county judge .
§ 6. The sheriffof Fayette county shall, by virtue
of his office, be collectorof said tax levied by this
act, and on his county levy bond be liable therefor;
and the county attorney shall, in the name of the
county, instituteand prosecute all necessary actions
against the sheriffas may be necessary to recover
said fund , or any part thereof, in the hands of the
sheriffgoing to said sheep fund .
§ 7. The sheriffshallhave allthe powers in collect-
ing said tax that he has in collectingthe county and
State taxes,and may proceed in likemanner , and no
property of any kind shallbe exempt from said taxes.
The sheriffshall collectsaid taxes and settle with the
county court, and shall pay the same over as said
court may order .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1495

!
§ 8. The sheriff, in collectingsaid taxes,shall first
apply thereforto the person who listedthe dog or
bitch, and collectthe same from him or her ; but if
such person failor refuse to pay said tax, and no
property is found from which said tax can be made ,
then the owner of the premises shall be responsible
for said tax. All of the taxes provided for by this
act shallbe due and collectibleeach year as soon as
the assessorreturnshis listto the county court clerk,
and the sheriffshallproceed to collectthe same ; and
for his servicesin collectingand paying over said
taxeshe shallbe allowed a commission ,to be fixedby
the county court and paid out of said fund .
§ 9. That any person residingin said county, and
having sheep killedby dogs,may ,on or beforethe
tenth of September next succeedingthe time of such
killing, produce and filein the county clerk'soffice
with said clerk a statement in writing, showing
when the sheep were killed, the number and value
of saidsheep and that they were killedby dogs, and
signed and sworn to by the owner of the sheep,and
must be accompanied by the affidavitsof at leasttwo
credible persons as to the value thereof, and that
they were killedby dogs . So soon as all the claims
presented, as hereinprovided for any year, are all
passed upon by the court,then itshallbe the duty of
the court to order the sheriffto pay over to the
claimants the amounts allowed by the court, after
paying all costs for collectingand distributingsaid
funds , including assessor's, sheriff'sand other fees
provided by thisact. If, however , the fund shall be
insufficientto pay allthe claimsin full,then the court
shall order said fund to be prorated amongst the
claimants whose claims are allowed .
§ 10. Should there be a surplus remaining in the
hands of the sheriffafter paying for all the sheep
killed by dogs provided for under the provisionsof
this act, then such surplus shall be carried over to the
next year forfiveyears. At the end of fiveyears, if

1496 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
thereshouldbe a surplusin the hands of the sheriff,
it shall be subject to appropriation for county pur-
poses by the county court.
§ 11. This act shallnot apply to the city of Lexing-
ton,and shallnot be enforced until the county court
of Fayette county, a majority of the justicescon-
curring,shallenteran orderapproving and adopting
the same as thelaw of saidcounty, exclusiveofthe
cityof Lexington.
Approved April26,1890.

the W. T. Pyne Mill and Supply Company , of Louisville, Ker-
tucky .

monwealth of Kentucky :

Supply Company ,of Louisville,Kentucky ,a corpora-
tioncreated under the general law of this State,and
doing businessin Louisville,Kentucky ,be approved
and confirmed as it is heretofore existed and doing

CHAPTER 1083 .
AN ACT prohibitingthechargingof certainpersonstollon turnpike
roadsin Woodford county,Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That the managers of any road in Woodford
- county ,whether the Stateor county have an interest
therein or not, shallpermit ministersof the gospel to
travel on such road without paying toll,when on min-
isterialduty ,and shallcharge no tollagainst pupils
going to and from school,or to personsgoing to or
from their accustomed place of worship : Provided ,
That if a vehicleshallcontainany other person than
- pupils goingtoor returningfrom schooland the bona
fide driverthereof,then fulltollshallbe charged and
collected for such vehicle.
§ 2. A violationof any provisionof this act shall
be punished by a fineof not lessthan twenty dollars
nor more than fiftydollarsforeach offense.
§ 3. This actshalltake effectfrom and afteritspas-
sage .
[Became a lawApril28,1890,withouttheapprovalof theGovernor.]

1510 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
CHAPTER 1089 .
AN ACT to prevent the sale of spirituousliquors in the vicinityof
schoolsand churches, and so forth, in the city of Mount Sterling,
Kentucky .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be unlawful for any person or
persons to sellor vend ardent spiritsby retail, or to
keep a saloon or coffee-house in the city of Mount
Sterling, Kentucky ,within fiftyyards of any church
or house used for the worship of God , or of any
school-house, or public or private hospital, within
the cityof Mount Sterling,Kentucky .
§ 2. That any person violatingthe firstsection of
this act shall be deemed guilty of a misdemeanor ,
and shallbe subjecttoa fineof notlessthan twenty
dollars nor more than one hundred dollars for each
offense, and each sale shall be deemed a separate
offense .
§ 3. That it shallbe no defense to any prosecution
under thisact that the party charged with so offend-
ing has a licenseto vend such spirits.
§ 4. That the finesunder thisact may be recovered
by prosecutionin the citycourtof Mount Sterling,
or any court of competent jurisdiction.
§ 5. This act shall be in force and take effect from
and after itspassage .
[Became a law April29,1890.withoutthe approval of the Governor.]

pleted on or before the firstday of November , one
thousand eighthundred and ninety.

of the county, shall appoint officersto act in their

ing threein number , who shallbe examined touching

of registration, shallbe allowed to vote afterhe shall

and shall have stated under oath that he lost said cer-
tificateof registration, and that said certificatehas
not passed into the hands of another person with his
knowledge and consent.
How Absentees May Register.
§ 15. Any person entitledto register,who was neces-
- sarilyabsent from the county of Powell during the
days allowed forregistrationunder and by the provis-
- ions of this act, or who was illduring said time,or
who was unable to attend the place of registration on
account of the sickness of some named member of his
family, may have his name placed upon the registry
- for the precinctof which he lives,by attending the
county clerk'sofficeat the times hereinafterspecified,
and making affidavitbefore the clerk, showing the
facts required to be statedin the registryby section
five of this act, and showing the absence or sickness
hereinbefore referred to .
§ 16. On the lastMonday in July in each year, and
on the Saturday preceding the congressionalelection,
the county clerkof Powell county shall receive,in a
bound book to be kept by him for that purpose , the
affidavits provided for in the preceding section, and
shallplacethe names of persons making such affidavits
upon the proper registration-books ,and shallwritehis
name as clerk,and the date of the registryafter the
name of the person so registered, in the column
headed " Remarks ." For this he shall receive a fee
of ten cents for each name registeredby him under
thisact, to be paid as otherexpensesof registration
are paid .
§ 17. Any person making falsestatementin any affi-
davit taken under this act shallbe deemed guiltyof
false swearing, and punished by confinement in the
penitentiary not less than one nor more than five
years ; and any county clerk or deputy thereof who

1560 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
fraudulentlyregistersthe name of any person , know-
ing that such person is not entitledto be registered,
or who registersa name at a time other than that pro-
vided in this act, shall, on indictment, be deemed
guiltyof a felony, and punished by confinement in
the penitentiarynot lessthan one nor more than five
years .
§ 18. Any person in the county court clerk's office
may challengethe rightof any voter to register under
thisact,and thereupon the county clerkshall examine
such voterand any witnesswho may be offered under
oath, and shallhear and determine the question of his
rightto register; and the dutiesherein imposed upов
the county clerk may be performed by his deputy ,
and he or hisdeputy may administer oaths under this
act ..
False Registration .
§19 . Any person who shall cause himself to be
registeredin more than one electionprecinct,other-
wise than is provided in sectiontwelve of this act,or
more than once in the same precinct, or who shall
cause himselfto be registered,knowing that he isnot
lawfullyentitledto registration,and any person who
shallaid and abet in the commission of any of said
acts, shallbe deemed guiltyof a misdemeanor , and
shallbe imprisonedin thecounty jailnot less than
one nor more than twelve months .
False Registration Books .
§ 20.Any officerof registrationor other person who
shallunlawfullyalterany registrationbooks or add
any name thereto,or who shallwillfullysecrete,sup-
pressor destroyany such book,or who shall aid in
making any false or fraudulent registration-book,
shallbe deemed guiltyof a felony, and shall be con
fined in the penitentiarynot lessthan one nor more
than fiveyears, and forfeitany officehe then holds.
and shallforeverbe disqualifiedfrom holding office.

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1561
Perjury .
§21 . Any person who shallwillfullymake any false
statement under oath lawfully administered under
thisact,shallbe deemed guiltyof perjury,and con-
fined in the penitentiarynot lessthan one nor more
than fiveyears .
Obstructing Registration .
§ 22. If any person by himself, or in aid of others,
- shall forciblybreak up, or attempt to break up, a
registrationheld under thisact, or shallforciblypre-
vent , or attempt to prevent, any person from ap-
proaching or entering a place of registrationfor the
purpose of registering, shall be fined not less than
one hundred nor more than fivehundred dollars, or
imprisoned in the county jailnot less than six nor
more than twelve months , or both .
§ 23.No witnessshallbe excused from testifying
- before any grand jury,or in any prosecutionor in-
dictment under thisact,on the ground that his testi-
mony may criminate himself; but no such testimony
shall be used againsthim in any prosecution,except
for perjury in the givingof the same .
Fees .
§ 24. For his servicesunder this act the county
clerk shallbe entitledto the following fees, and no
other : For copying the registrylistrequired to be
kept in his office,or tobe used in supplyinglostbooks ,
the sum of two cents for each voter whose name is
copied ; for his servicesunder sectionthirteenof this
act , the sum of twenty-fivecentsforthe name of each
voter strickenoffthe list; for receiptingto the clerks
of registrationand electionforregistration-books,the
sum of fivecentsfor each book receiptedfor. The
sheriffshall receiveforserving the notices provided
for in section two the sum of sixty cents for each
actual service. Officersof registrationshall receive

1562 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
two dollarsforeach day they are employed in attend-
ing at the placeof registration,but clerks of registra-
tionshallnot be entitledto any pay , except upon the
productionof the countyclerk'sreceiptfor theirre-
spectiveregistration-books,which receiptshallbe the
voucher of the officerpaying them . The county of
Powell shallpay allfeesand expenses incurred under
this act of registration.
§ 25. A copy of thisact shall be printed in each
registration-book made under itsprovisions.
§ 26. This act shall take effectand be in force from
itspassage .
Approved April 29,1890.

tracts; to sue and be sued ; to have a corporateseal;

ment , which sale shall be upon the same terms and

§1. That Thomas P. Wornall, BriceSteele, B. F. Corporators.
- Bedford , Junior,Ed .Ford and John Roseberry , their

Cane Ridge and Rogers Creek Turnpike Company ,
with power to sue and be sued , contract and be con-

struct a macadamized turnpike road in Bourbon
county ,beginning at a point on the Cane Ridge Turn-
pike ,near the residenceof H. M. Roseberry ; thence
on or near the line between H. M. Roseberry and
Brice Steele; thence through BriceSteele'sland to the
land of Mrs. Frank Ford ; thence through Mrs.
Frank Ford's land , on the east side of Rogers creek ,
to the land of W. H. Kerr , or to the line between
said W. H. Kerr and Mrs. Stone ; thence along
said line to a point fifty feet beyond certain
springs on Kerr's land (or on Kerr's land between said
line and springs, provided this can be done without
injury to the springs,and without preventingthe use
of same for stock purposes by W. H. Kerr) ; thence
on the land of W. H. Kerr to a point on the Paris and
North Middletown Turnpike , near the bridge over
Rogers creek .
§3 . The capital stock of said company shall not
exceed ten thousand dollars, and shall be divided into
shares of fiftydollars each .
§ 4. The above-named incorporators,or any of them ,
are hereby authorized to solicitsubscriptionsto the

CHAPTER 1146 .
AN ACT topermitmagisterialdistrictsin the county of Boone to vote
on bridgetaxand buildbridges.
Be itenactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That on the applicationof thirty tax -payers of
any magisterialdistrictin the county of Boone , made
to the county court or county commissioners of said
county ,in writing, not lessthan thirtydays beforea
regularelection, State or county , to be held in said
county,the saidcourt or commissioners shall make an
order submitting to the qualifiedvoters of said magis-
terialdistrict,the questionas to whether or not they
will levy a tax, not to exceed twenty cents on every
one hundred dollarsof taxable property in such dis-
trict, and one dollaron each tithe, for the purpose of
building necessary bridges in said district; said tax
to continue two years from the time levied if neces-
sary ; and when any stream is the division between
two districtsin said county , the said county court or
county commissioners shall, on the application of
thirtytax-payers from each magisterialdistrict, sub-
mit saidquestiontosuch districtsjointly, as provided
in this act .
§ 2. If a majority of the votes cast at such election
in said districtor districts, when any two vote to
gether,be in favorof the tax,itshallbe levied in such
districtor districts, and collected by the sheriffof
said county in the same way and manner that the
county and State revenue is now collected; and the
sheriffshallbe liableon his officialbond for the faith-
fulperformance of hisdutiesunder this act.
§ 3. For the purpose of ascertainingthe sense of the
qualifiedvotersofany districtordistricts,as provided
in thisact, the county court or county commissioners
of saidcounty shall,at the firstcounty or Stateelec-

tion held in said county afterthe applicationmade
to him as provided herein, he shallcause to be opened
such districtor districts, and it shall be the duty
of the officerswho may hold such electionto pro-
pound to each voter who may vote the question :
Are you in favor of the bridge tax ? and his vote shall
be recorded for or against the tax as he may direct.
The pollsshallbe returned as the other pollsin said
election, and shallbe examined and compared by the
same officers .
§ 4. If the majority of the legalvotes cast in such
- districtor districtsbe in favor of such tax , it shall be
the duty of the examining board to certifythe factto
the county court of saidcounty, and at the firstregu-
lar term of said court thereafterthe judge of said
court shall cause said certificateto be spread on the
-order -book of said court .

county ,as soon as possibleaftersuch electionisheld,
in the event any districtor districtsshallvote to levy

in such districtor districts,who shallbe styledBridge

CHAPTER 1148 .
AN ACT to authorize the sale of Mill Grove and Kirksville Turn-
pike Road ,in Madison county,and itsfranchisesand privileges.
WHEREAS , It has been represented that the Mill
Grove and KirksvilleTurnpike Road Company has
constructed itsroad from Mill Grove to Kirksville,
in Madison county,and has become involved in debt
by reason of said constructionand the operation of
same , and that the receiptsfrom tollsdo not more
than keep the same in repair; therefore,
Be it enactedby the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That on a petitionin equityfiledintheMadi-
son Circuit Court or in the Madison Common Pleas
Court by the presidentof the Mill Grove and Kirks-
ville Turnpike Road Company against Madison
county , which isthe largeststockholder in said com-
pany , praying for a sale of said road and itsfran-
chises and privileges, power and authoritybe,and is
hereby , conferred on said court to decree a sale of
same for the payment of debts, provided a major-
ity of the stockholders shall consent in writing to
said sale,and saidcourt shalldeem it equitable; and
to order a distributionof the overplus,if any , upon
equitable principles, among those who own stock in
proportion to the amounts of stock paid forand held
by them severally, and that the officersand directors
of the road may work aforesaidroad to pay the debts
of same .
§2 . That Madison county , being interested as a
stockholder in said road , must be a defendant to the
action , and be brought before the court by the service
of processon the presidingjudge of the county court
of said county .
§ 3. This act shall be in force from and after itspas-
sage .
Approved April 30,1890.

CHAPTER 1170 .
AN ACT to incorporatethe Kentucky Fire Insurance Company .
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1. That Robert Brodie, F. T. Gunther , A. C.
Incorporation. Tompkins , J. D. Powers, W. E. Parrish, R. S.
Hughes , M. P. Mattingly, J. H. Hickman , J. W.
Carter, or such of them as may act, and their associ-
ates,successorsand assigns,be,and they are hereby,
createda body-corporate,under the name and styleof
the Kentucky Fire InsuranceCompany , of Owens-
boro, Kentucky , with perpetual succession, and full
powers tocontractand be contractedwith, sue and be
sued; to acquire,use,hold and dispose of such real
and personalpropertyas may be necessary for the
proper conduct of itsbusiness; to investitscapital

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1701
and the funds accumulated in the course of business,
or any part thereof, in any bonds , stock, mortgages
and deeds of trust on real estate,in or out of the State
_ of Kentucky ; to loan itssaid capitalstock and accu-
mulated funds on such security as itsboard of direct-
ors may determine : Provided , Said investments and
loans are such as are required by the insurancelaws
of the State; to insurehouses,buildingsand allother
kinds of property against loss or damage by fire, or
from lightning or tornado, in or out of this State;
and to make all kinds of insurance on goods,mer-
chandise and other property in course of transporta-
tion,whether on land or water ; to cause itselfto be
insured against any loss or risk it may incur, and
upon the interestitmay have in any property, and to
- generallydo and perform allother matters and things
proper to promote these things; to enact by -laws and
regulationsnot in conflictwith this act. The capital
stock of said company shallbe one hundred thousand
dollars; but it may be increasedat any time, by a
vote of stockholdersholding two -thirdsof the capital

debtsofthecity,and amounts due the city,which state-

now in force, and not in conflictwith this charter.
shallremain and continue in force until repealedby
the council .

published in the city,or by the posting of at leastfive

ments ,or any ordinanceor matter designed to be pub
lished as hereinbeforeordered, may be made inthe

expense,such as repairof streetsand takingcareof

of the city, which would otherwise have been paid

and request,deliverto his successorin officeallthe

renceburg holding under the former charter of said
cityshall have all the powers ,duties and privileges
hereingranted the citycounciluntilthe same shall

CHAPTER 1174 .

monwealth of Kentucky :

vey , W. L. Elmore , R. C. James and J. W. Porter,

cession,with power tocontractand be contracted with
sue and be sued, and to acquire and own property.
both real and personal; to have and use a common
seal,with power to altersame at pleasure .
§ 2. Said corporation shall have power to buy, to
improve , and to sellreal estate situated in Fayette
county ,Kentucky ; to open streetsand alleys through
itsproperty;to lay offbuildinglots; to erect houses,
and sell, rentand leaselands and houses , and to hold,

may do ; and it may establishby -laws , and make all

agement of its affairs,not inconsistentwith the Con-
stitutionor laws of the State of Kentucky or of the
United States .
§ 3. The principalplace of transacting the business
of said corporationshallbe in the city of Lexington ,
Kentucky .
§ 4. The capitalstock of said corporation shall not
exceed one hundred thousand dollars, and said cap
italstock shall be divided into shares of one hundred
dollarseach. Said capitalstock shallbe paid inat
such times and upon such terms as the board of di-
rectorsmay ,from time to time, determine ; and for
the stock paid in certificatesof stock shall be issued:
said certificatesto be signed by the president ,counter-
signed by the secretary, and impressed with the cor-
porate seal.
§ 5. The privateproperty of the stockholders shall
be exempt from the corporatedebts .

LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1751
shall be stockholdersin the company , and shallhave
the control and management of its affairs. There
shallalsobe a president,vice-president, secretaryand
treasurer,and such other officersas may from time to
time be constituted by the by -laws, and chosen in
conformity therewith. As soon afterthe passage of
thisact as may be convenient, the stockholdersshall
meet and organize by the electionof a board of di-
rectors,a president,vice-president,secretaryand treas-
urer, who shall hold officeuntil theirsuccessorsbe
chosen as hereinafterprovided. On the firstSatur-
day in May , one thousand eight hundred and ninety,
and on the firstSaturday of May in each succeeding
year thereafter, the stockholdersof said corporation
shall meet and elect from their number a board of di-
rectors, who shall hold their officesuntil their success-
ors be chosen ; and the board of directors so chosen
shall electa president,a vice-president,a secretaryand
a treasurer, each of whom shall be removable at the
pleasure of the board of directors. If for any reason
the annual meeting of the stockholders be not held at
the time herein provided,it shallbe held on a day as
soon thereafteras practicable,tobe fixedby the board
of directors, noticeof saidmeeting to be given by ad-
vertisement in some newspaper published in Lexing-
ton, Kentucky ,for not lessthan ten days preceding
the meeting fixed by the board of directors. In the
event of the death or resignationof any director,his
place shallbe filledby the remainder of the board of
directors,who shallelectsome stockholder to fillthe
place made vacant by such death or resignation. In
the meetings of the stockholders a majorityin inter-
est of the stock issuedshallconstitutea quorum , and
each share of stock shall entitle the holder to one
vote. A majorityin number of the board of directors
shall constitutea quorum for the transactionof any
business .
§ 8. The corporation shall commence business at
once ,and shallcontinue for twenty -fiveyears, unless

1752 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
sooner dissolved,which may at any time be doneby
two -thirds of the stock in interest .
§ 9. This act shalltake effect and be forcefrom
and after its passage .
Approved May 8, 1890.

CHAPTER 1180 .
AN ACT toamend an act,entitled"An act to organizeand establisha
systemof publicgradedschoolsin Carlisle."
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§1 . That section three of an act, entitled " An act
to organize and establisha system of public graded
schools in Carlisle," approved March second, one
thousand eighthundred and eighty-eight,be,and the
same is hereby, repealed; and in lieuthereof, to be
hereafter known as sectionthree of said act, the fol-
lowing is enacted and adopted; to wit: The number

1768 LAWS OF KENTUCKY.

of trustees for said district shall be six, and shall be elected on the first Saturday in June, one thousand eight hundred and ninety, except in events herein provided for. Two of said trustees shall hold their offices for the term of one year, and until their successors are elected and qualified. Two others of said trustees shall hold their offices for two years, and until their successors are elected and qualified; and the other two shall hold their offices for three years, and until their successors are elected and qualified. Said trustees shall decide by lot which shall serve the respective terms. After said first election there shall be held, on the first Saturday in June of each year, except in the events herein provided for, an election for said district, at which two trustees shall be elected to fill the places of the outgoing trustees. In the event the trustees in office, from any cause, fail to hold the election for trustees on said first Saturday in June, they each shall be subject to a fine of fifty dollars, recoverable in the circuit court on the indictment of the grand jury; and it shall be the duty of the county superintendent of common schools for said county to appoint two good, discreet citizens of said district to act as judges, and one good, discreet citizen of said district to act as clerk of said election, and cause said election to be held within ten days after said first Saturday in June; but he shall cause notice of the time and place of said election to be posted at least five days before said election in at least five of the most conspicuous places in Carlisle. For a failure to perform the duty required of him herein, said county superintendent shall be subject to a fine of one hundred dollars. A majority of said board shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business; and in case of the death or resignation of any of said trustees, or if, from any cause, there shall be a vacancy, the other members of the board shall fill said vacancy by the appointment of some suitable and qualified person. Said trustees to qualify within one week after their

CHAPTER 1187 .
AN ACT toprovide for the plugging of allabandoned natural gas wells
in Breckinridge,Grayson and Hancock counties.
Be itenacted by the General Assembly of the Com
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That whenever any well shallhave been put
down forthe purpose of drillingor exploring forgas
in Breckinridge,Grayson and Hancock counties,upon
abandoning or ceasingto operatethe same , the person
- or corporation,and each and every of them in pos .
sessionof same , whether as owner , lessee, manager or
agent , shall, for the purpose of excluding all water
from the gas-bearing rock or stratum, and before
drawing the casing, fillup the well with sand or rock
sediment to a depth at leasttwenty feetabove the gas-
bearing rock or stratum, and drive a round ,seasoned

1786 LAWS OF KENTUCKY .
wooden plug, at leastthree feet in length , equalin
diameter to the diameter of the well below the casing.
to a point at leastfivefeet below the bottom ofthe
casing; and immediately after drawing the casing
shalldrivea round , seasoned wooden plug at a point
justbelow where the lower end of the casing rested,
which plug shallbe at leastthree feet in length,taper-
ing in form ,and of the same diameter at the distance
of eighteen inchesfrom the smaller end as the diam
eter of the hole below the point at which it is driven.
After the plug has been properly driven , thereshal
be fittedon top of the same sand or rock sedimentto
the depth of at leastfivefeet.
§2 . Any person or corporation who shall violate
any of the provisionsof the firstsection of thisact
shallbe liableto a penalty of two hundred dollarsfor
each and every violationthereof, and to the further
penaltyof two hundred dollars for each thirtydays
during which said violationshall continue ; and all
such penaltiesshallberecovered,upon indictmentand
conviction, in any court of competent jurisdiction.
§ 3. Whenever any person or corporation shall
abandon any gas well,and shall failto comply with
the requirementsof the firstsectionof this act within
ten days aftersaidabandonment ,any person or cor
porationlawfullyin possessionof lands adjacent to
or in the neighborhood of, said well, may enter uроn
the land upon which said well is situated and take
possessionof saidwell,and plug the same in the man
ner provided by the firstsection of this act ; ani
may maintain a civilactionin any court of this State
of competent jurisdictionagainst the owner or person
abandoning saidwell and every of them , jointlyand
severally, to recover the cost thereof. This shail
be in additionto the penaltiesprovided by the sec-
ond section of this act .
§ 4. This act shall take effect and be in force from
itspassage .
Approved May 3, 1800

Trust department.
The funds of the trust department of this corporation, should one be established, shall be invested in real estate, mortgage notes or bonds, or in some other interest-bearing or dividend-paying securities as are regarded by prudent business men as safe investments; and this corporation may make loans of such funds, with such securities as collateral, and this company may likewise invest such funds in securities as are now, or may hereafter be, authorized by law for trustee: *Provided*, That such funds shall not be loaned upon personal security alone or be invested in the bonds or securities of any railroad or other corporation, unless such railroad or other corporation has been in operation more than five years, and during that time has not defaulted in the payment of principal or interest on its bonded debts; nor in the bonds of a county, town or city that within ten years has defaulted in the payment of principal or interest on its bonded debt.

Loans.
The funds of the trust department shall be kept separate and apart from the other investments, and designated upon the books of the company as "Investments of Trust Capital," and said trust capital shall be specially and primarily liable for the obligation of said company as executor, administrator, guardian, receiver, assignee or trustee or in any other fiduciary capacity, and shall not be liable for any other debt or obligation of the company until all its trust liabilities aforesaid have been discharged. LAWS OF KENTUCKY . 1845
CHAPTER 1213 .
AN ACT topreventstockfrom runningatlargeon the Mount Sterling
and MaysvilleTurnpike Road ,in Montgomery county.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. That it shall be unlawful for live stock of
any kind to run at largeon the Mount Sterlingand
Maysville Turnpike Road , in Montgomery county .
§2 . Any person or persons, being the owner or
owners of, or having the custody or control of, live
stock of any kind , who shall sufferor permit any of
them to run at large on any portionof the said turn-
pike road,shall be fined for each and every offense
not lessthan two nor more than ten dollars,payable
to the trusteeof the jury fund ,to be recovered by
warrant in the name of the Commonwealth before
any county judge,policejudge,or justiceof the peace
in Montgomery county .
§ 3. Unless said fineis paid or replevied, the stock
30 running at large shall be subject to the fineand
costs, and an executionshallbe leviedupon the same ;
and the officershall proceed to collectthe fine and
costs as in case of other executions ; and none of said
stock so running at large upon said turnpike road
shallbe exempt from execution under thisact.
§ 4. This act shallonly apply to that portionof
said turnpike as is in Montgomery county ; and notice
shall be posted at threeor more of the most public
places on the lineof said turnpike road for at least
fifteen days prior to the time at which action can be
had against violatorsof the provisions of the same .
§ 5. This act shalltake effectfrom and after May
fifteenth, one thousand eighthundred and ninety.
Approved May 3, 1890.

§ 2. The capitalstock of said associationshall be
ive thousand dollars, divided into shares of twenty-
ive dollarseach,payable in such installmentsand at
uch times as the board of directorsmay provide,

ciationmay increaseitscapitalstock to any sum not

§ 3. The officersof said association shall consist of
a president, vice-president, treasurer, secretaryand
fivedirectors, to be chosen by the stockholders of
said associationat an annual meeting of the same , to
be held on the firstSaturday in May , in the town of
Sacramento . Each share shall represent one vote,
which may be cast in person or by written proxy of
the owner thereof, and a majorityof allthe votescast
shall be necessaryto electany officerof said associa-
- tion . The said officersso elected shall hold their re-
-spective officesfor one year,or until their successors
- shall have been duly electedand qualified. No per-
son ,who isnot a stockholderin said association, shall
be eligibleto any officein the same .
§ 4. The fiscal, prudential, and all affairswhatso-
ever of said association shall be under the control,
- management and directionof the presidentand board
of directorsof the same, and they may make such
rules,provide such regulations,and adopt and enforce
such by -laws, not inconsistentwith the laws of the

benefitof said association,and may alter,change,or

§5. Said associationmay hold on itsgrounds, at
such times as it may deem best, fairsfor the exhibi-
tion of stock, agriculturalproducts, mechanical im

1848 LAWS OF KENTUCKY.

plements and other articles, and for the trial of speed of horses in running, trotting or pacing; and may award such premiums as it may deem proper in such exhibits and trials of speed. Said association may fix the rate of entries for all exhibits of products and stock; may charge and collect fees for admission of spectators into its grounds; and shall have the power to license peddlers and mount performances upon its grounds. It shall have the power to appoint and pay as many policemen as may be necessary to preserve good order at its fairs; and such policemen shall have the same powers to arrest disorderly persons and to keep the peace that constables and sheriffs of this Commonwealth have or may hereafter have; but all persons arrested by such policemen shall be forthwith taken by them before some justice of the peace, police judge or other magistrate to be dealt with according to law; and for any wrongful act of such policeman, any person injured may sue said association, and recover of it damages for such injury.

§ 6. The said board of directors shall have the power to fill any vacancy that may occur in any office in said association at a call meeting, which may be held for that purpose, upon the order of the president, notice of which shall be duly given. The said directors shall have the power to hold call meetings for any purpose upon the order of the president, notice of which, containing a statement of the purpose of the meeting, shall be given the directors.

§ 7. All conveyances of realty by the association shall be acknowledged by the president and secretary thereof.

§ 8. The incorporators herein shall act as the first board of directors till the annual election of officers of the association succeeding the organization of the same; and they may fill, by election, the offices of president, vice-president, secretary and treasurer till such annual election.

§ 9. This act shall take effect from its passage.

Approved May 3, 1890
